community.general.aerospike_migrations.short_description: Check or wait for migrations between nodes
community.general.aerospike_migrations.description: This can be used to check for migrations in a cluster. This makes it easy to do a rolling upgrade/update on Aerospike nodes. If waiting for migrations is not desired, simply just poll until port 3000 if available or C(asinfo -v status) returns ok.

host.description: Which host do we use as seed for info connection.

port.description: Which port to connect to Aerospike on (service port).

connect_timeout.description: How long to try to connect before giving up (milliseconds).

consecutive_good_checks.description: How many times should the cluster report "no migrations" consecutively before returning OK back to ansible?

sleep_between_checks.description: How long to sleep between each check (seconds).

tries_limit.description: How many times do we poll before giving up and failing?

local_only.description: Do you wish to only check for migrations on the local node before returning, or do you want all nodes in the cluster to finish before returning?

min_cluster_size.description: Check fails until cluster size is met or until tries is exhausted.

fail_on_cluster_change.description: Fail if the cluster key changes if something else is changing the cluster, we may want to fail.

migrate_tx_key.description: The metric key used to determine if we have tx migrations remaining. Changeable due to backwards compatibility.

migrate_rx_key.description: The metric key used to determine if we have rx migrations remaining. Changeable due to backwards compatibility.

target_cluster_size.description: When all aerospike builds in the cluster are greater than version 4.3, then the C(cluster-stable) info command is used. Inside this command, you can optionally specify what the target cluster size is - but it is not necessary. You can still rely on O(min_cluster_size) if you do not want to use this option. If this option is specified on a cluster that has at least one host <4.3 then it is ignored until the min version reaches 4.3.

environment.description: The airbrake environment name, typically v(production), V(staging), and so on.

user.description: The username of the person doing the deployment.

revision.description: A hash, number, tag, or other identifier showing what revision from version control was deployed.

version.description: A string identifying what version was deployed.

url.description: Optional URL to submit the notification to. Use to send notifications to Airbrake-compliant tools like Errbit.

validate_certs.description: If V(false), SSL certificates for the target URL is not validated. This should only be used on personally controlled sites using self-signed certificates.

community.general.aix_devices.description: This module discovers, defines, removes and modifies attributes of AIX devices.

device.description: The name of the device. V(all) is valid to rescan C(available) all devices (AIX C(cfgmgr) command).

force.description: Forces action.

recursive.description: Removes or defines a device and children devices.

state.description: Controls the device state. V(available) (alias V(present)) rescan a specific device or all devices (when O(device) is not specified). V(removed) (alias V(absent) removes a device. V(defined) changes device to Defined state.

community.general.aix_devices.deprecated: The module is not actively maintained.

community.general.aix_filesystem.short_description: Configure LVM and NFS file systems for AIX
community.general.aix_filesystem.description: This module creates, removes, mount and unmount LVM and NFS file system for AIX using C(/etc/filesystems). For LVM file systems is possible to resize a file system.

account_subsystem.description: Specifies whether the file system is to be processed by the accounting subsystem.

attributes.description: Specifies attributes for files system separated by comma.

auto_mount.description: File system is automatically mounted at system restart.

device.description: Logical volume (LV) device name or remote export device to create a NFS file system. It is used to create a file system on an already existing logical volume or the exported NFS file system. If not mentioned a new logical volume name is created following AIX standards (LVM).

fs_type.description: Specifies the virtual file system type.

permissions.description: Set file system permissions. V(rw) (read-write) or V(ro) (read-only).

filesystem.description: Specifies the mount point, which is the directory where the file system will be mounted.

nfs_server.description: Specifies a Network File System (NFS) server.

rm_mount_point.description: Removes the mount point directory when used with state V(absent).

size.description: Specifies the file system size. For already present it resizes the filesystem. 512-byte blocks, megabytes or gigabytes. If the value has M specified it is in megabytes. If the value has G specified it is in gigabytes. If no M or G the value is 512-byte blocks. If V(+) is specified in begin of value, the value is added. If V(-) is specified in begin of value, the value is removed. If neither V(+) nor V(-) is specified, then the total value is the specified. Size respects the LVM AIX standards.

state.description: Controls the file system state. V(present) check if file system exists, creates or resize. V(absent) removes existing file system if already V(unmounted). V(mounted) checks if the file system is mounted or mount the file system. V(unmounted) check if the file system is unmounted or unmount the file system.

community.general.aix_filesystem.deprecated: The module is not actively maintained.

name.description: Name of the C(inittab) entry.

action.description: Action the C(init) process performs for this entry.

command.description: Command to be executed.

insertafter.description: After which C(inittab) line should the new entry inserted.

state.description: Whether the entry should be present or absent in the C(inittab) file.

community.general.aix_inittab.deprecated: The module is not actively maintained.

community.general.aix_lvg.short_description: Manage LVM volume groups on AIX
community.general.aix_lvg.description: This module creates, removes or resize volume groups on AIX LVM.

force.description: Force volume group creation.

pp_size.description: The size of the physical partition in megabytes.

pvs.description: List of comma-separated devices to use as physical devices in this volume group. Required when creating or extending (V(present) state) the volume group. If not informed reducing (V(absent) state) the volume group is removed.

state.description: Control if the volume group exists and volume group AIX state varyonvg V(varyon) or varyoffvg V(varyoff).

vg.description: The name of the volume group.

vg_type.description: The type of the volume group.

community.general.aix_lvg.deprecated: The module is not actively maintained.

community.general.aix_lvol.description: This module creates, removes or resizes AIX logical volumes. Inspired by M(community.general.lvol) module.

vg.description: The volume group this logical volume is part of.

lv.description: The name of the logical volume.

lv_type.description: The type of the logical volume.

size.description: The size of the logical volume with one of the [MGT] units.

copies.description: The number of copies of the logical volume. Maximum copies are 3.

policy.description: Sets the interphysical volume allocation policy. V(maximum) allocates logical partitions across the maximum number of physical volumes. V(minimum) allocates logical partitions across the minimum number of physical volumes.

state.description: Control if the logical volume exists. If V(present) and the volume does not already exist then the O(size) option is required.

opts.description: Free-form options to be passed to the mklv command.

pvs.description: A list of physical volumes, for example V([hdisk1, hdisk2]).

community.general.aix_lvol.deprecated: The module is not actively maintained.

community.general.alerta_customer.description: Create or delete customers in Alerta with the REST API.

match.description: The matching logged in user for the customer.

api_username.description: The username for the API using basic auth.

api_password.description: The password for the API using basic auth.

api_key.description: The access token for the API.

state.description: Whether the customer should exist or not. Both O(customer) and O(match) identify a customer that should be added or removed.

community.general.ali_instance.short_description: Create, Start, Stop, Restart or Terminate an Instance in ECS; Add or Remove Instance to/from a Security Group
community.general.ali_instance.description: Create, start, stop, restart, modify or terminate ECS instances. Add or remove ecs instances to/from security group.

state.description: The state of the instance after operating.

availability_zone.description: Aliyun availability zone ID in which to launch the instance. If it is not specified, it is allocated by system automatically.

image_id.description: Image ID used to launch instances. Required when O(state=present) and creating new ECS instances.

instance_type.description: Instance type used to launch instances. Required when O(state=present) and creating new ECS instances.

security_groups.description: A list of security group IDs.

vswitch_id.description: The subnet ID in which to launch the instances (VPC).

instance_name.description: The name of ECS instance, which is a string of 2 to 128 Chinese or English characters. It must begin with an uppercase/lowercase letter or a Chinese character and can contain numerals, V(.), V(_) or V(-). It cannot begin with V(http://) or V(https://).

description.description: The description of ECS instance, which is a string of 2 to 256 characters. It cannot begin with V(http://) or V(https://).

internet_charge_type.description: Internet charge type of ECS instance.

max_bandwidth_in.description: Maximum incoming bandwidth from the public network, measured in Mbps (Megabits per second).

max_bandwidth_out.description: Maximum outgoing bandwidth to the public network, measured in Mbps (Megabits per second). Required when O(allocate_public_ip=true). Ignored when O(allocate_public_ip=false).

host_name.description: Instance host name. Ordered hostname is not supported.

unique_suffix.description: Specifies whether to add sequential suffixes to the host_name. The sequential suffix ranges from 001 to 999.

password.description: The password to login instance. After rebooting instances, modified password is effective.

system_disk_size.description: Size of the system disk, in GB. The valid values are V(40)~V(500).

count.description: The number of the new instance. An integer value which indicates how many instances that match O(count_tag) should be running. Instances are either created or terminated based on this value.

count_tag.description: O(count) determines how many instances based on a specific tag criteria should be present. This can be expressed in multiple ways and is shown in the EXAMPLES section. The specified count_tag must already exist or be passed in as the O(tags) option. If it is not specified, it is replaced by O(instance_name).

allocate_public_ip.description: Whether allocate a public IP for the new instance.

instance_charge_type.description: The charge type of the instance.

period.description: The charge duration of the instance, in months. Required when O(instance_charge_type=PrePaid). The valid value are [V(1-9), V(12), V(24), V(36)].

auto_renew.description: Whether automate renew the charge of the instance.

auto_renew_period.description: The duration of the automatic renew the charge of the instance. Required when O(auto_renew=true).

instance_ids.description: A list of instance IDs. It is required when need to operate existing instances. If it is specified, O(count) is ignored.

force.description: Whether the current operation needs to be execute forcibly.

tags.description: A hash/dictionaries of instance tags, to add to the new instance or for starting/stopping instance by tag. V({"key":"value"}).

purge_tags.description: Delete any tags not specified in the task that are on the instance. If V(true), it means you have to specify all the desired tags on each task affecting an instance.

key_name.description: The name of key pair which is used to access ECS instance in SSH.

user_data.description: User-defined data to customize the startup behaviors of an ECS instance and to pass data into an ECS instance. It only takes effect when launching the new ECS instances.

ram_role_name.description: The name of the instance RAM role.

spot_price_limit.description: The maximum hourly price for the preemptible instance. This parameter supports a maximum of three decimal places and takes effect when the SpotStrategy parameter is set to SpotWithPriceLimit.

spot_strategy.description: The bidding mode of the pay-as-you-go instance. This parameter is valid when O(instance_charge_type=PostPaid).

period_unit.description: The duration unit that you are buying the resource. It is valid when O(instance_charge_type=PrePaid).

dry_run.description: Specifies whether to send a dry-run request. If O(dry_run=true), Only a dry-run request is sent and no instance is created. The system checks whether the required parameters are set, and validates the request format, service permissions, and available ECS instances. If the validation fails, the corresponding error code is returned. If the validation succeeds, the DryRunOperation error code is returned. If O(dry_run=false), a request is sent. If the validation succeeds, the instance is created.

include_data_disks.description: Whether to change instance disks charge type when changing instance charge type.

community.general.ali_instance_info.short_description: Gather information on instances of Alibaba Cloud ECS
community.general.ali_instance_info.description: This module fetches data from the Open API in Alicloud. The module must be called from within the ECS instance itself.

name_prefix.description: Use a instance name prefix to filter ECS instances.

tags.description: A hash/dictionaries of instance tags. C({"key":"value"}).

filters.description: A dict of filters to apply. Each dict item consists of a filter key and a filter value. The filter keys can be all of request parameters. See U(https://www.alibabacloud.com/help/doc-detail/25506.htm) for parameter details. Filter keys can be same as request parameter name or be lower case and use underscore (V("_")) or dash (V("-")) to connect different words in one parameter. C(InstanceIds) should be a list. C(Tag.n.Key) and C(Tag.n.Value) should be a dict and using O(tags) instead.

community.general.alternatives.short_description: Manages alternative programs for common commands
community.general.alternatives.description: Manages symbolic links using the C(update-alternatives) tool. Useful when multiple programs are installed but provide similar functionality (for example, different editors).

name.description: The generic name of the link.

path.description: The path to the real executable that the link should point to.

family.description: The family groups similar alternatives. This option is available only on RHEL-based distributions.

link.description: The path to the symbolic link that should point to the real executable. This option is always required on RHEL-based distributions. On Debian-based distributions this option is required when the alternative O(name) is unknown to the system.

priority.description: The priority of the alternative. If no priority is given for creation V(50) is used as a fallback.

state.description: V(present) - install the alternative (if not already installed), but do not set it as the currently selected alternative for the group. V(selected) - install the alternative (if not already installed), and set it as the currently selected alternative for the group. V(auto) - install the alternative (if not already installed), and set the group to auto mode. Added in community.general 5.1.0. V(absent) - removes the alternative. Added in community.general 5.1.0.

subcommands.description: A list of subcommands. Each subcommand needs a name, a link and a path parameter. Subcommands are also named C(slaves) or C(followers), depending on the version of C(alternatives).

community.general.android_sdk.description: Manages Android SDK packages. Allows installation from different channels (stable, beta, dev, canary). Allows installation of packages to a non-default SDK root directory.

accept_licenses.description: If this is set to V(true), the module attempts to accept license prompts generated by C(sdkmanager) during package installation. Otherwise, every license prompt is rejected.

name.description: A name of an Android SDK package (for instance, V(build-tools;34.0.0)).

state.description: Indicates the desired package(s) state. V(present) ensures that package(s) is/are present. V(absent) ensures that package(s) is/are absent. V(latest) ensures that package(s) is/are installed and updated to the latest version(s).

sdk_root.description: Provides path for an alternative directory to install Android SDK packages to. By default, all packages are installed to the directory where C(sdkmanager) is installed.

channel.description: Indicates what channel must C(sdkmanager) use for installation of packages.

community.general.ansible_galaxy_install.short_description: Install Ansible roles or collections using ansible-galaxy
community.general.ansible_galaxy_install.description: This module allows the installation of Ansible collections or roles using C(ansible-galaxy).

state.description: If O(state=present) then the collection or role is installed. Note that the collections and roles are not updated with this option. Currently the O(state=latest) is ignored unless O(type=collection), and it ensures the collection is installed and updated to the latest available version. Please note that O(force=true) can be used to perform upgrade regardless of O(type).

type.description: The type of installation performed by C(ansible-galaxy). If O(type=both), then O(requirements_file) must be passed and it may contain both roles and collections. Note however that the opposite is not true: if using a O(requirements_file), then O(type) can be any of the three choices.

name.description: Name of the collection or role being installed. Versions can be specified with C(ansible-galaxy) usual formats. For example, the collection V(community.docker:1.6.1) or the role V(ansistrano.deploy,3.8.0). O(name) and O(requirements_file) are mutually exclusive.

requirements_file.description: Path to a file containing a list of requirements to be installed. It works for O(type) equals to V(collection) and V(role). O(name) and O(requirements_file) are mutually exclusive.

dest.description: The path to the directory containing your collections or roles, according to the value of O(type). Please notice that C(ansible-galaxy) does not install collections with O(type=both), when O(requirements_file) contains both roles and collections and O(dest) is specified.

force.description: Force overwriting existing roles and/or collections. It can be used for upgrading, but the module output always reports RV(ignore:changed=true). Using O(force=true) is mandatory when downgrading.

community.general.apache2_mod_proxy.short_description: Set and/or get members' attributes of an Apache httpd 2.4 mod_proxy balancer pool
community.general.apache2_mod_proxy.description: Set and/or get members' attributes of an Apache httpd 2.4 mod_proxy balancer pool, using HTTP POST and GET requests. The httpd mod_proxy balancer-member status page has to be enabled and accessible, as this module relies on parsing this page.

balancer_url_suffix.description: Suffix of the balancer pool URL required to access the balancer pool status page (for example V(balancer_vhost[:port]/balancer_url_suffix)).

balancer_vhost.description: (IPv4|IPv6|FQDN):port of the Apache httpd 2.4 mod_proxy balancer pool.

member_host.description: (IPv4|IPv6|FQDN) of the balancer member to get or to set attributes to. Port number is autodetected and should not be specified here. If undefined, the M(community.general.apache2_mod_proxy) module returns a members list of dictionaries of all the current balancer pool members' attributes.

state.description: Desired state of the member host. States can be simultaneously invoked by separating them with a comma (for example V(state=drained,ignore_errors)), but it is recommended to specify them as a proper YAML list. States V(present) and V(absent) must be used without any other state.

tls.description: Use https to access balancer management page.

validate_certs.description: Validate ssl/tls certificates.

community.general.apache2_module.short_description: Enables/disables a module of the Apache2 webserver
community.general.apache2_module.description: Enables or disables a specified module of the Apache2 webserver.

name.description: Name of the module to enable/disable as given to C(a2enmod)/C(a2dismod).

identifier.description: Identifier of the module as listed by C(apache2ctl -M). This is optional and usually determined automatically by the common convention of appending V(_module) to O(name) as well as custom exception for popular modules.

force.description: Force disabling of default modules and override Debian warnings.

state.description: Desired state of the module.

ignore_configcheck.description: Ignore configuration checks about inconsistent module configuration. Especially for mpm_* modules.

warn_mpm_absent.description: Control the behavior of the warning process for MPM modules.

community.general.apk.description: Manages C(apk) packages for Alpine Linux.

available.description: During upgrade, reset versioned world dependencies and change logic to prefer replacing or downgrading packages (instead of holding them) if the currently installed package is no longer available from any repository.

name.description: A package name, like V(foo), or multiple packages, like V(foo,bar). Do not include additional whitespace when specifying multiple packages as a string. Prefer YAML lists over comma-separating multiple package names.

no_cache.description: Do not use any local cache path.

repository.description: A package repository or multiple repositories. Unlike with the underlying apk command, this list overrides the system repositories rather than supplement them.

state.description: Indicates the desired package(s) state. V(present) ensures the package(s) is/are present. V(installed) can be used as an alias. V(absent) ensures the package(s) is/are absent. V(removed) can be used as an alias. V(latest) ensures the package(s) is/are present and the latest version(s).

update_cache.description: Update repository indexes. Can be run with other steps or on its own.

upgrade.description: Upgrade all installed packages to their latest version.

world.description: Use a custom world file when checking for explicitly installed packages. The file is used only when a value is provided for O(name), and O(state) is set to V(present) or V(latest).

community.general.apt_repo.description: Manages APT repositories using C(apt-repo) tool. See U(https://www.altlinux.org/Apt-repo) for details about C(apt-repo).

repo.description: Name of the repository to add or remove.

state.description: Indicates the desired repository state.

remove_others.description: Remove other then added repositories. Used if O(state=present).

update.description: Update the package database after changing repositories.

community.general.apt_rpm.description: Manages packages with C(apt-rpm). Both low-level (C(rpm)) and high-level (C(apt-get)) package manager binaries required.

package.description: List of packages to install, upgrade, or remove. Since community.general 8.0.0, may include paths to local C(.rpm) files if O(state=installed) or O(state=present), requires C(rpm) Python module.

state.description: Indicates the desired package state. The states V(latest) and V(present_not_latest) have been added in community.general 8.6.0. Please note before community.general 11.0.0, V(present) and V(installed) were equivalent to V(latest). This changed in community.general 11.0.0. Now they are equivalent to V(present_not_latest).

update_cache.description: Run the equivalent of C(apt-get update) before the operation. Can be run as part of the package installation or as a separate step. Default is not to update the cache.

clean.description: Run the equivalent of C(apt-get clean) to clear out the local repository of retrieved package files. It removes everything but the lock file from C(/var/cache/apt/archives/) and C(/var/cache/apt/archives/partial/). Can be run as part of the package installation (clean runs before install) or as a separate step.

dist_upgrade.description: If true performs an C(apt-get dist-upgrade) to upgrade system.

update_kernel.description: If true performs an C(update-kernel) to upgrade kernel packages.

community.general.archive.short_description: Creates a compressed archive of one or more files or trees
community.general.archive.description: Creates or extends an archive. The source and archive are on the target host, and the archive I(is not) copied to the controller host. Source files can be deleted after archival by specifying O(remove=True).

path.description: Remote absolute path, glob, or list of paths or globs for the file or files to compress or archive.

format.description: The type of compression to use.

dest.description: The file name of the destination archive. The parent directory must exists on the remote host. This is required when O(path) refers to multiple files by either specifying a glob, a directory or multiple paths in a list. If the destination archive already exists, it is truncated and overwritten.

exclude_path.description: Remote absolute path, glob, or list of paths or globs for the file or files to exclude from O(path) list and glob expansion. Use O(exclusion_patterns) to instead exclude files or subdirectories below any of the paths from the O(path) list.

exclusion_patterns.description: Glob style patterns to exclude files or directories from the resulting archive. This differs from O(exclude_path) which applies only to the source paths from O(path).

force_archive.description: Allows you to force the module to treat this as an archive even if only a single file is specified. By default when a single file is specified it is compressed only (not archived). Enable this if you want to use M(ansible.builtin.unarchive) on an archive of a single file created with this module.

remove.description: Remove any added source files and trees after adding to archive.

community.general.atomic_container.short_description: Manage the containers on the atomic host platform
community.general.atomic_container.description: Manage the containers on the atomic host platform. Allows to manage the lifecycle of a container on the atomic host platform.

backend.description: Define the backend to use for the container.

name.description: Name of the container.

image.description: The image to use to install the container.

rootfs.description: Define the rootfs of the image.

state.description: State of the container.

mode.description: Define if it is an user or a system container.

values.description: Values for the installation of the container. This option is permitted only with mode 'user' or 'system'. The values specified here will be used at installation time as --set arguments for atomic install.

community.general.atomic_container.deprecated: Project Atomic was sunset by the end of 2019.

community.general.atomic_host.description: Manage the atomic host platform. Rebooting of Atomic host platform should be done outside this module.

revision.description: The version number of the atomic host to be deployed. Providing V(latest) will upgrade to the latest available version.

community.general.atomic_host.deprecated: Project Atomic was sunset by the end of 2019.

community.general.atomic_image.short_description: Manage the container images on the atomic host platform
community.general.atomic_image.description: Manage the container images on the atomic host platform. Allows to execute the commands specified by the RUN label in the container image when present.

backend.description: Define the backend where the image is pulled.

name.description: Name of the container image.

state.description: The state of the container image. The state V(latest) will ensure container image is upgraded to the latest version and forcefully restart container, if running.

community.general.atomic_image.deprecated: Project Atomic was sunset by the end of 2019.

community.general.awall.description: This modules allows for enable/disable/activate of C(awall) policies. Alpine Wall (C(awall)) generates a firewall configuration from the enabled policy files and activates the configuration on the system.

name.description: One or more policy names.

state.description: Whether the policies should be enabled or disabled.

activate.description: Activate the new firewall rules. Can be run with other steps or on its own. Idempotency is affected if O(activate=true), as the module always reports a changed state.

community.general.beadm.short_description: Manage ZFS boot environments on FreeBSD/Solaris/illumos systems
community.general.beadm.description: Create, delete or activate ZFS boot environments. Mount and unmount ZFS boot environments.

name.description: ZFS boot environment name.

snapshot.description: If specified, the new boot environment is cloned from the given snapshot or inactive boot environment.

description.description: Associate a description with a new boot environment. This option is available only on Solarish platforms.

options.description: Create the datasets for new BE with specific ZFS properties. Multiple options can be specified. This option is available only on Solarish platforms.

mountpoint.description: Path where to mount the ZFS boot environment.

state.description: Create or delete ZFS boot environment.

force.description: Specifies if the unmount should be forced.

community.general.bigpanda.description: Notify BigPanda when deployments start and end (successfully or not). Returns a deployment object containing all the parameters for future module calls.

component.description: The name of the component being deployed. Ex: V(billing).

token.description: API token.

state.description: State of the deployment.

hosts.description: Name of affected host name. Can be a list. If not specified, it defaults to the remote system's hostname.

env.description: The environment name, typically V(production), V(staging), and so on.

owner.description: The person responsible for the deployment.

description.description: Free text description of the deployment.

url.description: Base URL of the API server.

validate_certs.description: If V(false), SSL certificates for the target URL are not validated. This should only be used on personally controlled sites using self-signed certificates.

source_system.description: Source system used in the requests to the API.

community.general.bitbucket_access_key.description: Manages Bitbucket repository access keys (also called deploy keys).

workspace.description: The repository owner. B(Note:) O(ignore:username) used to be an alias of this option. Since community.general 6.0.0 it is an alias of O(user).

key.description: The SSH public key.

state.description: Indicates desired state of the access key.

community.general.bitbucket_pipeline_key.short_description: Manages Bitbucket pipeline SSH key pair
community.general.bitbucket_pipeline_key.description: Manages Bitbucket pipeline SSH key pair.

workspace.description: The repository owner. B(Note:) O(ignore:username) used to be an alias of this option. Since community.general 6.0.0 it is an alias of O(user).

state.description: Indicates desired state of the key pair.

community.general.bitbucket_pipeline_kno.description: Manages Bitbucket pipeline known hosts under the "SSH Keys" menu. The host fingerprint is retrieved automatically, but in case of an error, one can use O(key) field to specify it manually.

workspace.description: The repository owner. B(Note:) O(ignore:username) used to be an alias of this option. Since community.general 6.0.0 it is an alias of O(user).

name.description: The FQDN of the known host.

key.description: The public key.

state.description: Indicates desired state of the record.

workspace.description: The repository owner. B(Note:) O(ignore:username) used to be an alias of this option. Since community.general 6.0.0 it is an alias of O(user).

name.description: The pipeline variable name.

value.description: The pipeline variable value.

secured.description: Whether to encrypt the variable value.

state.description: Indicates desired state of the variable.

community.general.bootc_manage.description: This module manages the switching and upgrading of C(bootc).

state.description: Control whether to apply the latest image or switch the image. B(Note:) This does not reboot the system. Please use M(ansible.builtin.reboot) to reboot the system.

image.description: The image to switch to. This is required when O(state=switch).

name.description: The name of a bower package to install.

offline.description: Install packages from local cache, if the packages were installed before.

path.description: The base path where to install the bower packages.

relative_execpath.description: Relative path to bower executable from install path.

state.description: The state of the bower package.

community.general.btrfs_info.description: Query status of available btrfs filesystems, including UUID, label, subvolumes and mountpoints.

community.general.btrfs_subvolume.description: Creates, updates and deletes btrfs subvolumes and snapshots.

automount.description: Allow the module to temporarily mount the targeted btrfs filesystem in order to validate the current state and make any required changes.

default.description: Make the subvolume specified by O(name) the filesystem's default subvolume.

filesystem_device.description: A block device contained within the btrfs filesystem to be targeted. Useful when multiple btrfs filesystems are present to specify which filesystem should be targeted.

filesystem_label.description: A descriptive label assigned to the btrfs filesystem to be targeted. Useful when multiple btrfs filesystems are present to specify which filesystem should be targeted.

filesystem_uuid.description: A unique identifier assigned to the btrfs filesystem to be targeted. Useful when multiple btrfs filesystems are present to specify which filesystem should be targeted.

name.description: Name of the subvolume/snapshot to be targeted.

recursive.description: When true, indicates that parent/child subvolumes should be created/removedas necessary to complete the operation (for O(state=present) and O(state=absent) respectively).

snapshot_source.description: Identifies the source subvolume for the created snapshot. Infers that the created subvolume is a snapshot.

snapshot_conflict.description: Policy defining behavior when a subvolume already exists at the path of the requested snapshot. V(skip) - Create a snapshot only if a subvolume does not yet exist at the target location, otherwise indicate that no change is required. Warning, this option does not yet verify that the target subvolume was generated from a snapshot of the requested source. V(clobber) - If a subvolume already exists at the requested location, delete it first. This option is not idempotent and results in a new snapshot being generated on every execution. V(error) - If a subvolume already exists at the requested location, return an error. This option is not idempotent and results in an error on replay of the module.

state.description: Indicates the current state of the targeted subvolume.

community.general.bundler.short_description: Manage Ruby Gem dependencies with Bundler
community.general.bundler.description: Manage installation and Gem version dependencies for Ruby using the Bundler gem.

executable.description: The path to the bundler executable.

state.description: The desired state of the Gem bundle. V(latest) updates gems to the most recent, acceptable version.

chdir.description: The directory to execute the bundler commands from. This directory needs to contain a valid Gemfile or .bundle/ directory. If not specified, it defaults to the temporary working directory.

exclude_groups.description: A list of Gemfile groups to exclude during operations. This only applies when O(state=present). Bundler considers this a 'remembered' property for the Gemfile and automatically excludes groups in future operations even if O(exclude_groups) is not set.

clean.description: Only applies if O(state=present). If set removes any gems on the target host that are not in the gemfile.

gemfile.description: Only applies if O(state=present). The path to the gemfile to use to install gems. If not specified it defaults to the Gemfile in current directory.

local.description: If set only installs gems from the cache on the target host.

deployment_mode.description: Only applies if O(state=present). If set it installs gems in C(./vendor/bundle) instead of the default location. Requires a C(Gemfile.lock) file to have been created prior.

user_install.description: Only applies if O(state=present). Installs gems in the local user's cache or for all users.

gem_path.description: Only applies if O(state=present). Specifies the directory to install the gems into. If O(chdir) is set then this path is relative to O(chdir). If not specified the default RubyGems gem paths are used.

binstub_directory.description: Only applies if O(state=present). Specifies the directory to install any gem bins files to. When executed the bin files run within the context of the Gemfile and fail if any required gem dependencies are not installed. If O(chdir) is set then this path is relative to O(chdir).

extra_args.description: A space separated string of additional commands that can be applied to the Bundler command. Refer to the Bundler documentation for more information.

community.general.bzr.short_description: Deploy software (or files) from bzr branches
community.general.bzr.description: Manage C(bzr) branches to deploy files or software.

name.description: SSH or HTTP protocol address of the parent branch.

dest.description: Absolute path of where the branch should be cloned to.

version.description: What version of the branch to clone. This can be the bzr revno or revid.

force.description: If V(true), any modified files in the working tree is discarded.

executable.description: Path to C(bzr) executable to use. If not supplied, the normal mechanism for resolving binary paths is used.

community.general.campfire.description: Send a message to Campfire. Messages with newlines result in a "Paste" message being sent.

token.description: API token.

room.description: Room number to which the message should be sent.

notify.description: Send a notification sound before the message.

community.general.capabilities.description: This module manipulates files privileges using the Linux capabilities(7) system.

path.description: Specifies the path to the file to be managed.

capability.description: Desired capability to set (with operator and flags, if O(state=present)) or remove (if O(state=absent)).

state.description: Whether the entry should be present or absent in the file's capabilities.

executable.description: Path to the C(cargo) installed in the system. If not specified, the module looks for C(cargo) in E(PATH).

name.description: The name of a Rust package to install.

path.description: The base path where to install the Rust packages. Cargo automatically appends V(/bin). In other words, V(/usr/local) becomes V(/usr/local/bin).

version.description: The version to install. If O(name) contains multiple values, the module tries to install all of them in this version.

locked.description: Install with locked dependencies. This is only used when installing packages.

state.description: The state of the Rust package.

directory.description: Path to the source directory to install the Rust package from. This is only used when installing packages.

features.description: List of features to activate. This is only used when installing packages.

community.general.catapult.short_description: Send a sms / mms using the catapult bandwidth API
community.general.catapult.description: Allows notifications to be sent using SMS / MMS using the catapult bandwidth API.

src.description: One of your catapult telephone numbers the message should come from (must be in E.164 format, like V(+19195551212)).

dest.description: The phone number or numbers the message should be sent to (must be in E.164 format, like V(+19195551212)).

msg.description: The contents of the text message (must be 2048 characters or less).

media.description: For MMS messages, a media URL to the location of the media to be sent with the message.

user_id.description: User ID from API account page.

api_token.description: API Token from API account page.

api_secret.description: API Secret from API account page.

community.general.catapult.deprecated: DNS fails to resolve the API endpoint used by the module since Oct 2024. See L(the associated issue, https://github.com/ansible-collections/community.general/issues/10318) for details.

community.general.circonus_annotation.description: Create an annotation event with a given category, title and description. Optionally start, end or durations can be provided.

api_key.description: Circonus API key.

start.description: Unix timestamp of event start. If not specified, it defaults to "now".

stop.description: Unix timestamp of event end. If not specified, it defaults to "now" + O(duration).

community.general.cisco_webex.short_description: Send a message to a Cisco Webex Teams Room or Individual
community.general.cisco_webex.description: Send a message to a Cisco Webex Teams Room or Individual with options to control the formatting.

recipient_type.description: The request parameter you would like to send the message to. Messages can be sent to either a room or individual (by ID or E-Mail).

recipient_id.description: The unique identifier associated with the supplied O(recipient_type).

msg_type.description: Specifies how you would like the message formatted.

personal_token.description: Your personal access token required to validate the Webex Teams API.

msg.description: The message you would like to send.

community.general.cloud_init_data_facts.description: Gathers facts by reading the C(status.json) and C(result.json) of cloud-init.

community.general.cloudflare_dns.description: Manages DNS records using the Cloudflare API, see the docs: U(https://api.cloudflare.com/).

api_token.description: API token. Required for API token authentication. You can obtain your API token from the bottom of the Cloudflare 'My Account' page, found here: U(https://dash.cloudflare.com/). Can be specified in E(CLOUDFLARE_TOKEN) environment variable since community.general 2.0.0.

account_api_key.description: Account API key. Required for API keys authentication. You can obtain your API key from the bottom of the Cloudflare 'My Account' page, found here: U(https://dash.cloudflare.com/).

account_email.description: Account email. Required for API keys authentication.

algorithm.description: Algorithm number. Required for O(type=DS) and O(type=SSHFP) when O(state=present).

cert_usage.description: Certificate usage number. Required for O(type=TLSA) when O(state=present).

comment.description: Comments or notes about the DNS record.

flag.description: Issuer Critical Flag. Should be set either to V(0) for not critical, or to V(128) for critical. Before community.general 12.3.0, only V(0) and V(1) were valid options. The value V(1) is still available for backwards-compatibility reasons. Required for O(type=CAA) when O(state=present).

tag.description: CAA issue restriction. Required for O(type=CAA) when O(state=present).

hash_type.description: Hash type number. Required for O(type=DS), O(type=SSHFP) and O(type=TLSA) when O(state=present).

key_tag.description: DNSSEC key tag. Needed for O(type=DS) when O(state=present).

port.description: Service port. Required for O(type=SRV) and O(type=TLSA).

priority.description: Record priority. Required for O(type=MX) and O(type=SRV).

proto.description: Service protocol. Required for O(type=SRV) and O(type=TLSA). Common values are TCP and UDP.

proxied.description: Proxy through Cloudflare network or just use DNS.

record.description: Record to add. Required if O(state=present). Default is V(@) (that is, the zone name).

selector.description: Selector number. Required for O(type=TLSA) when O(state=present).

solo.description: Whether the record should be the only one for that record type and record name. Only use with O(state=present). This deletes all other records with the same record name and type.

state.description: Whether the record(s) should exist or not.

tags.description: Custom tags for the DNS record.

timeout.description: Timeout for Cloudflare API calls.

ttl.description: The TTL to give the new record. Must be between V(120) and V(2,147,483,647) seconds, or V(1) for automatic.

type.description: The type of DNS record to create. Required if O(state=present). Support for V(SPF) has been removed from community.general 9.0.0 since that record type is no longer supported by CloudFlare. Support for V(PTR) has been added in community.general 11.1.0.

value.description: The record value. Required for O(state=present).

zone.description: The name of the Zone to work with (for example V(example.com)). The Zone must already exist.

host.description: The name or IP address of the Cobbler system.

port.description: Port number to be used for REST connection. The default value depends on parameter O(use_ssl).

username.description: The username to log in to Cobbler.

password.description: The password to log in to Cobbler.

use_ssl.description: If V(false), an HTTP connection is used instead of the default HTTPS connection.

validate_certs.description: If V(false), SSL certificates are not validated. This should only set to V(false) when used on personally controlled sites using self-signed certificates.

community.general.cobbler_system.description: Add, modify or remove systems in Cobbler.

host.description: The name or IP address of the Cobbler system.

port.description: Port number to be used for REST connection. The default value depends on parameter O(use_ssl).

username.description: The username to log in to Cobbler.

password.description: The password to log in to Cobbler.

use_ssl.description: If V(false), an HTTP connection is used instead of the default HTTPS connection.

validate_certs.description: If V(false), SSL certificates are not validated. This should only set to V(false) when used on personally controlled sites using self-signed certificates.

name.description: The system name to manage.

interfaces.description: A list of dictionaries containing interface options.

sync.description: Sync on changes. Concurrently syncing Cobbler is bound to fail.

state.description: Whether the system should be present, absent or a query is made.

community.general.composer.description: Composer is a tool for dependency management in PHP. It allows you to declare the dependent libraries your project needs and it installs them in your project for you.

command.description: Composer command like V(install), V(update) and so on.

arguments.description: Composer arguments like required package, version and so on.

executable.description: Path to PHP executable on the remote host, if PHP is not in E(PATH).

working_dir.description: Directory of your project (see C(--working-dir)). This is required when the command is not run globally. This is ignored if O(global_command=true).

prefer_source.description: Forces installation from package sources when possible (see C(--prefer-source)).

prefer_dist.description: Forces installation from package dist even for dev versions (see C(--prefer-dist)).

no_dev.description: Disables installation of require-dev packages (see C(--no-dev)).

no_scripts.description: Skips the execution of all scripts defined in composer.json (see C(--no-scripts)).

optimize_autoloader.description: Optimize autoloader during autoloader dump (see C(--optimize-autoloader)). Convert PSR-0/4 autoloading to classmap to get a faster autoloader. Recommended especially for production, but can take a bit of time to run.

classmap_authoritative.description: Autoload classes from classmap only. Implicitly enable optimize_autoloader. Recommended especially for production, but can take a bit of time to run.

apcu_autoloader.description: Uses APCu to cache found/not-found classes.

ignore_platform_reqs.description: Ignore C(php), C(hhvm), C(lib-*) and C(ext-*) requirements and force the installation even if the local machine does not fulfill these.

composer_executable.description: Path to composer executable on the remote host, if composer is not in E(PATH) or a custom composer is needed.

community.general.consul.short_description: Add, modify & delete services within a Consul cluster
community.general.consul.description: Registers services and checks for an agent with a Consul cluster. A service is some process running on the agent node that should be advertised by Consul's discovery mechanism. It may optionally supply a check definition, a periodic service test to notify the Consul cluster of service's health. Checks may also be registered per node, for example disk usage, or cpu usage and notify the health of the entire node to the cluster. Service level checks do not require a check name or ID as these are derived by Consul from the Service name and ID respectively by appending V(service:) Node level checks require a O(check_name) and optionally a O(check_id). Currently, there is no complete way to retrieve the script, interval or TTL metadata for a registered check. Without this metadata it is not possible to tell if the data supplied with ansible represents a change to a check. As a result this does not attempt to determine changes and it always reports a changed occurred. An API method is planned to supply this metadata so at that stage change management is to be added. See U(http://consul.io) for more details.

state.description: Register or deregister the Consul service, defaults to present.

service_name.description: Unique name for the service on a node, must be unique per node, required if registering a service. May be omitted if registering a node level check.

service_id.description: The ID for the service, must be unique per node. If O(state=absent), defaults to the service name if supplied.

host.description: Host of the Consul agent defaults to localhost.

port.description: The port on which the Consul agent is running.

scheme.description: The protocol scheme on which the Consul agent is running.

validate_certs.description: Whether to verify the TLS certificate of the Consul agent.

notes.description: Notes to attach to check when registering it.

service_port.description: The port on which the service is listening. Can optionally be supplied for registration of a service, that is if O(service_name) or O(service_id) is set.

service_address.description: The address to advertise that the service is listening on. This value is passed as the C(address) parameter to Consul's C(/v1/agent/service/register) API method, so refer to the Consul API documentation for further details.

tags.description: Tags that are attached to the service registration.

script.description: The script/command that is run periodically to check the health of the service. Requires O(interval) to be provided. Mutually exclusive with O(ttl), O(tcp) and O(http).

interval.description: The interval at which the service check is run. This is a number with a V(s) or V(m) suffix to signify the units of seconds or minutes, for example V(15s) or V(1m). If no suffix is supplied V(s) is used by default, for example V(10) is V(10s). Required if one of the parameters O(script), O(http), or O(tcp) is specified.

check_id.description: An ID for the service check. If O(state=absent), defaults to O(check_name). Ignored if part of a service definition.

check_name.description: Name for the service check. Required if standalone, ignored if part of service definition.

ttl.description: Checks can be registered with a TTL instead of a O(script) and O(interval) this means that the service checks in with the agent before the TTL expires. If it does not the check is considered failed. Required if registering a check and the script an interval are missing Similar to the interval this is a number with a V(s) or V(m) suffix to signify the units of seconds or minutes, for example V(15s) or V(1m). If no suffix is supplied V(s) is used by default, for example V(10) is equivalent to V(10s). Mutually exclusive with O(script), O(tcp) and O(http).

tcp.description: Checks can be registered with a TCP port. This means that Consul checks if the connection attempt to that port is successful (that is, the port is currently accepting connections). The format is V(host:port), for example V(localhost:80). Requires O(interval) to be provided. Mutually exclusive with O(script), O(ttl) and O(http).

http.description: Checks can be registered with an HTTP endpoint. This means that Consul checks that the http endpoint returns a successful HTTP status. Requires O(interval) to be provided. Mutually exclusive with O(script), O(ttl) and O(tcp).

timeout.description: A custom HTTP check timeout. The Consul default is 10 seconds. Similar to the interval this is a number with a V(s) or V(m) suffix to signify the units of seconds or minutes, for example V(15s) or V(1m). If no suffix is supplied V(s) is used by default, for example V(10) is equivalent to V(10s).

token.description: The token key identifying an ACL rule set. May be required to register services.

community.general.consul_acl_bootstrap.description: Allows bootstrapping of ACLs in a Consul cluster, see U(https://developer.hashicorp.com/consul/api-docs/acl#bootstrap-acls) for details.

state.description: Whether the token should be present or absent.

bootstrap_secret.description: The secret to be used as secret ID for the initial token. Needs to be an UUID.

community.general.consul_agent_check.short_description: Add, modify, and delete checks within a Consul cluster
community.general.consul_agent_check.description: Allows the addition, modification and deletion of checks in a Consul cluster using the agent. For more details on using and configuring Checks, see U(https://developer.hashicorp.com/consul/api-docs/agent/check). Currently, there is no complete way to retrieve the script, interval or TTL metadata for a registered check. Without this metadata it is not possible to tell if the data supplied with ansible represents a change to a check. As a result, the module does not attempt to determine changes and it always reports a changed occurred. An API method is planned to supply this metadata so at that stage change management is to be added.

state.description: Whether the check should be present or absent.

name.description: Required name for the service check.

id.description: Specifies a unique ID for this check on the node. This defaults to the O(name) parameter, but it may be necessary to provide an ID for uniqueness. This value is returned in the response as V(CheckId).

interval.description: The interval at which the service check is run. This is a number with a V(s) or V(m) suffix to signify the units of seconds or minutes, for example V(15s) or V(1m). If no suffix is supplied V(s) is used by default, for example V(10) is equivalent to V(10s). Required if one of the parameters O(args), O(http), or O(tcp) is specified.

notes.description: Notes to attach to check when registering it.

args.description: Specifies command arguments to run to update the status of the check. Requires O(interval) to be provided. Mutually exclusive with O(ttl), O(tcp) and O(http).

ttl.description: Checks can be registered with a TTL instead of a O(args) and O(interval) this means that the service checks in with the agent before the TTL expires. If it does not the check is considered failed. Required if registering a check and the script an interval are missing Similar to the interval this is a number with a V(s) or V(m) suffix to signify the units of seconds or minutes, for example V(15s) or V(1m). If no suffix is supplied V(s) is used by default, for example V(10) is equivalent to V(10s). Mutually exclusive with O(args), O(tcp) and O(http).

tcp.description: Checks can be registered with a TCP port. This means that Consul will check if the connection attempt to that port is successful (that is, the port is currently accepting connections). The format is V(host:port), for example V(localhost:80). Requires O(interval) to be provided. Mutually exclusive with O(args), O(ttl) and O(http).

http.description: Checks can be registered with an HTTP endpoint. This means that Consul checks that the HTTP endpoint returns a successful HTTP status. Requires O(interval) to be provided. Mutually exclusive with O(args), O(ttl) and O(tcp).

timeout.description: A custom HTTP check timeout. The Consul default is 10 seconds. Similar to the interval this is a number with a V(s) or V(m) suffix to signify the units of seconds or minutes, for example V(15s) or V(1m). If no suffix is supplied V(s) is used by default, for example V(10) is equivalent to V(10s).

service_id.description: The ID for the service, must be unique per node. If O(state=absent), defaults to the service name if supplied.

community.general.consul_agent_service.short_description: Add, modify and delete services within a Consul cluster
community.general.consul_agent_service.description: Allows the addition, modification and deletion of services in a Consul cluster using the agent. There are currently no plans to create services and checks in one. This is because the Consul API does not provide checks for a service and the checks themselves do not match the module parameters. Therefore, only a service without checks can be created in this module.

state.description: Whether the service should be present or absent.

name.description: Unique name for the service on a node, must be unique per node, required if registering a service.

id.description: Specifies a unique ID for this service. This must be unique per agent. This defaults to the O(name) parameter if not provided. If O(state=absent), defaults to the service name if supplied.

tags.description: Tags that are attached to the service registration.

address.description: The address to advertise that the service listens on. This value is passed as the C(address) parameter to Consul's C(/v1/agent/service/register) API method, so refer to the Consul API documentation for further details.

meta.description: Optional meta data used for filtering. For keys, the characters C(A-Z), C(a-z), C(0-9), C(_), C(-) are allowed. Not allowed characters are replaced with underscores.

service_port.description: The port on which the service is listening. Can optionally be supplied for registration of a service, that is if O(name) or O(id) is set.

enable_tag_override.description: Specifies to disable the anti-entropy feature for this service's tags. If C(EnableTagOverride) is set to true then external agents can update this service in the catalog and modify the tags.

community.general.consul_auth_method.description: Allows the addition, modification and deletion of auth methods in a Consul cluster using the agent. For more details on using and configuring ACLs, see U(https://www.consul.io/docs/guides/acl.html).

state.description: Whether the token should be present or absent.

name.description: Specifies a name for the ACL auth method. The name can contain alphanumeric characters, dashes C(-), and underscores C(_).

type.description: The type of auth method being configured. This field is immutable. Required when the auth method is created.

description.description: Free form human readable description of the auth method.

display_name.description: An optional name to use instead of O(name) when displaying information about this auth method.

max_token_ttl.description: This specifies the maximum life of any token created by this auth method. Can be specified in the form of V(60s) or V(5m) (that is, 60 seconds or 5 minutes, respectively).

token_locality.description: Defines the kind of token that this auth method should produce.

config.description: The raw configuration to use for the chosen auth method. Contents vary depending upon the O(type) chosen. Required when the auth method is created.

community.general.consul_binding_rule.description: Allows the addition, modification and deletion of binding rules in a Consul cluster using the agent. For more details on using and configuring binding rules, see U(https://developer.hashicorp.com/consul/api-docs/acl/binding-rules).

state.description: Whether the binding rule should be present or absent.

name.description: Specifies a name for the binding rule. Note: This is used to identify the binding rule. But since the API does not support a name, it is prefixed to the description.

description.description: Free form human readable description of the binding rule.

auth_method.description: The name of the auth method that this rule applies to.

selector.description: Specifies the expression used to match this rule against valid identities returned from an auth method validation. If empty this binding rule matches all valid identities returned from the auth method.

bind_type.description: Specifies the way the binding rule affects a token created at login.

bind_name.description: The name to bind to a token at login-time. What it binds to can be adjusted with different values of the O(bind_type) parameter.

bind_vars.description: Specifies the templated policy variables when O(bind_type) is set to V(templated-policy).

community.general.consul_kv.short_description: Manipulate entries in the key/value store of a Consul cluster
community.general.consul_kv.description: Allows the retrieval, addition, modification and deletion of key/value entries in a Consul cluster using the agent. The entire contents of the record, including the indices, flags and session are returned as RV(ignore:value). If the O(key) represents a prefix then note that when a value is removed, the existing value if any is returned as part of the results. See http://www.consul.io/docs/agent/http.html#kv for more details.

state.description: The action to take with the supplied key and value. If the state is V(present) and O(value) is set, the key contents is set to the value supplied and RV(ignore:changed) is set to V(true) only if the value was different to the current contents. If the state is V(present) and O(value) is not set, the existing value associated to the key is returned. The state V(absent) is used to remove the key/value pair, again RV(ignore:changed) is set to V(true) only if the key actually existed prior to the removal. An attempt can be made to obtain or free the lock associated with a key/value pair with the states V(acquire) or V(release) respectively. A valid session must be supplied to make the attempt RV(ignore:changed) is V(true) if the attempt is successful, V(false) otherwise.

key.description: The key at which the value should be stored.

value.description: The value should be associated with the given key, required if O(state) is V(present).

recurse.description: If the key represents a prefix, each entry with the prefix can be retrieved by setting this to V(true).

retrieve.description: If the O(state) is V(present) and O(value) is set, perform a read after setting the value and return this value.

session.description: The session that should be used to acquire or release a lock associated with a key/value pair.

token.description: The token key identifying an ACL rule set that controls access to the key value pair.

cas.description: Used when acquiring a lock with a session. If the O(cas) is V(0), then Consul only puts the key if it does not already exist. If the O(cas) value is non-zero, then the key is only set if the index matches the ModifyIndex of that key.

flags.description: Opaque positive integer value that can be passed when setting a value.

host.description: Host of the Consul agent.

port.description: The port on which the Consul agent is running.

scheme.description: The protocol scheme on which the Consul agent is running.

validate_certs.description: Whether to verify the tls certificate of the Consul agent.

datacenter.description: The name of the datacenter to query. If unspecified, the query defaults to the datacenter of the Consul agent on O(host).

community.general.consul_policy.description: Allows the addition, modification and deletion of policies in a Consul cluster using the agent. For more details on using and configuring ACLs, see U(https://www.consul.io/docs/guides/acl.html).

state.description: Whether the policy should be present or absent.

valid_datacenters.description: Valid datacenters for the policy. All if list is empty.

name.description: The name that should be associated with the policy, this is opaque to Consul.

rules.description: Rule document that should be associated with the current policy.

community.general.consul_role.description: Allows the addition, modification and deletion of roles in a Consul cluster using the agent. For more details on using and configuring ACLs, see U(https://www.consul.io/docs/guides/acl.html).

name.description: A name used to identify the role.

state.description: Whether the role should be present or absent.

description.description: Description of the role. If not specified, the assigned description is not changed.

policies.description: List of policies to attach to the role. Each policy is a dict. If the parameter is left blank, any policies currently assigned are not changed. Any empty array (V([])) clears any policies previously set.

templated_policies.description: The list of templated policies that should be applied to the role.

service_identities.description: List of service identities to attach to the role. If not specified, any service identities currently assigned are not changed. If the parameter is an empty array (V([])), any node identities assigned are unassigned.

node_identities.description: List of node identities to attach to the role. If not specified, any node identities currently assigned are not changed. If the parameter is an empty array (V([])), any node identities assigned are unassigned.

community.general.consul_session.description: Allows the addition, modification and deletion of sessions in a Consul cluster. These sessions can then be used in conjunction with key value pairs to implement distributed locks. In depth documentation for working with sessions can be found at U(http://www.consul.io/docs/internals/sessions.html).

id.description: ID of the session, required when O(state) is either V(info) or V(remove).

state.description: Whether the session should be present, in other words it should be created if it does not exist, or absent, removed if present. If created, the O(id) for the session is returned in the output. If V(absent), O(id) is required to remove the session. Info for a single session, all the sessions for a node or all available sessions can be retrieved by specifying V(info), V(node) or V(list) for the O(state); for V(node) or V(info), the node O(name) or session O(id) is required as parameter.

name.description: The name that should be associated with the session. Required when O(state=node) is used.

delay.description: The optional lock delay that can be attached to the session when it is created. Locks for invalidated sessions ar blocked from being acquired until this delay has expired. Durations are in seconds.

node.description: The name of the node that with which the session is associated. By default this is the name of the agent.

datacenter.description: The name of the datacenter in which the session exists or should be created.

checks.description: Checks that are used to verify the session health. If all the checks fail, the session is invalidated and any locks associated with the session are released and can be acquired once the associated lock delay has expired.

behavior.description: The optional behavior that can be attached to the session when it is created. This controls the behavior when a session is invalidated.

ttl.description: Specifies the duration of a session in seconds (between 10 and 86400).

community.general.consul_token.description: Allows the addition, modification and deletion of tokens in a Consul cluster using the agent. For more details on using and configuring ACLs, see U(https://www.consul.io/docs/guides/acl.html).

state.description: Whether the token should be present or absent.

accessor_id.description: Specifies a UUID to use as the token's Accessor ID. If not specified a UUID is generated for this field.

secret_id.description: Specifies a UUID to use as the token's Secret ID. If not specified a UUID is generated for this field.

description.description: Free form human readable description of the token.

policies.description: List of policies to attach to the token. Each policy is a dict. If the parameter is left blank, any policies currently assigned are not changed. Any empty array (V([])) clears any policies previously set.

roles.description: List of roles to attach to the token. Each role is a dict. If the parameter is left blank, any roles currently assigned are not changed. Any empty array (V([])) clears any roles previously set.

templated_policies.description: The list of templated policies that should be applied to the role.

service_identities.description: List of service identities to attach to the token. If not specified, any service identities currently assigned are not changed. If the parameter is an empty array (V([])), any node identities assigned are unassigned.

node_identities.description: List of node identities to attach to the token. If not specified, any node identities currently assigned are not changed. If the parameter is an empty array (V([])), any node identities assigned are unassigned.

local.description: If true, indicates that the token should not be replicated globally and instead be local to the current datacenter.

expiration_ttl.description: This is a convenience field and if set it initializes the C(expiration_time). Can be specified in the form of V(60s) or V(5m) (that is, 60 seconds or 5 minutes, respectively). Ingored when the token is updated!

community.general.copr.short_description: Manage one of the Copr repositories
community.general.copr.description: This module can enable, disable or remove the specified repository.

host.description: The Copr host to work with.

protocol.description: This indicate which protocol to use with the host.

name.description: Copr directory name, for example C(@copr/copr-dev).

state.description: Whether to set this project as V(enabled), V(disabled), or V(absent).

chroot.description: The name of the chroot that you want to enable/disable/remove in the project, for example V(epel-7-x86_64). Default chroot is determined by the operating system, version of the operating system, and architecture on which the module is run.

includepkgs.description: List of packages to include in all operations.  Inverse of O(excludepkgs), DNF will exclude any package in the repository that does not match this list.  Matches a name or a glob.

excludepkgs.description: List of packages in this repository to exclude from all operations.  Matches a name or a glob.

community.general.cpanm.description: Manage Perl library dependencies using cpanminus.

name.description: The Perl library to install. Valid values change according to the O(mode), see notes for more details. Note that for installing from a local path the parameter O(from_path) should be used.

from_path.description: The local directory or C(tar.gz) file to install from.

locallib.description: Specify the install base to install modules.

mirror.description: Specifies the base URL for the CPAN mirror to use.

mirror_only.description: Use the mirror's index file instead of the CPAN Meta DB.

install_recommendations.description: If V(true), installs dependencies declared as recommends per META spec. If V(false), it ensures the dependencies declared as recommends are not installed, overriding any decision made earlier in E(PERL_CPANM_OPT). If parameter is not set, C(cpanm) uses its existing defaults. When these dependencies fail to install, cpanm continues the installation, since they are just recommendation.

install_suggestions.description: If V(true), installs dependencies declared as suggests per META spec. If V(false), it ensures the dependencies declared as suggests are not installed, overriding any decision made earlier in E(PERL_CPANM_OPT). If parameter is not set, C(cpanm) uses its existing defaults. When these dependencies fail to install, C(cpanm) continues the installation, since they are just suggestions.

version.description: Version specification for the perl module. When O(mode=new), C(cpanm) version operators are accepted.

executable.description: Override the path to the C(cpanm) executable.

mode.description: Controls the module behavior. See notes below for more details. The default changed from V(compatibility) to V(new) in community.general 9.0.0. O(mode=new): The O(name) parameter may refer to a module name, a distribution file, a HTTP URL or a git repository URL as described in C(cpanminus) documentation. C(cpanm) version specifiers are recognized. This is the default mode from community.general 9.0.0 onwards. O(mode=compatibility): This was the default mode before community.general 9.0.0. O(name) must be either a module name or a distribution file. If the perl module given by O(name) is installed (at the exact O(version) when specified), then nothing happens. Otherwise, it is installed using the C(cpanm) executable. O(name) cannot be an URL, or a git URL. C(cpanm) version specifiers do not work in this mode. B(ATTENTION): V(compatibility) mode is deprecated and will be removed in community.general 13.0.0.

name_check.description: When O(mode=new), this parameter can be used to check if there is a module O(name) installed (at O(version), when specified).

community.general.cronvar.description: Use this module to manage crontab variables. This module allows you to create, update, or delete cron variable definitions.

name.description: Name of the crontab variable.

value.description: The value to set this variable to. Required if O(state=present).

insertafter.description: If specified, the variable is inserted after the variable specified. Used with O(state=present).

insertbefore.description: Used with O(state=present). If specified, the variable is inserted just before the variable specified.

state.description: Whether to ensure that the variable is present or absent.

user.description: The specific user whose crontab should be modified. This parameter defaults to V(root) when unset.

cron_file.description: If specified, uses this file instead of an individual user's crontab. Without a leading V(/), this is assumed to be in C(/etc/cron.d). With a leading V(/), this is taken as absolute.

backup.description: If set, create a backup of the crontab before it is modified. The location of the backup is returned in the C(backup) variable by this module.

community.general.crypttab.description: Control Linux encrypted block devices that are set up during system boot in C(/etc/crypttab).

name.description: Name of the encrypted block device as it appears in the C(/etc/crypttab) file, or optionally prefixed with V(/dev/mapper/), as it appears in the filesystem. V(/dev/mapper/) is stripped from O(name).

state.description: Use V(present) to add a line to C(/etc/crypttab) or update its definition if already present. Use V(absent) to remove a line with matching O(name). Use V(opts_present) to add options to those already present; options with different values are updated. Use V(opts_absent) to remove options from the existing set.

backing_device.description: Path to the underlying block device or file, or the UUID of a block-device prefixed with V(UUID=).

password.description: Encryption password, the path to a file containing the password, or V(-) or unset if the password should be entered at boot.

opts.description: A comma-delimited list of options. See V(crypttab(5\)) for details.

path.description: Path to file to use instead of V(/etc/crypttab). This might be useful in a chroot environment.

community.general.datadog_downtime.description: Manages downtimes within Datadog. Options as described on U(https://docs.datadoghq.com/api/v1/downtimes/).

api_key.description: Your Datadog API key.

api_host.description: The URL to the Datadog API. This value can also be set with the E(DATADOG_HOST) environment variable.

state.description: The designated state of the downtime.

id.description: The identifier of the downtime. If empty, a new downtime gets created, otherwise it is either updated or deleted depending of the O(state). To keep your playbook idempotent, you should save the identifier in a file and read it in a lookup.

monitor_tags.description: A list of monitor tags to which the downtime applies. The resulting downtime applies to monitors that match ALL provided monitor tags.

scope.description: A list of scopes to which the downtime applies. The resulting downtime applies to sources that matches ALL provided scopes.

monitor_id.description: The ID of the monitor to mute. If not provided, the downtime applies to all monitors.

downtime_message.description: A message to include with notifications for this downtime. Email notifications can be sent to specific users by using the same "@username" notation as events.

start.description: POSIX timestamp to start the downtime. If not provided, the downtime starts the moment it is created.

end.description: POSIX timestamp to end the downtime. If not provided, the downtime is in effect until you cancel it.

rrule.description: The C(RRULE) standard for defining recurring events. For example, to have a recurring event on the first day of each month, select a type of rrule and set the C(FREQ) to C(MONTHLY) and C(BYMONTHDAY) to C(1). Most common rrule options from the iCalendar Spec are supported. Attributes specifying the duration in C(RRULE) are not supported (for example C(DTSTART), C(DTEND), C(DURATION)).

community.general.datadog_event.description: Allows to post events to Datadog (www.datadoghq.com) service. Uses http://docs.datadoghq.com/api/#events API.

api_key.description: Your DataDog API key.

date_happened.description: POSIX timestamp of the event. Default value is now.

host.description: Host name to associate with the event. If not specified, it defaults to the remote system's hostname.

tags.description: Comma separated list of tags to apply to the event.

aggregation_key.description: An arbitrary string to use for aggregation.

validate_certs.description: If V(false), SSL certificates are not validated. This should only be used on personally controlled sites using self-signed certificates.
