to.description: The email-address(es) the mail is being sent to. This is a list, which may contain address and phrase portions.

cc.description: The email-address(es) the mail is being copied to. This is a list, which may contain address and phrase portions.

bcc.description: The email-address(es) the mail is being 'blind' copied to. This is a list, which may contain address and phrase portions.

subject.description: The subject of the email being sent.

body.description: The body of the email being sent.

username.description: If SMTP requires username.

password.description: If SMTP requires password.

host.description: The mail server.

port.description: The mail server port. This must be a valid integer between V(1) and V(65534).

attach.description: A list of pathnames of files to attach to the message. Attached files have their content-type set to C(application/octet-stream).

headers.description: A list of headers which should be added to the message. Each individual header is specified as V(header=value) (see example below).

charset.description: The character set of email being sent.

subtype.description: The minor mime type, can be either V(plain) or V(html). The major type is always V(text).

secure.description: If V(always), the connection only sends email if the connection is Encrypted. If the server does not accept the encrypted connection it fails. If V(try), the connection attempts to setup a secure SSL/TLS session, before trying to send. If V(never), the connection does not attempt to setup a secure SSL/TLS session, before sending. If V(starttls), the connection tries to upgrade to a secure SSL/TLS connection, before sending. If it is unable to do so it fails.

timeout.description: Sets the timeout in seconds for connection attempts.

ehlohost.description: Allows for manual specification of host for EHLO.

message_id_domain.description: The domain name to use for the L(Message-ID header, https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Message-ID).

chdir.description: Change to this directory before running make.

jobs.description: Set the number of make jobs to run concurrently. Typically if set, this would be the number of processors and/or threads available to the machine. This is not supported by all make implementations.

params.description: Any extra parameters to pass to make. If the value is empty, only the key is used. For example, V(FOO:) produces V(FOO), not V(FOO=).

target.description: The target to run. Typically this would be something like V(install), V(test), or V(all). O(target) and O(targets) are mutually exclusive.

targets.description: The list of targets to run. Typically this would be something like V(install), V(test), or V(all). O(target) and O(targets) are mutually exclusive.

community.general.manageiq_alert_profile.short_description: Configuration of alert profiles for ManageIQ
community.general.manageiq_alert_profile.description: The manageiq_alert_profiles module supports adding, updating and deleting alert profiles in ManageIQ.

state.description: V(absent) - alert profile should not exist, V(present) - alert profile should exist.

name.description: The unique alert profile name in ManageIQ.

resource_type.description: The resource type for the alert profile in ManageIQ. Required when O(state=present).

alerts.description: List of alert descriptions to assign to this profile. Required if O(state=present).

community.general.manageiq_alerts.description: The manageiq_alerts module supports adding, updating and deleting alerts in ManageIQ.

state.description: V(absent) - alert should not exist, V(present) - alert should exist.

description.description: The unique alert description in ManageIQ. Required when state is "absent" or "present".

resource_type.description: The entity type for the alert in ManageIQ. Required when O(state=present).

expression.description: The alert expression for ManageIQ. Can either be in the "Miq Expression" format or the "Hash Expression format". Required if O(state=present).

enabled.description: Enable or disable the alert. Required if O(state=present).

options.description: Additional alert options, such as notification type and frequency.

community.general.manageiq_group.description: The manageiq_group module supports adding, updating and deleting groups in ManageIQ.

state.description: V(absent) - group should not exist, V(present) - group should exist.

role.description: The the group role name. The O(role_id) has precedence over the O(role) when supplied.

tenant_id.description: The tenant for the group identified by the tenant ID.

tenant.description: The tenant for the group identified by the tenant name. The O(tenant_id) has precedence over the O(tenant) when supplied. Tenant names are case sensitive.

managed_filters_merge_mode.description: In V(merge) mode existing categories are kept or updated, new categories are added. In V(replace) mode all categories are replaced with the supplied O(managed_filters).

belongsto_filters.description: A list of strings with a reference to the allowed host, cluster or folder.

belongsto_filters_merge_mode.description: In merge mode existing settings are merged with the supplied O(belongsto_filters). In replace mode current values are replaced with the supplied O(belongsto_filters).

community.general.manageiq_policies.short_description: Management of resource policy_profiles in ManageIQ
community.general.manageiq_policies.description: The manageiq_policies module supports adding and deleting policy_profiles in ManageIQ.

state.description: V(absent) - policy_profiles should not exist, V(present) - policy_profiles should exist.

policy_profiles.description: List of dictionaries, each includes the policy_profile V(name) key. Required if O(state) is V(present) or V(absent).

resource_type.description: The type of the resource to which the profile should be [un]assigned.

resource_name.description: The name of the resource to which the profile should be [un]assigned. Must be specified if O(resource_id) is not set. Both options are mutually exclusive.

resource_id.description: The ID of the resource to which the profile should be [un]assigned. Must be specified if O(resource_name) is not set. Both options are mutually exclusive.

community.general.manageiq_policies_info.short_description: Listing of resource policy_profiles in ManageIQ
community.general.manageiq_policies_info.description: The manageiq_policies module supports listing policy_profiles in ManageIQ.

resource_type.description: The type of the resource to obtain the profile for.

resource_name.description: The name of the resource to obtain the profile for. Must be specified if O(resource_id) is not set. Both options are mutually exclusive.

resource_id.description: The ID of the resource to obtain the profile for. Must be specified if O(resource_name) is not set. Both options are mutually exclusive.

community.general.manageiq_provider.description: The manageiq_provider module supports adding, updating, and deleting provider in ManageIQ.

state.description: V(absent) - provider should not exist, V(present) - provider should be present, V(refresh) - provider is refreshed.

name.description: The provider's name.

type.description: The provider's type.

zone.description: The ManageIQ zone name that manages the provider.

provider_region.description: The provider region name to connect to (for example AWS region for Amazon).

host_default_vnc_port_start.description: The first port in the host VNC range.

host_default_vnc_port_end.description: The last port in the host VNC range.

project.description: Google Compute Engine Project ID.

tenant_mapping_enabled.description: Whether to enable mapping of existing tenants.

provider.description: Default endpoint connection information, required if state is true.

ssh_keypair.description: SSH key pair used for SSH connections to all hosts in this provider.

community.general.manageiq_tags.short_description: Management of resource tags in ManageIQ
community.general.manageiq_tags.description: The manageiq_tags module supports adding, updating and deleting tags in ManageIQ.

state.description: V(absent) - tags should not exist, V(present) - tags should exist.

tags.description: V(tags) - list of dictionaries, each includes C(name) and C(category) keys. Required if O(state) is V(present) or V(absent).

resource_type.description: The relevant resource type in manageiq.

resource_name.description: The name of the resource at which tags are be controlled. Must be specified if O(resource_id) is not set. Both options are mutually exclusive.

resource_id.description: The ID of the resource at which tags are controlled. Must be specified if O(resource_name) is not set. Both options are mutually exclusive.

community.general.manageiq_tags_info.description: This module supports retrieving resource tags from ManageIQ.

resource_type.description: The relevant resource type in ManageIQ.

resource_name.description: The name of the resource at which tags are controlled. Must be specified if O(resource_id) is not set. Both options are mutually exclusive.

resource_id.description: The ID of the resource at which tags are controlled. Must be specified if O(resource_name) is not set. Both options are mutually exclusive.

community.general.manageiq_tenant.description: The manageiq_tenant module supports adding, updating and deleting tenants in ManageIQ.

state.description: V(absent) - tenant should not exist, V(present) - tenant should be.

name.description: The tenant name.

parent_id.description: The ID of the parent tenant. If not supplied the root tenant is used. The O(parent_id) takes president over O(parent) when supplied.

parent.description: The name of the parent tenant. If not supplied and no O(parent_id) is supplied the root tenant is used.

quotas.description: The tenant quotas. All parameters case sensitive. Valid attributes are: - V(cpu_allocated) (int): use null to remove the quota. - V(mem_allocated) (GB): use null to remove the quota. - V(storage_allocated) (GB): use null to remove the quota. - V(vms_allocated) (int): use null to remove the quota. - V(templates_allocated) (int): use null to remove the quota.

community.general.manageiq_user.description: The manageiq_user module supports adding, updating and deleting users in ManageIQ.

state.description: V(absent) - user should not exist, V(present) - user should be.

userid.description: The unique userid in manageiq, often mentioned as username.

name.description: The users' full name.

password.description: The users' password.

group.description: The name of the group to which the user belongs.

update_password.description: V(always) updates passwords unconditionally. V(on_create) only sets the password for a newly created user.

community.general.mas.short_description: Manage Mac App Store applications with mas-cli
community.general.mas.description: Installs, uninstalls and updates macOS applications from the Mac App Store using the C(mas-cli).

id.description: The Mac App Store identifier of the app(s) you want to manage. This can be found by running C(mas search APP_NAME) on your machine.

state.description: Desired state of the app installation. The V(absent) value requires root permissions, also see the examples.

upgrade_all.description: Upgrade all installed Mac App Store apps.

community.general.matrix.description: This module sends HTML formatted notifications to matrix rooms.

msg_plain.description: Plain text form of the message to send to matrix, usually markdown.

msg_html.description: HTML form of the message to send to matrix.

room_id.description: ID of the room to send the notification to.

hs_url.description: URL of the homeserver, where the CS-API is reachable.

token.description: Authentication token for the API call. If provided, O(user_id) and O(password) are not required.

user_id.description: The user ID of the user.

password.description: The password to log in with.

community.general.mattermost.description: Sends notifications to U(http://your.mattermost.url) using the Incoming WebHook integration.

url.description: Mattermost URL (for example V(http://mattermost.yourcompany.com)).

api_key.description: Mattermost webhook API key. Log into your Mattermost site, go to Menu -> Integration -> Incoming Webhook -> Add Incoming Webhook. This gives you a full URL. O(api_key) is the last part. U(http://mattermost.example.com/hooks/API_KEY).

text.description: Text to send. Note that the module does not handle escaping characters. Required when O(attachments) is not set.

attachments.description: Define a list of attachments. For more information, see U(https://developers.mattermost.com/integrate/admin-guide/admin-message-attachments/). Required when O(text) is not set.

channel.description: Channel to send the message to. If absent, the message goes to the channel selected for the O(api_key).

username.description: This is the sender of the message (Username Override need to be enabled by mattermost admin, see mattermost doc).

icon_url.description: URL for the message sender's icon.

priority.description: Set a priority for the message.

validate_certs.description: If V(false), SSL certificates are not validated. This should only be used on personally controlled sites using self-signed certificates.

community.general.maven_artifact.short_description: Downloads an Artifact from a Maven Repository
community.general.maven_artifact.description: Downloads an artifact from a maven repository given the maven coordinates provided to the module. Can retrieve snapshots or release versions of the artifact and resolve the latest available version if one is not available.

version.description: The maven version coordinate. Mutually exclusive with O(version_by_spec).

version_by_spec.description: The maven dependency version ranges. See supported version ranges on U(https://cwiki.apache.org/confluence/display/MAVENOLD/Dependency+Mediation+and+Conflict+Resolution). The range type V((,1.0],[1.2,\)) and V((,1.1\),(1.1,\)) is not supported. Mutually exclusive with O(version).

repository_url.description: The URL of the Maven Repository to download from. Use V(s3://...) if the repository is hosted on Amazon S3. Use V(file://...) if the repository is local.

username.description: The username to authenticate as to the Maven Repository. Use AWS secret key of the repository is hosted on S3.

password.description: The password to authenticate with to the Maven Repository. Use AWS secret access key of the repository is hosted on S3.

headers.description: Add custom HTTP headers to a request in hash/dict format.

force_basic_auth.description: C(httplib2), the library used by the URI module only sends authentication information when a webservice responds to an initial request with a 401 status. Since some basic auth services do not properly send a 401, logins fail. This option forces the sending of the Basic authentication header upon initial request.

dest.description: The path where the artifact should be written to. If file mode or ownerships are specified and destination path already exists, they affect the downloaded file.

state.description: The desired state of the artifact.

timeout.description: Specifies a timeout in seconds for the connection attempt.

validate_certs.description: If V(false), SSL certificates are not validated. This should only be set to V(false) when no other option exists.

client_cert.description: PEM formatted certificate chain file to be used for SSL client authentication. This file can also include the key as well, and if the key is included, O(client_key) is not required.

client_key.description: PEM formatted file that contains your private key to be used for SSL client authentication. If O(client_cert) contains both the certificate and key, this option is not required.

keep_name.description: If V(true), the downloaded artifact's name is preserved, in other words the version number remains part of it. This option only has effect when O(dest) is a directory and O(version) is set to V(latest) or O(version_by_spec) is defined.

verify_checksum.description: If V(never), the MD5/SHA1 checksum is never downloaded and verified. If V(download), the MD5/SHA1 checksum is downloaded and verified only after artifact download. This is the default. If V(change), the MD5/SHA1 checksum is downloaded and verified if the destination already exist, to verify if they are identical. This was the behaviour before 2.6. Since it downloads the checksum before (maybe) downloading the artifact, and since some repository software, when acting as a proxy/cache, return a 404 error if the artifact has not been cached yet, it may fail unexpectedly. If you still need it, you should consider using V(always) instead - if you deal with a checksum, it is better to use it to verify integrity after download. V(always) combines V(download) and V(change).

checksum_alg.description: If V(md5), checksums use the MD5 algorithm. This is the default. If V(sha1), checksums use the SHA1 algorithm. This can be used on systems configured to use FIPS-compliant algorithms, since MD5 is blocked on such systems.

unredirected_headers.description: A list of headers that should not be included in the redirection. This headers are sent to the C(fetch_url) function. On ansible-core version 2.12 or later, the default of this option is V([Authorization, Cookie]). Useful if the redirection URL does not need to have sensitive headers in the request. Requires ansible-core version 2.12 or later.

directory_mode.description: Filesystem permission mode applied recursively to O(dest) when it is a directory.

community.general.memset_dns_reload.short_description: Request reload of Memset's DNS infrastructure,
community.general.memset_dns_reload.description: Request a reload of Memset's DNS infrastructure, and optionally poll until it finishes.

api_key.description: The API key obtained from the Memset control panel.

poll.description: If V(true), it polls the reload job's status and return when the job has completed (unless the 30 second timeout is reached first). If the timeout is reached then the task does not return as failed, but stderr indicates that the polling failed.

api_key.description: The API key obtained from the Memset control panel.

name.description: The Memstore product name (that is, V(mstestyaa1)).

api_key.description: The API key obtained from the Memset control panel.

name.description: The server product name (that is, C(testyaa1)).

community.general.memset_zone.short_description: Creates and deletes Memset DNS zones
community.general.memset_zone.description: Manage DNS zones in a Memset account.

state.description: Indicates desired state of resource.

api_key.description: The API key obtained from the Memset control panel.

name.description: The zone nickname; usually the same as the main domain. Ensure this value has at most 250 characters.

ttl.description: The default TTL for all records created in the zone. This must be a valid int from U(https://www.memset.com/apidocs/methods_dns.html#dns.zone_create).

force.description: Forces deletion of a zone and all zone domains/zone records it contains.

community.general.memset_zone_domain.short_description: Create and delete domains in Memset DNS zones
community.general.memset_zone_domain.description: Manage DNS zone domains in a Memset account.

state.description: Indicates desired state of resource.

api_key.description: The API key obtained from the Memset control panel.

domain.description: The zone domain name. Ensure this value has at most 250 characters.

zone.description: The zone to add the domain to (this must already exist).

community.general.memset_zone_record.short_description: Create and delete records in Memset DNS zones
community.general.memset_zone_record.description: Manage DNS records in a Memset account.

state.description: Indicates desired state of resource.

api_key.description: The API key obtained from the Memset control panel.

address.description: The address for this record (can be IP or text string depending on record type).

priority.description: C(SRV) and C(TXT) record priority, in the range 0 > 999 (inclusive).

type.description: The type of DNS record to create.

relative.description: If set then the current domain is added onto the address field for C(CNAME), C(MX), C(NS) and C(SRV)record types.

ttl.description: The record's TTL in seconds (will inherit zone's TTL if not explicitly set). This must be a valid int from U(https://www.memset.com/apidocs/methods_dns.html#dns.zone_record_create).

zone.description: The name of the zone to which to add the record to.

community.general.mksysb.description: This module manages a basic AIX mksysb (image) of rootvg.

name.description: Backup name.

software_packing.description: Exclude files from packing option listed in C(/etc/exclude_packing.rootvg).

storage_path.description: Storage path where the mksysb backup is stored.

name.description: Name of kernel module to manage.

state.description: Whether the module should be present or absent.

persistent.description: Persistency between reboots for configured module. This option creates files in C(/etc/modules-load.d/) and C(/etc/modprobe.d/) that make your module configuration persistent during reboots. If V(present), adds module name to C(/etc/modules-load.d/) and params to C(/etc/modprobe.d/) so the module is loaded on next reboot. If V(absent), comments out module name from C(/etc/modules-load.d/) and comments out params from C(/etc/modprobe.d/) so the module is not loaded on next reboot. If V(disabled), does not touch anything and leaves C(/etc/modules-load.d/) and C(/etc/modprobe.d/) as it is. Note that it is usually a better idea to rely on the automatic module loading by PCI IDs, USB IDs, DMI IDs or similar triggers encoded in the kernel modules themselves instead of configuration like this. In fact, most modern kernel modules are prepared for automatic loading already. B(Note:) This option works only with distributions that use C(systemd) when set to values other than V(disabled).

community.general.monit.short_description: Manage the state of a program monitored using Monit
community.general.monit.description: Manage the state of a program monitored using Monit.

name.description: The name of the C(monit) program/process to manage.

state.description: The state of service.

timeout.description: If there are pending actions for the service monitored by monit, then it checks for up to this many seconds to verify the requested action has been performed. The module sleeps for five seconds between each check.

community.general.mqtt.short_description: Publish a message on an MQTT topic for the IoT
community.general.mqtt.description: Publish a message on an MQTT topic.

port.description: MQTT broker port number.

username.description: Username to authenticate against the broker.

password.description: Password for O(username) to authenticate against the broker.

client_id.description: MQTT client identifier. If not specified, it uses a value C(hostname + pid).

payload.description: Payload. The special string V("None") may be used to send a NULL (that is, empty) payload which is useful to simply notify with the O(topic) or to clear previously retained messages.

retain.description: Setting this flag causes the broker to retain (in other words keep) the message so that applications that subsequently subscribe to the topic can received the last retained message immediately.

ca_cert.description: The path to the Certificate Authority certificate files that are to be treated as trusted by this client. If this is the only option given then the client operates in a similar manner to a web browser. That is to say it requires the broker to have a certificate signed by the Certificate Authorities in ca_certs and communicates using TLS v1, but does not attempt any form of authentication. This provides basic network encryption but may not be sufficient depending on how the broker is configured.

client_cert.description: The path pointing to the PEM encoded client certificate. If this is set it is used as client information for TLS based authentication. Support for this feature is broker dependent.

client_key.description: The path pointing to the PEM encoded client private key. If this is set it is used as client information for TLS based authentication. Support for this feature is broker dependent.

tls_version.description: Specifies the version of the SSL/TLS protocol to be used. By default (if the python version supports it) the highest TLS version is detected. If unavailable, TLS v1 is used.

community.general.mssql_db.short_description: Add or remove MSSQL databases from a remote host
community.general.mssql_db.description: Add or remove MSSQL databases from a remote host.

name.description: Name of the database to add or remove.

login_user.description: The username used to authenticate with.

login_password.description: The password used to authenticate with.

login_port.description: Port of the MSSQL server. Requires login_host be defined as other than localhost if login_port is used.

state.description: The database state.

target.description: Location, on the remote host, of the dump file to read from or write to. Uncompressed SQL files (C(.sql)) files are supported.

autocommit.description: Automatically commit the change only if the import succeed. Sometimes it is necessary to use autocommit=true, since some content can not be changed within a transaction.

community.general.mssql_script.short_description: Execute SQL scripts on a MSSQL database
community.general.mssql_script.description: Execute SQL scripts on a MSSQL database.

name.description: Database to run script against.

login_user.description: The username used to authenticate with.

login_password.description: The password used to authenticate with.

login_port.description: Port of the MSSQL server. Requires O(login_host) be defined as well.

script.description: The SQL script to be executed. Script can contain multiple SQL statements. Multiple Batches can be separated by V(GO) command. Each batch must return at least one result set.

transaction.description: If transactional mode is requested, start a transaction and commit the change only if the script succeed. Otherwise, rollback the transaction. If transactional mode is not requested (default), automatically commit the change.

output.description: With V(default) each row is returned as a list of values. See RV(query_results). Output format V(dict) returns dictionary with the column names as keys. See RV(query_results_dict). V(dict) requires named columns to be returned by each query otherwise an error is thrown.

params.description:
Parameters passed to the script as SQL parameters.
(Query V('SELECT %(name\)s"') with V(example: '{"name": "John Doe"}).)'.

community.general.nagios.short_description: Perform common tasks in Nagios related to downtime and notifications
community.general.nagios.description: The C(nagios) module has two basic functions: scheduling downtime and toggling alerts for services or hosts. The C(nagios) module is not idempotent. All actions require the O(host) parameter to be given explicitly. In playbooks you can use the C({{inventory_hostname}}) variable to refer to the host the playbook is currently running on. The module executes commands and needs to be run directly on the Nagios server with a user that has appropriate access rights. It does not use Nagios' HTTP API. Searches for a I(nagios.cfg) in I(/etc/nagios), I(/etc/nagios2), I(/etc/nagios3), I(/usr/local/etc/nagios), I(/usr/local/groundwork/nagios/etc), I(/omd/sites/oppy/tmp/nagios), I(/usr/local/nagios/etc), I(/usr/local/nagios), I(/opt/nagios/etc), and I(/opt/nagios), or a I(icinga.cfg) in I(/etc/icinga) and I(/usr/local/icinga/etc). (The Nagios configuration file should be readable by the Ansible user.)

action.description: Action to take. The V(acknowledge) and V(forced_check) actions were added in community.general 1.2.0.

host.description: Host to operate on in Nagios.

cmdfile.description: Path to the nagios I(command file) (FIFO pipe). Only required if auto-detection fails.

author.description: Author to leave downtime comments as. Only used when O(action) is V(downtime) or V(acknowledge).

comment.description: Comment when O(action) is V(downtime) or V(acknowledge).

start.description: When downtime should start, in C(time_t) format (epoch seconds).

minutes.description: Minutes to schedule downtime for. Only usable with O(action=downtime).

services.description: What to manage downtime/alerts for. Separate multiple services with commas. B(Required) option when O(action) is one of: V(downtime), V(acknowledge), V(forced_check), V(enable_alerts), V(disable_alerts). When specifying what O(services) to handle there is a special service value, V(host), which handles alerts/downtime/acknowledge for the I(host itself), for example O(services=host). This keyword may not be given with other services at the same time. B(Setting alerts/downtime/acknowledge for a host does not affect alerts/downtime/acknowledge for any of the services running on it.) To schedule downtime for all O(services) on particular host use keyword V(all), for example O(services=all). Before community.general 11.2.0, one could specify multiple services at once by separating them with commas, for example O(services=httpd,nfs,puppet). Since community.general 11.2.0, there can be spaces around the commas, and an actual list can be provided.

servicegroup.description: The Servicegroup we want to set downtimes/alerts for. B(Required) option when using the V(servicegroup_service_downtime) and V(servicegroup_host_downtime) O(action).

command.description: The raw command to send to Nagios, which should not include the submitted time header or the line-feed. B(Required) option when O(action=command).

community.general.netcup_dns.description: Manages DNS records using the Netcup API, see the docs U(https://ccp.netcup.net/run/webservice/servers/endpoint.php).

api_key.description: API key for authentication, must be obtained using the netcup CCP (U(https://ccp.netcup.net)).

api_password.description: API password for authentication, must be obtained using the netcup CCP (U(https://ccp.netcup.net)).

domain.description: Domainname the records should be added / removed.

record.description: Record to add or delete, supports wildcard (V(*)). Default is V(@) (that is, the zone name).

type.description: Record type. Support for V(OPENPGPKEY), V(SMIMEA) and V(SSHFP) was added in community.general 8.1.0. Record types V(OPENPGPKEY) and V(SMIMEA) require nc-dnsapi >= 0.1.5. Record type V(SSHFP) requires nc-dnsapi >= 0.1.6.

value.description: Record value.

solo.description: Whether the record should be the only one for that record type and record name. Only use with O(state=present). This deletes all other records with the same record name and type.

state.description: Whether the record should exist or not.

timeout.description: HTTP(S) connection timeout in seconds.

community.general.newrelic_deployment.short_description: Notify New Relic about app deployments
community.general.newrelic_deployment.description: Notify New Relic about app deployments (see U(https://docs.newrelic.com/docs/apm/new-relic-apm/maintenance/record-monitor-deployments/)).

token.description: API token to place in the Api-Key header.

app_name.description: The value of C(app_name) in the C(newrelic.yml) file used by the application. One of O(app_name) or O(application_id) is required.

application_id.description: The application ID found in the metadata of the application in APM. One of O(app_name) or O(application_id) is required.

changelog.description: A list of changes for this deployment.

description.description: Text annotation for the deployment - notes for you.

revision.description: A revision number (for example, git commit SHA).

user.description: The name of the user/process that triggered this deployment.

validate_certs.description: If V(false), SSL certificates are not validated. This should only be used on personally controlled sites using self-signed certificates.

app_name_exact_match.description: If this flag is set to V(true) then the application ID lookup by name would only work for an exact match. If set to V(false) it returns the first result.

community.general.nexmo.description: Send a SMS message using nexmo.

api_key.description: Nexmo API Key.

dest.description: Phone number(s) to send SMS message to.

msg.description: Message text to send. Messages longer than 160 characters are split into multiple messages.

validate_certs.description: If V(false), SSL certificates are not validated. This should only be used on personally controlled sites using self-signed certificates.

community.general.nginx_status_info.description: Gathers information from nginx from an URL having C(stub_status) enabled.

url.description: URL of the nginx status.

timeout.description: HTTP connection timeout in seconds.

community.general.nictagadm.short_description: Manage nic tags on SmartOS systems
community.general.nictagadm.description: Create or delete nic tags on SmartOS systems.

name.description: Name of the nic tag.

mac.description: Specifies the O(mac) address to attach the nic tag to when not creating an O(etherstub). Parameters O(mac) and O(etherstub) are mutually exclusive.

etherstub.description: Specifies that the nic tag is attached to a created O(etherstub). Parameter O(etherstub) is mutually exclusive with both O(mtu), and O(mac).

mtu.description: Specifies the size of the O(mtu) of the desired nic tag. Parameters O(mtu) and O(etherstub) are mutually exclusive.

force.description: When O(state=absent) this switch uses the C(-f) parameter and delete the nic tag regardless of existing VMs.

state.description: Create or delete a SmartOS nic tag.

community.general.nmcli.description: Manage the network devices. Create, modify and manage various connection and device type, for example V(ethernet), V(team), V(bond), V(vlan) and so on. On CentOS 8 and Fedora >=29 like systems, the requirements can be met by installing the following packages: C(NetworkManager). On CentOS 7 and Fedora <=28 like systems, the requirements can be met by installing the following packages: C(NetworkManager-tui). On Ubuntu and Debian like systems, the requirements can be met by installing the following packages: C(network-manager). On openSUSE, the requirements can be met by installing the following packages: C(NetworkManager).

state.description: Whether the device should exist or not, taking action if the state is different from what is stated. Using O(state=present) creates connection set to be brought up automatically. Using O(state=up) and O(state=down) does not modify connection with other parameters. These states have been added in community.general 9.5.0.

autoconnect.description: Whether the connection should start on boot. Whether the connection profile can be automatically activated.

autoconnect_priority.description: The priority of the connection profile for autoconnect. If set, connection profiles with higher priority are preferred.

autoconnect_retries.description: The number of times to retry autoconnecting.

conn_name.description: The name used to call the connection. Pattern is V(<type>[-<ifname>][-<num>]).

conn_reload.description: Whether the connection should be reloaded if it was modified.

ifname.description: The interface to bind the connection to. The connection is only applicable to this interface name. A special value of V(*) can be used for interface-independent connections. The O(ifname) argument is mandatory for all connection types except bond, team, bridge, vlan and vpn. This parameter defaults to O(conn_name) when left unset for all connection types except vpn that removes it.

type.description: This is the type of device or network connection that you wish to create or modify. Type V(dummy) is added in community.general 3.5.0. Type V(gsm) is added in community.general 3.7.0. Type V(infiniband) is added in community.general 2.0.0. Type V(loopback) is added in community.general 8.1.0. Type V(macvlan) is added in community.general 6.6.0. Type V(ovs-bridge) is added in community.general 8.6.0. Type V(ovs-interface) is added in community.general 8.6.0. Type V(ovs-port) is added in community.general 8.6.0. Type V(wireguard) is added in community.general 4.3.0. Type V(vpn) is added in community.general 5.1.0. Type V(vrf) is added in community.general 10.4.0. Using V(bond-slave), V(bridge-slave), or V(team-slave) implies V(ethernet) connection type with corresponding O(slave_type) option. If you want to control non-ethernet connection attached to V(bond), V(bridge), or V(team) consider using O(slave_type) option.

mode.description: This is the type of device or network connection that you wish to create for a bond or bridge.

transport_mode.description: This option sets the connection type of Infiniband IPoIB devices.

infiniband_mac.description: MAC address of the Infiniband IPoIB devices.

slave_type.description: Type of the device of this slave's master connection (for example V(bond)). Type V(ovs-port) is added in community.general 8.6.0.

master.description: Master <master (ifname, or connection UUID or conn_name) of bridge, team, bond, ovs-port master connection profile. Mandatory if O(slave_type) is defined.

ip4.description: List of IPv4 addresses to this interface. Use the format V(192.0.2.24/24) or V(192.0.2.24). If defined and O(method4) is not specified, automatically set C(ipv4.method) to V(manual).

gw4.description: The IPv4 gateway for this interface. Use the format V(192.0.2.1). This parameter is mutually_exclusive with never_default4 parameter.

routes4.description: The list of IPv4 routes. Use the format V(192.0.3.0/24 192.0.2.1). To specify more complex routes, use the O(routes4_extended) option.

route_metric4.description: Set metric level of ipv4 routes configured on interface.

routing_rules4.description: Is the same as in an C(ip rule add) command, except always requires specifying a priority.

routing_rules6.description: Is the same as in an C(ip rule add) command, except always requires specifying a priority.

never_default4.description: Set as default route. This parameter is mutually_exclusive with gw4 parameter.

dns4.description: A list of up to 3 DNS servers. The entries must be IPv4 addresses, for example V(192.0.2.53).

dns4_search.description: A list of DNS search domains.

dns4_ignore_auto.description: Ignore automatically configured IPv4 name servers.

method4.description: Configuration method to be used for IPv4. If O(ip4) is set, C(ipv4.method) is automatically set to V(manual) and this parameter is not needed.

may_fail4.description: If you need O(ip4) configured before C(network-online.target) is reached, set this option to V(false). This option applies when O(method4) is not V(disabled).

ip6.description: List of IPv6 addresses to this interface. Use the format V(abbe::cafe/128) or V(abbe::cafe). If defined and O(method6) is not specified, automatically set C(ipv6.method) to V(manual).

gw6.description: The IPv6 gateway for this interface. Use the format V(2001:db8::1).

routes6.description: The list of IPv6 routes. Use the format V(fd12:3456:789a:1::/64 2001:dead:beef::1). To specify more complex routes, use the O(routes6_extended) option.

routes6_extended.description: The list of IPv6 routes but with parameters.

route_metric6.description: Set metric level of IPv6 routes configured on interface.

dns6.description: A list of up to 3 DNS servers. The entries must be IPv6 addresses, for example V(2001:4860:4860::8888).

dns6_search.description: A list of DNS search domains.

dns6_ignore_auto.description: Ignore automatically configured IPv6 name servers.

method6.description: Configuration method to be used for IPv6. If O(ip6) is set, C(ipv6.method) is automatically set to V(manual) and this parameter is not needed. V(disabled) was added in community.general 3.3.0.

ip_privacy6.description: If enabled, it makes the kernel generate a temporary IPv6 address in addition to the public one.

addr_gen_mode6.description: Configure method for creating the address for use with IPv6 Stateless Address Autoconfiguration. V(default) and V(default-or-eui64) have been added in community.general 6.5.0.

mtu.description: The connection MTU, for example V(9000). This can not be applied when creating the interface and is done once the interface has been created. Can be used when modifying Team, VLAN, Ethernet (Future plans to implement wifi, gsm, pppoe, infiniband). This parameter defaults to V(1500) when unset.

dhcp_client_id.description: DHCP Client Identifier sent to the DHCP server.

primary.description: This is only used with bond and is the primary interface name (for "active-backup" mode), this is the usually the 'ifname'.

miimon.description: This is only used with bond - miimon. This parameter defaults to V(100) when unset.

downdelay.description: This is only used with bond - downdelay.

updelay.description: This is only used with bond - updelay.

xmit_hash_policy.description: This is only used with bond - xmit_hash_policy type.

fail_over_mac.description: This is only used with bond - fail_over_mac.

arp_interval.description: This is only used with bond - ARP interval.

arp_ip_target.description: This is only used with bond - ARP IP target.

stp.description: This is only used with bridge and controls whether Spanning Tree Protocol (STP) is enabled for this bridge.

priority.description: This is only used with 'bridge' - sets STP priority.

forwarddelay.description: This is only used with bridge - [forward-delay <2-30>] STP forwarding delay, in seconds.

hellotime.description: This is only used with bridge - [hello-time <1-10>] STP hello time, in seconds.

maxage.description: This is only used with bridge - [max-age <6-42>] STP maximum message age, in seconds.

ageingtime.description: This is only used with bridge - [ageing-time <0-1000000>] the Ethernet MAC address aging time, in seconds.

mac.description: MAC address of the connection. Note this requires a recent kernel feature, originally introduced in 3.15 upstream kernel.

slavepriority.description: This is only used with 'bridge-slave' - [<0-63>] - STP priority of this slave.

path_cost.description: This is only used with 'bridge-slave' - [<1-65535>] - STP port cost for destinations using this slave.

hairpin.description: This is only used with 'bridge-slave' - 'hairpin mode' for the slave, which allows frames to be sent back out through the slave the frame was received on. The default change to V(false) in community.general 7.0.0. It used to be V(true) before.

runner.description: This is the type of device or network connection that you wish to create for a team.

runner_hwaddr_policy.description: This defines the policy of how hardware addresses of team device and port devices should be set during the team lifetime.

runner_fast_rate.description: Option specifies the rate at which our link partner is asked to transmit LACPDU packets. If this is V(true) then packets are sent once per second. Otherwise they are sent every 30 seconds. Only allowed for O(runner=lacp).

vlanid.description: This is only used with VLAN - VLAN ID in range <0-4095>.

vlandev.description: This is only used with VLAN - parent device this VLAN is on, can use ifname.

flags.description: This is only used with VLAN - flags.

ingress.description: This is only used with VLAN - VLAN ingress priority mapping.

egress.description: This is only used with VLAN - VLAN egress priority mapping.

vxlan_id.description: This is only used with VXLAN - VXLAN ID.

vxlan_remote.description: This is only used with VXLAN - VXLAN destination IP address.

vxlan_parent.description: This is only used with VXLAN - VXLAN parent device (required when using a multicast remote address).

vxlan_local.description: This is only used with VXLAN - VXLAN local IP address.

ip_tunnel_dev.description: This is used with GRE/IPIP/SIT - parent device this GRE/IPIP/SIT tunnel, can use ifname.

ip_tunnel_remote.description: This is used with GRE/IPIP/SIT - GRE/IPIP/SIT destination IP address.

ip_tunnel_local.description: This is used with GRE/IPIP/SIT - GRE/IPIP/SIT local IP address.

ip_tunnel_input_key.description: The key used for tunnel input packets. Only used when O(type=gre).

ip_tunnel_output_key.description: The key used for tunnel output packets. Only used when O(type=gre).

table.description: This is only used with VRF - VRF table number.

zone.description: The trust level of the connection. When updating this property on a currently activated connection, the change takes effect immediately.

wifi_sec.description: The security configuration of the WiFi connection. Note the list of suboption attributes may vary depending on which version of NetworkManager/nmcli is installed on the host. An up-to-date list of supported attributes can be found here: U(https://networkmanager.dev/docs/api/latest/settings-802-11-wireless-security.html). For instance to use common WPA-PSK auth with a password: V({key-mgmt: wpa-psk, psk: my_password}).

ssid.description: Name of the Wireless router or the access point.

wifi.description: The configuration of the WiFi connection. Note the list of suboption attributes may vary depending on which version of NetworkManager/nmcli is installed on the host. An up-to-date list of supported attributes can be found here: U(https://networkmanager.dev/docs/api/latest/settings-802-11-wireless.html). For instance to create a hidden AP mode WiFi connection: V({hidden: true, mode: ap}).

ignore_unsupported_suboptions.description: Ignore suboptions which are invalid or unsupported by the version of NetworkManager/nmcli installed on the host. Only O(wifi) and O(wifi_sec) options are currently affected.

gsm.description: The configuration of the GSM connection. Note the list of suboption attributes may vary depending on which version of NetworkManager/nmcli is installed on the host. An up-to-date list of supported attributes can be found here: U(https://networkmanager.dev/docs/api/latest/settings-gsm.html). For instance to use apn, pin, username and password: V({apn: provider.apn, pin: 1234, username: apn.username, password: apn.password}).

macvlan.description: The configuration of the MAC VLAN connection. Note the list of suboption attributes may vary depending on which version of NetworkManager/nmcli is installed on the host. An up-to-date list of supported attributes can be found here: U(https://networkmanager.dev/docs/api/latest/settings-macvlan.html).

wireguard.description: The configuration of the Wireguard connection. Note the list of suboption attributes may vary depending on which version of NetworkManager/nmcli is installed on the host. An up-to-date list of supported attributes can be found here: U(https://networkmanager.dev/docs/api/latest/settings-wireguard.html). For instance to configure a listen port: V({listen-port: 12345}).

vpn.description: Configuration of a VPN connection (PPTP and L2TP). In order to use L2TP you need to be sure that C(network-manager-l2tp) - and C(network-manager-l2tp-gnome) if host has UI - are installed on the host.

sriov.description: Allow to configure SR-IOV settings. An up-to-date list of supported attributes can be found here: U(https://networkmanager.pages.freedesktop.org/NetworkManager/NetworkManager/settings-sriov.html).

community.general.nomad_job.description: Launch a Nomad job. Stop a Nomad job. Force start a Nomad job.

name.description: Name of job for delete, stop and start job without source. Name of job for delete, stop and start job without source. Either this or O(content) must be specified.

state.description: Deploy or remove job.

content.description: Content of Nomad job. Either this or O(name) must be specified.

content_format.description: Type of content of Nomad job.

community.general.nomad_job_info.description: Get info for one Nomad job. List Nomad jobs.

name.description: Name of job for Get info. If not specified, lists all jobs.

community.general.nomad_token.description: This module allows to create Bootstrap tokens, create ACL tokens, update ACL tokens, and delete ACL tokens.

name.description: Name of ACL token to create.

token_type.description: The type of the token can be V(client), V(management), or V(bootstrap).

policies.description: A list of the policies assigned to the token.

global_replicated.description: Indicates whether or not the token was created with the C(--global).

state.description: Create or remove ACL token.

community.general.nosh.description: Control running and enabled state for system-wide or user services. BSD and Linux systems are supported.

name.description: Name of the service to manage.

state.description: V(started)/V(stopped) are idempotent actions that do not run commands unless necessary. V(restarted) always bounces the service. V(reloaded) sends a SIGHUP or starts the service. V(reset) starts or stops the service according to whether it is enabled or not.

enabled.description: Enable or disable the service, independently of C(*.preset) file preference or running state. Mutually exclusive with O(preset). It takes effect prior to O(state=reset).

preset.description: Enable or disable the service according to local preferences in C(*.preset) files. Mutually exclusive with O(enabled). Only has an effect if set to true. It takes effect prior to O(state=reset).

user.description: Run system-control talking to the calling user's service manager, rather than the system-wide service manager.

community.general.npm.description: Manage node.js packages with Node Package Manager (npm).

name.description: The name of a node.js library to install.

path.description: The base path where to install the node.js libraries. When O(global=true), then O(path) is not used to install the packages.

global.description: Install the node.js library globally. When O(global=true), then O(path) is not used to install the packages.

executable.description: The executable location for npm. This is useful if you are using a version manager, such as nvm.

ignore_scripts.description: Use the C(--ignore-scripts) flag when installing.

unsafe_perm.description: Use the C(--unsafe-perm) flag when installing.

ci.description: Install packages based on package-lock file, same as running C(npm ci).

production.description: Install dependencies in production mode, excluding devDependencies.

registry.description: The registry to install modules from.

state.description: The state of the node.js library.

no_optional.description: Use the C(--no-optional) flag when installing.

no_bin_links.description: Use the C(--no-bin-links) flag when installing.

force.description: Use the C(--force) flag when installing.

community.general.nsupdate.description: Create, update and remove DNS records using DDNS updates.

state.description: Manage DNS record.

server.description: Apply DNS modification on this server, specified by IPv4/IPv6 address or FQDN. FQDNs are supported since community.general 12.3.0.

port.description: Use this TCP port when connecting to O(server).

key_name.description: Use TSIG key name to authenticate against DNS O(server). Not required when using O(key_algorithm=gss-tsig).

key_secret.description: Use TSIG key secret, associated with O(key_name), to authenticate against O(server). Not required when using O(key_algorithm=gss-tsig).

key_algorithm.description: Specify key algorithm used by O(key_secret). Use V(gss-tsig) for GSS-TSIG authentication (requires the gssapi library and Kerberos credentials). V(gss-tsig) was added in community.general 12.3.0.

zone.description: DNS record is modified on this O(zone). When omitted, DNS is queried to attempt finding the correct zone.

record.description: Sets the DNS record to modify. When zone is omitted this has to be absolute (ending with a dot).

type.description: Sets the record type.

value.description: Sets the record value.

protocol.description: Sets the transport protocol (TCP or UDP). TCP is the recommended and a more robust option.

community.general.ocapi_command.short_description: Manages Out-Of-Band controllers using Open Composable API (OCAPI)
community.general.ocapi_command.description: Builds OCAPI URIs locally and sends them to remote OOB controllers to perform an action. Manages OOB controller such as Indicator LED, Reboot, Power Mode, Firmware Update.

category.description: Category to execute on OOB controller.

command.description: Command to execute on OOB controller.

proxy_slot_number.description: For proxied inband requests, the slot number of the IOM. Only applies if O(baseuri) is a proxy server.

update_image_path.description: For O(command=FWUpload), the path on the local filesystem of the firmware update image.

job_name.description: For O(command=DeleteJob) command, the name of the job to delete.

username.description: Username for authenticating to OOB controller.

password.description: Password for authenticating to OOB controller.

timeout.description: Timeout in seconds for URL requests to OOB controller.

community.general.ocapi_info.short_description: Manages Out-Of-Band controllers using Open Composable API (OCAPI)
community.general.ocapi_info.description: Builds OCAPI URIs locally and sends them to remote OOB controllers to get information back.

category.description: Category to execute on OOB controller.

command.description: Command to execute on OOB controller.

proxy_slot_number.description: For proxied inband requests, the slot number of the IOM. Only applies if O(baseuri) is a proxy server.

username.description: Username for authenticating to OOB controller.

password.description: Password for authenticating to OOB controller.

timeout.description: Timeout in seconds for URL requests to OOB controller.

job_name.description: Name of job for fetching status.

community.general.oci_vcn.short_description: Manage Virtual Cloud Networks(VCN) in OCI
community.general.oci_vcn.description: This module allows the user to create, delete and update virtual cloud networks(VCNs) in OCI. The complete Oracle Cloud Infrastructure Ansible Modules can be downloaded from U(https://github.com/oracle/oci-ansible-modules/releases).

cidr_block.description: The CIDR IP address block of the VCN. Required when creating a VCN with O(state=present).

compartment_id.description: The OCID of the compartment to contain the VCN. Required when creating a VCN with O(state=present). This option is mutually exclusive with O(vcn_id).

display_name.description: A user-friendly name. Does not have to be unique, and it is changeable.

dns_label.description: A DNS label for the VCN, used in conjunction with the VNIC's hostname and subnet's DNS label to form a fully qualified domain name (FQDN) for each VNIC within this subnet (for example, V(bminstance-1.subnet123.vcn1.oraclevcn.com)). Not required to be unique, but it is a best practice to set unique DNS labels for VCNs in your tenancy. Must be an alphanumeric string that begins with a letter. The value cannot be changed.

state.description: Create or update a VCN with O(state=present). Use O(state=absent) to delete a VCN.

vcn_id.description: The OCID of the VCN. Required when deleting a VCN with O(state=absent) or updating a VCN with O(state=present). This option is mutually exclusive with O(compartment_id).

dsn.description: The connection string passed into ODBC.

params.description: Parameters to pass to the SQL query.

commit.description: Perform a commit after the execution of the SQL query. Some databases allow a commit after a select whereas others raise an exception. Default is V(true) to support legacy module behavior.

community.general.office_365_connector_c.short_description: Use webhooks to create Connector Card messages within an Office 365 group
community.general.office_365_connector_c.description: Creates Connector Card messages through Office 365 Connectors. See U(https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/microsoftteams/platform/task-modules-and-cards/cards/cards-reference#connector-card-for-microsoft-365-groups).

webhook.description: The webhook URL is given to you when you create a new Connector.

summary.description: A string used for summarizing card content. This is the message subject. This is required if the text parameter is not populated.

color.description: Accent color used for branding or indicating status in the card.

title.description: A title for the Connector message. Shown at the top of the message.

text.description: The main text of the card. This is rendered below the sender information and optional title, And above any sections or actions present.

actions.description: This array of objects is used to power the action links found at the bottom of the card.

sections.description: Contains a list of sections to display in the card. For more information see U(https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/outlook/actionable-messages/message-card-reference#section-fields).

community.general.ohai.description: Similar to the M(community.general.facter_facts) module, this runs the I(Ohai) discovery program (U(https://docs.chef.io/ohai.html)) on the remote host and returns JSON inventory data. I(Ohai) data is a bit more verbose and nested than I(facter).

community.general.omapi_host.description: Manage OMAPI hosts into compatible DHCPd servers.

state.description: Create or remove OMAPI host.

hostname.description: Sets the host lease hostname (mandatory if O(state=present)).

host.description: Sets OMAPI server host to interact with.

port.description: Sets the OMAPI server port to interact with.

key_name.description: Sets the TSIG key name for authenticating against OMAPI server.

key.description: Sets the TSIG key content for authenticating against OMAPI server.

macaddr.description: Sets the lease host MAC address.

ip.description: Sets the lease host IP address.

statements.description: Attach a list of OMAPI DHCP statements with host lease (without ending semicolon).

ddns.description: Enable dynamic DNS updates for this host.

name.description: Hostname of the machine to manage.

state.description: Takes the host to the desired lifecycle state. If V(absent) the host is deleted from the cluster. If V(present) the host is created in the cluster (includes V(enabled), V(disabled) and V(offline) states). If V(enabled) the host is fully operational. V(disabled), for example to perform maintenance operations. V(offline), host is totally offline.

im_mad_name.description: The name of the information manager, this values are taken from the oned.conf with the tag name IM_MAD (name).

vmm_mad_name.description: The name of the virtual machine manager mad name, this values are taken from the oned.conf with the tag name VM_MAD (name).

template.description: The template or attribute changes to merge into the host template.

id.description: A O(id) of the image you would like to manage.

name.description: A O(name) of the image you would like to manage. Required if O(create=true).

state.description: V(present) - state that is used to manage the image. V(absent) - delete the image. V(cloned) - clone the image. V(renamed) - rename the image to the O(new_name).

enabled.description: Whether the image should be enabled or disabled.

new_name.description: A name that is assigned to the existing or new image. In the case of cloning, by default O(new_name) is set to the name of the origin image with the prefix 'Copy of'.

persistent.description: Whether the image should be persistent or non-persistent.

create.description: Whether the image should be created if not present. This is ignored if O(state=absent).

template.description: Use with O(create=true) to specify image template.

datastore_id.description: Use with O(create=true) to specify datastore for image.

wait_timeout.description: Seconds to wait until image is ready, deleted or cloned.

ids.description: A list of images IDs whose facts you want to gather. Module can use integers too.

name.description: A O(name) of the image whose facts is gathered. If the O(name) begins with V(~) the O(name) is used as regex pattern, which restricts the list of images (whose facts is returned) whose names match specified regex. Also, if the O(name) begins with V(~*) case-insensitive matching is performed. See examples for more details.

api_url.description: URL of the OpenNebula OneFlow API server. It is recommended to use HTTPS so that the username/password are not transferred over the network unencrypted. If not set then the value of the E(ONEFLOW_URL) environment variable is used.

api_username.description: Name of the user to login into the OpenNebula OneFlow API server. If not set then the value of the E(ONEFLOW_USERNAME) environment variable is used.

api_password.description: Password of the user to login into OpenNebula OneFlow API server. If not set then the value of the E(ONEFLOW_PASSWORD) environment variable is used.

template_name.description: Name of service template to use to create a new instance of a service.

template_id.description: ID of a service template to use to create a new instance of a service.

service_id.description: ID of a service instance that you would like to manage.

service_name.description: Name of a service instance that you would like to manage.

unique.description: Setting O(unique=true) ensures that there is only one service instance running with a name set with O(service_name) when instantiating a service from a template specified with O(template_id) or O(template_name). Check examples below.

state.description: V(present) - instantiate a service from a template specified with O(template_id) or O(template_name). V(absent) - terminate an instance of a service specified with O(template_id) or O(template_name).

mode.description: Set permission mode of a service instance in octet format, for example V(0600) to give owner C(use) and C(manage) and nothing to group and others.

owner_id.description: ID of the user which is set as the owner of the service.

group_id.description: ID of the group which is set as the group of the service.

wait.description: Wait for the instance to reach RUNNING state after DEPLOYING or COOLDOWN state after SCALING.

wait_timeout.description: How long before wait gives up, in seconds.

custom_attrs.description: Dictionary of key/value custom attributes which is used when instantiating a new service.

role.description: Name of the role whose cardinality should be changed.

cardinality.description: Number of VMs for the specified role.

force.description: Force the new cardinality even if it is outside the limits.

id.description: A O(id) of the template you would like to manage. If not set then a new template is created with the given O(name).

name.description: A O(name) of the template you would like to manage. If a template with the given name does not exist it is created, otherwise it is managed by this module.

template.description: A string containing the template contents.

state.description: V(present) - state that is used to manage the template. V(absent) - delete the template.

filter.description: V(user_primary_group) - Resources belonging to the user's primary group. V(user) - Resources belonging to the user. V(all) - All resources. V(user_groups) - Resources belonging to the user and any of his groups.

api_url.description: URL of the OpenNebula RPC server. It is recommended to use HTTPS so that the username/password are not transferred over the network unencrypted. If not set then the value of the E(ONE_URL) environment variable is used.

api_username.description: Name of the user to login into the OpenNebula RPC server. If not set then the value of the E(ONE_USERNAME) environment variable is used.

api_password.description: Password of the user to login into OpenNebula RPC server. If not set then the value of the E(ONE_PASSWORD) environment variable is used. if both O(api_username) or O(api_password) are not set, then it tries to authenticate with ONE auth file. Default path is C(~/.one/one_auth). Set environment variable E(ONE_AUTH) to override this path.

template_name.description: Name of VM template to use to create a new instance.

template_id.description: ID of a VM template to use to create a new instance.

vm_start_on_hold.description: Set to true to put VM on hold while creating.

instance_ids.description: A list of instance IDs used for states: V(absent), V(running), V(rebooted), V(poweredoff).

state.description: V(present) - create instances from a template specified with C(template_id)/C(template_name). V(running) - run instances. V(poweredoff) - power-off instances. V(rebooted) - reboot instances. V(absent) - terminate instances.

hard.description: Reboot, power-off or terminate instances C(hard).

wait.description: Wait for the instance to reach its desired state before returning. Keep in mind if you are waiting for instance to be in running state it does not mean that you are able to SSH on that machine only that boot process have started on that instance. See the example using the M(ansible.builtin.wait_for) module for details.

wait_timeout.description: How long before wait gives up, in seconds.

attributes.description: A dictionary of key/value attributes to add to new instances, or for setting C(state) of instances with these attributes. Keys are case insensitive and OpenNebula automatically converts them to upper case. Be aware V(NAME) is a special attribute which sets the name of the VM when it is deployed. C(#) character(s) can be appended to the C(NAME) and the module automatically adds indexes to the names of VMs. For example: V(NAME: foo-###) would create VMs with names V(foo-000), V(foo-001),... When used with O(count_attributes) and O(exact_count) the module matches the base name without the index part.

labels.description: A list of labels to associate with new instances, or for setting C(state) of instances with these labels.

count_attributes.description: A dictionary of key/value attributes that can only be used with O(exact_count) to determine how many nodes based on a specific attributes criteria should be deployed. This can be expressed in multiple ways and is shown in the EXAMPLES section.

count_labels.description: A list of labels that can only be used with O(exact_count) to determine how many nodes based on a specific labels criteria should be deployed. This can be expressed in multiple ways and is shown in the EXAMPLES section.

exact_count.description: Indicates how many instances that match O(count_attributes) and O(count_labels) parameters should be deployed. Instances are either created or terminated based on this value. B(NOTE:) Instances with the least IDs are terminated first.

mode.description: Set permission mode of the instance in octet format, for example V(0600) to give owner C(use) and C(manage) and nothing to group and others.

owner_id.description: ID of the user which is set as the owner of the instance.

group_id.description: ID of the group which is set as the group of the instance.

memory.description: The size of the memory for new instances (in MB, GB, ..).

disk_size.description: The size of the disk created for new instances (in MB, GB, TB,...). B(NOTE:) If The Template hats Multiple Disks the Order of the Sizes is matched against the order specified in O(template_id)/O(template_name).

cpu.description: Percentage of CPU divided by 100 required for the new instance. Half a processor is written 0.5.

vcpu.description: Number of CPUs (cores) the new VM uses.

networks.description: A list of dictionaries with network parameters. See examples for more details.

disk_saveas.description: Creates an image from a VM disk. It is a dictionary where you have to specify C(name) of the new image. Optionally you can specify C(disk_id) of the disk you want to save. By default C(disk_id) is 0. B(NOTE:) This operation is only performed on the first VM (if more than one VM ID is passed) and the VM has to be in the C(poweredoff) state. Also this operation fails if an image with specified C(name) already exists.

persistent.description: Create a private persistent copy of the template plus any image defined in DISK, and instantiate that copy.

datastore_id.description: Name of Datastore to use to create a new instance.

datastore_name.description: Name of Datastore to use to create a new instance.

updateconf.description: When O(instance_ids) is provided, updates running VMs with the C(updateconf) API call. When new VMs are being created, emulates the C(updateconf) API call using direct template merge. Allows for complete modifications of the C(CONTEXT) attribute. Supported attributes include: B(BACKUP_CONFIG:) V(BACKUP_VOLATILE), V(FS_FREEZE), V(INCREMENT_MODE), V(KEEP_LAST), V(MODE); B(CONTEXT:) (Any value, except V(ETH*). Variable substitutions are made); B(CPU_MODEL:) V(FEATURES), V(MODEL); B(FEATURES:) V(ACPI), V(APIC), V(GUEST_AGENT), V(HYPERV), V(IOTHREADS), V(LOCALTIME), V(PAE), V(VIRTIO_BLK_QUEUES), V(VIRTIO_SCSI_QUEUES); B(GRAPHICS:) V(COMMAND), V(KEYMAP), V(LISTEN), V(PASSWD), V(PORT), V(TYPE); B(INPUT:) V(BUS), V(TYPE); B(OS:) V(ARCH), V(BOOT), V(BOOTLOADER), V(FIRMWARE), V(INITRD), V(KERNEL), V(KERNEL_CMD), V(MACHINE), V(ROOT), V(SD_DISK_BUS), V(UUID); B(RAW:) V(DATA), V(DATA_VMX), V(TYPE), V(VALIDATE); B(VIDEO:) V(ATS), V(IOMMU), V(RESOLUTION), V(TYPE), V(VRAM).

id.description: A O(id) of the network you would like to manage. If not set then a new network is created with the given O(name).

name.description: A O(name) of the network you would like to manage. If a network with the given name does not exist it, then is created, otherwise it is managed by this module.

template.description: A string containing the network template contents.

state.description: V(present) - state that is used to manage the network. V(absent) - delete the network.

community.general.oneandone_firewall_pol.description: Create, remove, reconfigure, update firewall policies. This module has a dependency on 1and1 >= 1.0.

state.description: Define a firewall policy state to create, remove, or update.

auth_token.description: Authenticating API token provided by 1&1.

api_url.description: Custom API URL. Overrides the E(ONEANDONE_API_URL) environment variable.

name.description: Firewall policy name used with present state. Used as identifier (id or name) when used with absent state. maxLength=128.

firewall_policy.description: The identifier (id or name) of the firewall policy used with update state.

rules.description: List of rules that are set for the firewall policy. Each rule must contain protocol parameter, in addition to three optional parameters (port_from, port_to, and source).

add_server_ips.description: A list of server identifiers (ID or name) to be assigned to a firewall policy. Used in combination with update state.

remove_server_ips.description: A list of server IP IDs to be unassigned from a firewall policy. Used in combination with update state.

add_rules.description: List of rules that are added to an existing firewall policy. It is syntax is the same as the one used for rules parameter. Used in combination with update state.

remove_rules.description: List of rule IDs that are removed from an existing firewall policy. Used in combination with update state.

wait.description: Wait for the instance to be in state 'running' before returning.

wait_timeout.description: How long before wait gives up, in seconds.

wait_interval.description: Defines the number of seconds to wait when using the _wait_for methods.

community.general.oneandone_firewall_pol.deprecated: DNS fails to resolve the API endpoint used by the module.

community.general.oneandone_load_balance.description: Create, remove, update load balancers. This module has a dependency on 1and1 >= 1.0.

state.description: Define a load balancer state to create, remove, or update.

auth_token.description: Authenticating API token provided by 1&1.

load_balancer.description: The identifier (id or name) of the load balancer used with update state.

api_url.description: Custom API URL. Overrides the E(ONEANDONE_API_URL) environment variable.

name.description: Load balancer name used with present state. Used as identifier (ID or name) when used with absent state. maxLength=128.

health_check_test.description: Type of the health check. At the moment, HTTP is not allowed.

health_check_interval.description: Health check period in seconds. minimum=5, maximum=300, multipleOf=1.

health_check_path.description: URL to call for checking. Required for HTTP health check. maxLength=1000.

health_check_parse.description: Regular expression to check. Required for HTTP health check. maxLength=64.

persistence_time.description: Persistence time in seconds. Required if persistence is enabled. minimum=30, maximum=1200, multipleOf=1.

datacenter.description: ID or country code of the datacenter where the load balancer is created. If not specified, it defaults to V(US).

rules.description: A list of rule objects that are set for the load balancer. Each rule must contain protocol, port_balancer, and port_server parameters, in addition to source parameter, which is optional.

description.description: Description of the load balancer. maxLength=256.

add_server_ips.description: A list of server identifiers (id or name) to be assigned to a load balancer. Used in combination with O(state=update).

remove_server_ips.description: A list of server IP IDs to be unassigned from a load balancer. Used in combination with O(state=update).

add_rules.description: A list of rules that are added to an existing load balancer. It is syntax is the same as the one used for rules parameter. Used in combination with O(state=update).

remove_rules.description: A list of rule IDs that are removed from an existing load balancer. Used in combination with O(state=update).

wait.description: Wait for the instance to be in state 'running' before returning.

wait_timeout.description: How long before wait gives up, in seconds.

wait_interval.description: Defines the number of seconds to wait when using the _wait_for methods.

community.general.oneandone_load_balance.deprecated: DNS fails to resolve the API endpoint used by the module.

community.general.oneandone_monitoring_p.description: Create, remove, update monitoring policies (and add/remove ports, processes, and servers). This module has a dependency on 1and1 >= 1.0.

state.description: Define a monitoring policy's state to create, remove, update.

auth_token.description: Authenticating API token provided by 1&1.

api_url.description: Custom API URL. Overrides the E(ONEANDONE_API_URL) environment variable.

name.description: Monitoring policy name used with present state. Used as identifier (id or name) when used with absent state. maxLength=128.

monitoring_policy.description: The identifier (id or name) of the monitoring policy used with update state.

thresholds.description: Monitoring policy thresholds. Each of the suboptions have warning and critical, which both have alert and value suboptions. Warning is used to set limits for warning alerts, critical is used to set critical alerts. alert enables alert, and value is used to advise when the value is exceeded.

ports.description: Array of ports that are to be monitored.

processes.description: Array of processes that are to be monitored.

add_ports.description: Ports to add to the monitoring policy.

add_processes.description: Processes to add to the monitoring policy.

add_servers.description: Servers to add to the monitoring policy.

remove_ports.description: Ports to remove from the monitoring policy.

remove_processes.description: Processes to remove from the monitoring policy.

remove_servers.description: Servers to remove from the monitoring policy.

update_ports.description: Ports to be updated on the monitoring policy.

update_processes.description: Processes to be updated on the monitoring policy.

wait.description: Wait for the instance to be in state 'running' before returning.

wait_timeout.description: How long before wait gives up, in seconds.

wait_interval.description: Defines the number of seconds to wait when using the _wait_for methods.

community.general.oneandone_monitoring_p.deprecated: DNS fails to resolve the API endpoint used by the module.

community.general.oneandone_private_netw.description: Create, remove, reconfigure, update a private network. This module has a dependency on 1and1 >= 1.0.

state.description: Define a network's state to create, remove, or update.

auth_token.description: Authenticating API token provided by 1&1.

private_network.description: The identifier (id or name) of the network used with update state.

api_url.description: Custom API URL. Overrides the E(ONEANDONE_API_URL) environment variable.

name.description: Private network name used with present state. Used as identifier (id or name) when used with absent state.

description.description: Set a description for the network.

datacenter.description: The identifier of the datacenter where the private network is created.

network_address.description: Set a private network space, for example V(192.168.1.0).

subnet_mask.description: Set the netmask for the private network, for example V(255.255.255.0).

add_members.description: List of server identifiers (name or id) to be added to the private network.

remove_members.description: List of server identifiers (name or id) to be removed from the private network.

wait.description: Wait for the instance to be in state 'running' before returning.

wait_timeout.description: How long before wait gives up, in seconds.

wait_interval.description: Defines the number of seconds to wait when using the _wait_for methods.

community.general.oneandone_private_netw.deprecated: DNS fails to resolve the API endpoint used by the module.

community.general.oneandone_public_ip.description: Create, update, and remove public IPs. This module has a dependency on 1and1 >= 1.0.

state.description: Define a public IP state to create, remove, or update.

auth_token.description: Authenticating API token provided by 1&1.

api_url.description: Custom API URL. Overrides the E(ONEANDONE_API_URL) environment variable.

datacenter.description: ID of the datacenter where the IP is created (only for unassigned IPs).

type.description: Type of IP. Currently, only IPV4 is available.

public_ip_id.description: The ID of the public IP used with update and delete states.

wait.description: Wait for the instance to be in state 'running' before returning.

wait_timeout.description: How long before wait gives up, in seconds.

wait_interval.description: Defines the number of seconds to wait when using the _wait_for methods.

community.general.oneandone_public_ip.deprecated: DNS fails to resolve the API endpoint used by the module.

community.general.oneandone_server.short_description: Create, destroy, start, stop, and reboot a 1&1 Host server
community.general.oneandone_server.description: Create, destroy, update, start, stop, and reboot a 1&1 Host server. When the server is created it can optionally wait for it to be 'running' before returning.

state.description: Define a server's state to create, remove, start or stop it.

auth_token.description: Authenticating API token provided by 1&1. Overrides the E(ONEANDONE_AUTH_TOKEN) environment variable.

api_url.description: Custom API URL. Overrides the E(ONEANDONE_API_URL) environment variable.

hostname.description: The hostname or ID of the server. Only used when state is 'present'.

appliance.description: The operating system name or ID for the server. It is required only for 'present' state.
