fixed_instance_size.description: The instance size name or ID of the server. It is required only for 'present' state, and it is mutually exclusive with vcore, cores_per_processor, ram, and hdds parameters. The available choices are: V(S), V(M), V(L), V(XL), V(XXL), V(3XL), V(4XL), V(5XL).

vcore.description: The total number of processors. It must be provided with O(cores_per_processor), O(ram), and O(hdds) parameters.

cores_per_processor.description: The number of cores per processor. It must be provided with O(vcore), O(ram), and O(hdds) parameters.

ram.description: The amount of RAM memory. It must be provided with with O(vcore), O(cores_per_processor), and O(hdds) parameters.

hdds.description: A list of hard disks with nested O(ignore:hdds[].size) and O(ignore:hdds[].is_main) properties. It must be provided with O(vcore), O(cores_per_processor), and O(ram) parameters.

private_network.description: The private network name or ID.

firewall_policy.description: The firewall policy name or ID.

load_balancer.description: The load balancer name or ID.

monitoring_policy.description: The monitoring policy name or ID.

server.description: Server identifier (ID or hostname). It is required for all states except 'running' and 'present'.

count.description: The number of servers to create.

ssh_key.description: User's public SSH key (contents, not path).

server_type.description: The type of server to be built.

wait.description: Wait for the server to be in state 'running' before returning. Also used for delete operation (set to V(false) if you do not want to wait for each individual server to be deleted before moving on with other tasks).

wait_timeout.description: How long before wait gives up, in seconds.

wait_interval.description: Defines the number of seconds to wait when using the wait_for methods.

auto_increment.description: When creating multiple servers at once, whether to differentiate hostnames by appending a count after them or substituting the count where there is a %02d or %03d in the hostname string.

community.general.oneandone_server.deprecated: DNS fails to resolve the API endpoint used by the module.

community.general.onepassword_info.description: M(community.general.onepassword_info) wraps the C(op) command line utility to fetch data about one or more 1Password items. A fatal error occurs if any of the items being searched for can not be found. Recommend using with the C(no_log) option to avoid logging the values of the secrets being retrieved.

search_terms.description: A list of one or more search terms. Each search term can either be a simple string or it can be a dictionary for more control. When passing a simple string, O(search_terms[].field) is assumed to be V(password). When passing a dictionary, the following fields are available.

auto_login.description: A dictionary containing authentication details. If this is set, the module attempts to sign in to 1Password automatically. Without this option, you must have already logged in using the 1Password CLI before running Ansible. It is B(highly) recommended to store 1Password credentials in an Ansible Vault. Ensure that the key used to encrypt the Ansible Vault is equal to or greater in strength than the 1Password master password.

cli_path.description: Used to specify the exact path to the C(op) command line interface.

community.general.oneview_datacenter_inf.short_description: Retrieve information about the OneView Data Centers
community.general.oneview_datacenter_inf.description: Retrieve information about the OneView Data Centers.

name.description: Data Center name.

options.description: Retrieve additional information. Options available: V(visualContent).

community.general.oneview_enclosure_info.short_description: Retrieve information about one or more Enclosures
community.general.oneview_enclosure_info.description: Retrieve information about one or more of the Enclosures from OneView.

name.description: Enclosure name.

options.description: List with options to gather additional information about an Enclosure and related resources. Options allowed: V(script), V(environmentalConfiguration), and V(utilization). For the option V(utilization), you can provide specific parameters.

community.general.oneview_ethernet_netwo.description: Provides an interface to manage Ethernet Network resources. Can create, update, or delete.

state.description: Indicates the desired state for the Ethernet Network resource. V(present) ensures data properties are compliant with OneView. V(absent) removes the resource from OneView, if it exists. V(default_bandwidth_reset) resets the network connection template to the default.

community.general.oneview_ethernet_netwo.short_description: Retrieve the information about one or more of the OneView Ethernet Networks
community.general.oneview_ethernet_netwo.description: Retrieve the information about one or more of the Ethernet Networks from OneView.

name.description: Ethernet Network name.

options.description: List with options to gather additional information about an Ethernet Network and related resources. Options allowed: V(associatedProfiles) and V(associatedUplinkGroups).

community.general.oneview_fc_network.short_description: Manage OneView Fibre Channel Network resources
community.general.oneview_fc_network.description: Provides an interface to manage Fibre Channel Network resources. Can create, update, and delete.

state.description: Indicates the desired state for the Fibre Channel Network resource. V(present) ensures data properties are compliant with OneView. V(absent) removes the resource from OneView, if it exists.

data.description: List with the Fibre Channel Network properties.

community.general.oneview_fc_network_inf.short_description: Retrieve the information about one or more of the OneView Fibre Channel Networks
community.general.oneview_fc_network_inf.description: Retrieve the information about one or more of the Fibre Channel Networks from OneView.

name.description: Fibre Channel Network name.

community.general.oneview_fcoe_network.description: Provides an interface to manage FCoE Network resources. Can create, update, or delete.

state.description: Indicates the desired state for the FCoE Network resource. V(present) ensures data properties are compliant with OneView. V(absent) removes the resource from OneView, if it exists.

community.general.oneview_fcoe_network_i.short_description: Retrieve the information about one or more of the OneView FCoE Networks
community.general.oneview_fcoe_network_i.description: Retrieve the information about one or more of the FCoE Networks from OneView.

name.description: FCoE Network name.

community.general.oneview_logical_interc.short_description: Manage OneView Logical Interconnect Group resources
community.general.oneview_logical_interc.description: Provides an interface to manage Logical Interconnect Group resources. Can create, update, or delete.

state.description: Indicates the desired state for the Logical Interconnect Group resource. V(absent) removes the resource from OneView, if it exists. V(present) ensures data properties are compliant with OneView.

data.description: List with the Logical Interconnect Group properties.

community.general.oneview_logical_interc.short_description: Retrieve information about one or more of the OneView Logical Interconnect Groups
community.general.oneview_logical_interc.description: Retrieve information about one or more of the Logical Interconnect Groups from OneView.

name.description: Logical Interconnect Group name.

community.general.oneview_network_set.short_description: Manage HPE OneView Network Set resources
community.general.oneview_network_set.description: Provides an interface to manage Network Set resources. Can create, update, or delete.

state.description: Indicates the desired state for the Network Set resource. V(present) ensures data properties are compliant with OneView. V(absent) removes the resource from OneView, if it exists.

data.description: List with the Network Set properties.

community.general.oneview_network_set_in.short_description: Retrieve information about the OneView Network Sets
community.general.oneview_network_set_in.description: Retrieve information about the Network Sets from OneView.

name.description: Network Set name.

options.description: List with options to gather information about Network Set. Option allowed: V(withoutEthernet). The option V(withoutEthernet) retrieves the list of network_sets excluding Ethernet networks.

community.general.oneview_san_manager.description: Provides an interface to manage SAN Manager resources. Can create, update, or delete.

state.description: Indicates the desired state for the Uplink Set resource. V(present) ensures data properties are compliant with OneView. V(absent) removes the resource from OneView, if it exists. V(connection_information_set) updates the connection information for the SAN Manager. This operation is non-idempotent.

community.general.oneview_san_manager_in.short_description: Retrieve information about one or more of the OneView SAN Managers
community.general.oneview_san_manager_in.description: Retrieve information about one or more of the SAN Managers from OneView.

params.description: List of params to delimit, filter and sort the list of resources. Params allowed: V(start): The first item to return, using 0-based indexing. V(count): The number of resources to return. V(query): A general query string to narrow the list of resources returned. V(sort): The sort order of the returned data set.

community.general.open_iscsi.description: Discover targets on given portal, (dis)connect targets, mark targets to manually or auto start, return device nodes of connected targets.

portal.description: The domain name or IP address of the iSCSI target.

port.description: The port on which the iSCSI target process listens.

login.description: Whether the target node should be connected. When O(target) is omitted, it logins to all available.

auto_node_startup.description: Whether the target node should be automatically connected at startup.

auto_portal_startup.description: Whether the target node portal should be automatically connected at startup.

discover.description: Whether the list of target nodes on the portal should be (re)discovered and added to the persistent iSCSI database. Keep in mind that C(iscsiadm) discovery resets configuration, like C(node.startup) to manual, hence combined with O(auto_node_startup=true) always returns a changed state.

show_nodes.description: Whether the list of nodes in the persistent iSCSI database should be returned by the module.

rescan.description: Rescan an established session for discovering new targets. When O(target) is omitted, it rescans all sessions.

community.general.openbsd_pkg.description: Manage packages on OpenBSD using the pkg tools.

name.description: A name or a list of names of the packages.

state.description: V(present) ensures the package is installed. V(latest) ensures the latest version of the package is installed. V(absent) ensures the specified package is not installed.

build.description: Build the package from source instead of downloading and installing a binary. Requires that the port source tree is already installed. Automatically builds and installs the C(sqlports) package, if it is not already installed. Mutually exclusive with O(snapshot).

snapshot.description: Force C(%c) and C(%m) to expand to C(snapshots), even on a release kernel. Mutually exclusive with O(build).

ports_dir.description: When used in combination with the O(build) option, allows overriding the default ports source directory.

clean.description: When updating or removing packages, delete the extra configuration file(s) in the old packages which are annotated with C(@extra) in the packaging-list.

quick.description: Replace or delete packages quickly; do not bother with checksums before removing normal files.

autoremove.description: Calls C(pkg_delete -a) to remove automatically installed packages which are no longer needed.

community.general.opendj_backendprop.short_description: Update the backend configuration of OpenDJ using the dsconfig set-backend-prop command
community.general.opendj_backendprop.description: This module updates settings for OpenDJ with the command C(set-backend-prop). It checks first using C(get-backend-prop) if configuration needs to be applied.

opendj_bindir.description: The path to the bin directory of OpenDJ.

hostname.description: The hostname of the OpenDJ server.

port.description: The Admin port on which the OpenDJ instance is available.

username.description: The username to connect with.

password.description: The password for O(username). Either O(password) or O(passwordfile) is needed.

passwordfile.description: Location to the password file which holds the password for O(username). Either O(password) or O(passwordfile) is needed.

backend.description: The name of the backend on which the property needs to be updated.

name.description: The configuration setting to update.

value.description: The value for the configuration item.

state.description: If configuration needs to be added/updated.

community.general.openwrt_init.description: Controls OpenWrt services on remote hosts.

name.description: Name of the service.

state.description: V(started)/V(stopped) are idempotent actions that do not run commands unless necessary. V(restarted) always bounces the service. V(reloaded) always reloads.

enabled.description: Whether the service should start on boot. B(At least one) of O(state) and O(enabled) are required.

pattern.description: If the service does not respond to the C(running) command, name a substring to look for as would be found in the output of the C(ps) command as a stand-in for a C(running) result. If the string is found, the service is assumed to be running.

community.general.opkg.short_description: Package manager for OpenWrt and Openembedded/Yocto based Linux distributions
community.general.opkg.description: Manages ipk packages for OpenWrt and Openembedded/Yocto based Linux distributions.

name.description: Name of package(s) to install/remove. V(NAME=VERSION) syntax is also supported to install a package in a certain version. See the examples. This only works on Yocto based Linux distributions (opkg>=0.3.2) and not for OpenWrt. This is supported since community.general 6.2.0.

state.description: State of the package.

force.description: The C(opkg --force) parameter used.

community.general.osx_defaults.description: This module allows users to read, write, and delete macOS user defaults from Ansible scripts. MacOS applications and other programs use the defaults system to record user preferences and other information that must be maintained when the applications are not running (such as default font for new documents, or the position of an Info panel).

domain.description: The domain is a domain name of the form C(com.companyname.appname).

host.description: The host on which the preference should apply. The special value V(currentHost) corresponds to the C(-currentHost) switch of the defaults commandline tool.

key.description: The key of the user preference.

type.description: The type of value to write.

check_type.description: Checks if the type of the provided O(value) matches the type of an existing default. If the types do not match, raises an error.

array_add.description: Add new elements to the array for a key which has an array as its value.

value.description: The value to write. Only required when O(state=present).

state.description: The state of the user defaults. If set to V(list) it queries the given parameter specified by O(key). Returns V(null) is nothing found or misspelled.

path.description: The path in which to search for C(defaults).

community.general.ovh_ip_failover.description: Manage OVH (French European hosting provider) IP Failover Address. For now, this module can only be used to move an IP failover (or failover block) between services.

name.description: The IP address to manage (can be a single IP like V(1.1.1.1) or a block like V(1.1.1.1/28)).

service.description: The name of the OVH service this IP address should be routed.

endpoint.description: The endpoint to use (for instance V(ovh-eu)).

wait_completion.description: If V(true), the module waits for the IP address to be moved. If false, exit without waiting. The C(taskId) is returned in module output.

wait_task_completion.description: If not V(0), the module waits for this task ID to be completed. Use O(wait_task_completion) if you want to wait for completion of a previously executed task with O(wait_completion=false). You can execute this module repeatedly on a list of failover IPs using O(wait_completion=false) (see examples).

timeout.description: The timeout in seconds used to wait for a task to be completed. Default is 120 seconds.

community.general.ovh_ip_loadbalancing_b.description: Manage OVH (French European hosting provider) LoadBalancing IP backends.

name.description: Name of the LoadBalancing internal name (V(ip-X.X.X.X)).

backend.description: The IP address of the backend to update / modify / delete.

state.description: Determines whether the backend is to be created/modified or deleted.

probe.description: Determines the type of probe to use for this backend.

weight.description: Determines the weight for this backend.

endpoint.description: The endpoint to use (for instance V(ovh-eu)).

timeout.description: The timeout in seconds used to wait for a task to be completed.

community.general.ovh_monthly_billing.description: Enable monthly billing on OVH cloud instances (be aware OVH does not allow to disable it).

project_id.description: ID of the project, get it with U(https://api.ovh.com/console/#/cloud/project#GET).

instance_id.description: ID of the instance, get it with U(https://api.ovh.com/console/#/cloud/project/%7BserviceName%7D/instance#GET).

endpoint.description: The endpoint to use (for instance V(ovh-eu)).

community.general.pacemaker_cluster.description: This module can manage a pacemaker cluster and nodes from Ansible using the pacemaker CLI.

state.description: Indicate desired state of the cluster. The value V(maintenance) has been added in community.general 11.1.0.

name.description: Specify which node of the cluster you want to manage. V(null) == the cluster status itself, V(all) == check the status of all nodes.

timeout.description: Timeout period (in seconds) for polling the cluster operation.

force.description: Force the change of the cluster state.

community.general.pacemaker_info.description: Gather information about a Pacemaker cluster.

community.general.pacemaker_resource.description: This module can manage resources in a Pacemaker cluster using the pacemaker CLI.

state.description: Indicate desired state for cluster resource. The states V(cleanup) and V(cloned) have been added in community.general 11.3.0. If O(state=cloned) or O(state=present), you can set O(resource_clone_ids) and O(resource_clone_meta) to determine exactly what and how to clone.

name.description: Specify the resource name to create or clone to. This is required if O(state=present), O(state=absent), O(state=enabled), or O(state=disabled).

resource_option.description: Specify the resource option to create.

resource_operation.description: List of operations to associate with resource.

resource_meta.description: List of meta to associate with resource.

resource_clone_ids.description: List of clone resource IDs to clone from.

resource_clone_meta.description: List of metadata to associate with clone resource.

wait.description: Timeout period for polling the resource creation.

community.general.pacemaker_stonith.description: This module manages STONITH in a Pacemaker cluster using the Pacemaker CLI.

state.description: Indicate desired state for cluster STONITH.

name.description: Specify the STONITH name to create.

stonith_options.description: Specify the STONITH option to create.

stonith_operations.description: List of operations to associate with STONITH.

stonith_metas.description: List of metadata to associate with STONITH.

agent_validation.description: Enabled agent validation for STONITH creation.

wait.description: Timeout period for polling the STONITH creation.

community.general.packet_device.short_description: Manage a bare metal server in the Packet Host
community.general.packet_device.description: Manage a bare metal server in the Packet Host (a "device" in the API terms). When the machine is created it can optionally wait for public IP address, or for active state. This module has a dependency on packet >= 1.0. API is documented at U(https://www.packet.net/developers/api/devices).

auth_token.description: Packet API token. You can also supply it in environment variable E(PACKET_API_TOKEN).

count.description: The number of devices to create. Count number can be included in hostname using the C(%d) string formatter.

count_offset.description: From which number to start the count.

device_ids.description: List of device IDs on which to operate.

tags.description: List of device tags. Currently implemented only for device creation.

facility.description: Facility slug for device creation. See Packet API for current list - U(https://www.packet.net/developers/api/facilities/).

features.description: Dict with "features" for device creation. See Packet API docs for details.

hostnames.description: A hostname of a device, or a list of hostnames. If given string or one-item list, you can use the C("%d") Python string format to expand numbers from O(count). If only one hostname, it might be expanded to list if O(count)>1.

locked.description: Whether to lock a created device.

operating_system.description: OS slug for device creation. See Packet API for current list - U(https://www.packet.net/developers/api/operatingsystems/).

plan.description: Plan slug for device creation. See Packet API for current list - U(https://www.packet.net/developers/api/plans/).

project_id.description: ID of project of the device.

state.description: Desired state of the device. If set to V(present) (the default), the module call returns immediately after the device-creating HTTP request successfully returns. If set to V(active), the module call blocks until all the specified devices are in state active due to the Packet API, or until O(wait_timeout).

user_data.description: Userdata blob made available to the machine.

wait_for_public_IPv.description: Whether to wait for the instance to be assigned a public IPv4/IPv6 address. If set to V(4), it waits until IPv4 is assigned to the instance. If set to V(6), it waits until public IPv6 is assigned to the instance.

wait_timeout.description: How long (seconds) to wait either for automatic IP address assignment, or for the device to reach the V(active) state. If O(wait_for_public_IPv) is set and O(state=active), the module waits for both events consequently, applying the timeout twice.

ipxe_script_url.description: URL of custom iPXE script for provisioning. More about custom iPXE for Packet devices at U(https://help.packet.net/technical/infrastructure/custom-ipxe).

always_pxe.description: Persist PXE as the first boot option. Normally, the PXE process happens only on the first boot. Set this arg to have your device continuously boot to iPXE.

community.general.packet_ip_subnet.short_description: Assign IP subnet to a bare metal server
community.general.packet_ip_subnet.description: Assign or unassign IPv4 or IPv6 subnets to or from a device in the Packet host. IPv4 subnets must come from already reserved block. IPv6 subnets must come from publicly routable /56 block from your project. See U(https://support.packet.com/kb/articles/elastic-ips) for more info on IP block reservation.

auth_token.description: Packet API token. You can also supply it in environment variable E(PACKET_API_TOKEN).

hostname.description: A hostname of a device to/from which to assign/remove a subnet.

device_id.description: UUID of a device to/from which to assign/remove a subnet.

project_id.description: UUID of a project of the device to/from which to assign/remove a subnet.

device_count.description: The number of devices to retrieve from the project. The max allowed value is 1000. See U(https://www.packet.com/developers/api/#retrieve-all-devices-of-a-project) for more info.

cidr.description: IPv4 or IPv6 subnet which you want to manage. It must come from a reserved block for your project in the Packet Host.

state.description: Desired state of the IP subnet on the specified device. With O(state=present), you must specify either O(hostname) or O(device_id). Subnet with given CIDR is then assigned to the specified device. With O(state=absent), you can specify either O(hostname) or O(device_id). The subnet is then removed from specified devices. If you leave both O(hostname) and O(device_id) empty, the subnet is then removed from any device it is assigned to.

community.general.packet_project.short_description: Create/delete a project in Packet host
community.general.packet_project.description: Create/delete a project in Packet host. API is documented at U(https://www.packet.com/developers/api/#projects).

state.description: Indicate desired state of the target.

payment_method.description: Payment method is name of one of the payment methods available to your user. When blank, the API assumes the default payment method.

auth_token.description: Packet API token. You can also supply it in environment variable E(PACKET_API_TOKEN).

name.description: Name for/of the project.

org_id.description: UUID of the organization to create a project for. When blank, the API assumes the default organization.

id.description: UUID of the project which you want to remove.

custom_data.description: Custom data about the project to create.

community.general.packet_sshkey.short_description: Create/delete an SSH key in Packet host
community.general.packet_sshkey.description: Create/delete an SSH key in Packet host. API is documented at U(https://www.packet.net/help/api/#page:ssh-keys,header:ssh-keys-ssh-keys-post).

state.description: Indicate desired state of the target.

auth_token.description: Packet API token. You can also supply it in environment variable E(PACKET_API_TOKEN).

label.description: Label for the key. If you keep it empty, it is read from key string.

id.description: UUID of the key which you want to remove.

fingerprint.description: Fingerprint of the key which you want to remove.

key.description: Public Key string (V({type} {base64 encoded key} {description})).

community.general.packet_volume.short_description: Create/delete a volume in Packet host
community.general.packet_volume.description: Create/delete a volume in Packet host. API is documented at U(https://www.packet.com/developers/api/#volumes).

state.description: Desired state of the volume.

project_id.description: ID of project of the device.

auth_token.description: Packet API token. You can also supply it in environment variable E(PACKET_API_TOKEN).

name.description: Selector for API-generated name of the volume.

description.description: User-defined description attribute for Packet volume. It is used used as idempotent identifier - if volume with given description exists, new one is not created.

id.description: UUID of a volume.

plan.description: V(storage_1) for standard tier, V(storage_2) for premium (performance) tier. Tiers are described at U(https://www.packet.com/cloud/storage/).

facility.description: Location of the volume. Volumes can only be attached to device in the same location.

size.description: Size of the volume in gigabytes.

community.general.packet_volume_attachme.short_description: Attach/detach a volume to a device in the Packet host
community.general.packet_volume_attachme.description: Attach/detach a volume to a device in the Packet host. API is documented at U(https://www.packet.com/developers/api/volumes/). This module creates the attachment route in the Packet API. In order to discover the block devices on the server, you have to run the Attach Scripts, as documented at U(https://help.packet.net/technical/storage/packet-block-storage-linux).

state.description: Indicate desired state of the attachment.

auth_token.description: Packet API token. You can also supply it in environment variable E(PACKET_API_TOKEN).

project_id.description: UUID of the project to which the device and volume belong.

volume.description: Selector for the volume. It can be a UUID, an API-generated volume name, or user-defined description string. Example values: V(4a347482-b546-4f67-8300-fb5018ef0c5), V(volume-4a347482), V(my volume).

device.description: Selector for the device. It can be a UUID of the device, or a hostname. Example values: 98a14f7a-3d27-4478-b7cf-35b5670523f3, "my device".

community.general.pacman.description: Manage packages with the I(pacman) package manager, which is used by Arch Linux and its variants.

name.description: Name or list of names of the package(s) or file(s) to install, upgrade, or remove. Cannot be used in combination with O(upgrade).

state.description: Whether to install (V(present) or V(installed), V(latest)), or remove (V(absent) or V(removed)) a package. V(present) and V(installed) simply ensure that a desired package is installed. V(latest) updates the specified package if it is not of the latest available version. V(absent) and V(removed) remove the specified package.

force.description: When removing packages, forcefully remove them, without any checks. Same as O(extra_args="--nodeps --nodeps"). When combined with O(update_cache), force a refresh of all package databases. Same as O(update_cache_extra_args="--refresh --refresh").

remove_nosave.description: When removing packages, do not save modified configuration files as C(.pacsave) files. (passes C(--nosave) to pacman).

executable.description: Path of the binary to use. This can either be C(pacman) or a pacman compatible AUR helper. Pacman compatibility is unfortunately ill defined, in particular, this modules makes extensive use of the C(--print-format) directive which is known not to be implemented by some AUR helpers (notably, C(yay)). Beware that AUR helpers might behave unexpectedly and are therefore not recommended.

extra_args.description: Additional option to pass to pacman when enforcing O(state).

update_cache.description: Whether or not to refresh the master package lists. This can be run as part of a package installation or as a separate step. If not specified, it defaults to V(false). Please note that this option only had an influence on the module's RV(ignore:changed) state if O(name) and O(upgrade) are not specified before community.general 5.0.0. See the examples for how to keep the old behavior.

update_cache_extra_args.description: Additional option to pass to pacman when enforcing O(update_cache).

upgrade.description: Whether or not to upgrade the whole system. Cannot be used in combination with O(name). If not specified, it defaults to V(false).

upgrade_extra_args.description: Additional option to pass to pacman when enforcing O(upgrade).

reason.description: The install reason to set for the packages.

reason_for.description: Set the install reason for V(all) packages or only for V(new) packages. In case of O(state=latest) already installed packages which are updated to a newer version are not counted as V(new).

community.general.pacman_key.short_description: Manage pacman's list of trusted keys
community.general.pacman_key.description: Add or remove gpg keys from the pacman keyring.

id.description: The 40 character identifier of the key. Including this allows check mode to correctly report the changed state. Do not specify a subkey ID, instead specify the primary key ID.

data.description: The keyfile contents to add to the keyring. Must be of C(PGP PUBLIC KEY BLOCK) type.

file.description: The path to a keyfile on the remote server to add to the keyring. Remote file must be of C(PGP PUBLIC KEY BLOCK) type.

url.description: The URL to retrieve keyfile from. Remote file must be of C(PGP PUBLIC KEY BLOCK) type.

keyserver.description: The keyserver used to retrieve key from.

verify.description: Whether or not to verify the keyfile's key ID against specified key ID.

force_update.description: This forces the key to be updated if it already exists in the keyring.

keyring.description: The full path to the keyring folder on the remote server. If not specified, module uses pacman's default (V(/etc/pacman.d/gnupg)). Useful if the remote system requires an alternative gnupg directory.

state.description: Ensures that the key is V(present) (added) or V(absent) (revoked).

ensure_trusted.description: Ensure that the key is trusted (signed by the Pacman machine key and not expired).

community.general.pagerduty.description: This module lets you create PagerDuty maintenance windows.

state.description: Create a maintenance window or get a list of ongoing windows.

name.description: PagerDuty unique subdomain. Obsolete. It is not used with PagerDuty REST v2 API.

user.description: PagerDuty user ID. Obsolete. Please, use O(token) for authorization.

token.description: A pagerduty token, generated on the pagerduty site. It is used for authorization.

requester_id.description: ID of user making the request. Only needed when creating a maintenance_window.

service.description: A comma separated list of PagerDuty service IDs.

window_id.description: ID of maintenance window. Only needed when absent a maintenance_window.

hours.description: Length of maintenance window in hours.

minutes.description: Maintenance window in minutes (this is added to the hours).

validate_certs.description: If V(false), SSL certificates are not validated. This should only be used on personally controlled sites using self-signed certificates.

community.general.pagerduty_alert.short_description: Trigger, acknowledge or resolve PagerDuty incidents
community.general.pagerduty_alert.description: This module lets you trigger, acknowledge or resolve a PagerDuty incident by sending events.

name.description: PagerDuty unique subdomain. Obsolete. It is not used with PagerDuty REST v2 API.

api_key.description: The pagerduty API key (readonly access), generated on the pagerduty site. Required if O(api_version=v1).

integration_key.description: The GUID of one of your 'Generic API' services. This is the 'integration key' listed on a 'Integrations' tab of PagerDuty service.

service_id.description: ID of PagerDuty service when incidents are triggered, acknowledged or resolved. Required if O(api_version=v1).

service_key.description: The GUID of one of your 'Generic API' services. Obsolete. Please use O(integration_key).

state.description: Type of event to be sent.

api_version.description: The API version we want to use to run the module. V1 is more limited with option we can provide to trigger incident. V2 has more variables for example, O(severity), O(source), O(custom_details) and so on.

client.description: The name of the monitoring client that is triggering this event.

client_url.description: The URL of the monitoring client that is triggering this event.

component.description: Component of the source machine that is responsible for the event, for example C(mysql) or C(eth0).

custom_details.description: Additional details about the event and affected system. A dictionary with custom keys and values.

desc.description: For O(state=triggered) - Required. Short description of the problem that led to this trigger. This field (or a truncated version) is used when generating phone calls, SMS messages and alert emails. It also appears on the incidents tables in the PagerDuty UI. The maximum length is 1024 characters. For O(state=acknowledged) or O(state=resolved) - Text that appears in the incident's log associated with this event.

incident_class.description: The class/type of the event, for example C(ping failure) or C(cpu load).

incident_key.description: Identifies the incident to which this O(state) should be applied. For O(state=triggered) - If there is no open (in other words unresolved) incident with this key, a new one is created. If there is already an open incident with a matching key, this event is appended to that incident's log. The event key provides an easy way to 'de-dup' problem reports. If no O(incident_key) is provided, then it is generated by PagerDuty. For O(state=acknowledged) or O(state=resolved) - This should be the incident_key you received back when the incident was first opened by a trigger event. Acknowledge events referencing resolved or nonexistent incidents is discarded.

link_url.description: Relevant link URL to the alert. For example, the website or the job link.

link_text.description: A short description of the O(link_url).

source.description: The unique location of the affected system, preferably a hostname or FQDN. Required in case of O(state=trigger) and O(api_version=v2).

severity.description: The perceived severity of the status the event is describing with respect to the affected system. Required in case of O(state=trigger) and O(api_version=v2).

community.general.pagerduty_change.short_description: Track a code or infrastructure change as a PagerDuty change event
community.general.pagerduty_change.description: This module lets you create a PagerDuty change event each time the module is run. This is not an idempotent action and a new change event is created each time it is run.

integration_key.description: The integration key that identifies the service the change was made to. This can be found by adding an integration to a service in PagerDuty.

summary.description: A short description of the change that occurred.

source.description: The source of the change event.

user.description: The name of the user or process that triggered this deployment.

repo.description: The URL of the project repository.

revision.description: An identifier of the revision being deployed, typically a number or SHA from a version control system.

environment.description: The environment name, typically V(production), V(staging), and so on.

link_url.description: A URL where more information about the deployment can be obtained.

link_text.description: Descriptive text for a URL where more information about the deployment can be obtained.

url.description: URL to submit the change event to.

validate_certs.description: If V(false), SSL certificates for the target URL are not validated. This should only be used on personally controlled sites using self-signed certificates.

community.general.pagerduty_user.short_description: Manage a user account on PagerDuty
community.general.pagerduty_user.description: This module manages the creation/removal of a user account on PagerDuty.

access_token.description: An API access token to authenticate with the PagerDuty REST API.

pd_user.description: Name of the user in PagerDuty.

pd_email.description: The user's email address. O(pd_email) is the unique identifier used and cannot be updated using this module.

state.description: State of the user. On V(present), it creates a user if the user does not exist. On V(absent), it removes a user if the account exists.

pd_teams.description: The teams to which the user belongs. Required if O(state=present).

community.general.pam_limits.description: The M(community.general.pam_limits) module modifies PAM limits. The default file is V(/etc/security/limits.conf). For the full documentation, see C(man 5 limits.conf).

limit_type.description: Limit type, see C(man 5 limits.conf) for an explanation.

value.description: The value of the limit. Value must either be V(unlimited), V(infinity) or V(-1), all of which indicate no limit, or a limit of 0 or larger. Value must be a number in the range -20 to 19 inclusive, if O(limit_item) is set to V(nice) or V(priority). Refer to the C(man 5 limits.conf) manual pages for more details.

backup.description: Create a backup file including the timestamp information so you can get the original file back if you somehow clobbered it incorrectly.

use_min.description: If set to V(true), the minimal value is used or conserved. If the specified value is inferior to the value in the file, file content is replaced with the new value, else content is not modified.

use_max.description: If set to V(true), the maximal value is used or conserved. If the specified value is superior to the value in the file, file content is replaced with the new value, else content is not modified.

community.general.pamd.description: Edit PAM service's type, control, module path and module arguments. In order for a PAM rule to be modified, the type, control and module_path must match an existing rule. See man(5) pam.d for details.

name.description: The name generally refers to the PAM service file to change, for example system-auth.

type.description: The type of the PAM rule being modified. The O(type), O(control), and O(module_path) options all must match a rule to be modified.

control.description: The control of the PAM rule being modified. This may be a complicated control with brackets. If this is the case, be sure to put "[bracketed controls]" in quotes. The O(type), O(control), and O(module_path) options all must match a rule to be modified.

module_path.description: The module path of the PAM rule being modified. The O(type), O(control), and O(module_path) options all must match a rule to be modified.

new_type.description: The new type to assign to the new rule.

new_control.description: The new control to assign to the new rule.

new_module_path.description: The new module path to be assigned to the new rule.

module_arguments.description: When O(state=updated), the O(module_arguments) replace existing module_arguments. When O(state=args_absent) args matching those listed in O(module_arguments) are removed. When O(state=args_present) any args listed in O(module_arguments) are added if missing from the existing rule. Furthermore, if the module argument takes a value denoted by C(=), the value changes to that specified in module_arguments.

state.description: The default of V(updated) modifies an existing rule if type, control and module_path all match an existing rule. With V(before), the new rule is inserted before a rule matching type, control and module_path. Similarly, with V(after), the new rule is inserted after an existing rulematching type, control and module_path. With either V(before) or V(after) O(new_type), O(new_control), and O(new_module_path) must all be specified. If state is V(args_absent) or V(args_present), O(new_type), O(new_control), and O(new_module_path) are ignored. State V(absent) removes the rule.

path.description: This is the path to the PAM service files.

backup.description: Create a backup file including the timestamp information so you can get the original file back if you somehow clobbered it incorrectly.

community.general.parted.description: This module allows configuring block device partition using the C(parted) command line tool. For a full description of the fields and the options check the GNU parted manual.

device.description: The block device (disk) where to operate. Regular files can also be partitioned, but it is recommended to create a loopback device using C(losetup) to easily access its partitions.

align.description: Set alignment for newly created partitions. Use V(undefined) for parted default alignment.

number.description: The partition number being affected. Required when performing any action on the disk, except fetching information.

unit.description: Selects the current default unit that Parted uses to display locations and capacities on the disk and to interpret those given by the user if they are not suffixed by an unit. When fetching information about a disk, it is recommended to always specify a unit.

label.description: Disk label type or partition table to use. If O(device) already contains a different label, it is changed to O(label) and any previous partitions are lost. A O(name) must be specified for a V(gpt) partition table.

part_type.description: May be specified only with O(label=msdos) or O(label=dvh). Neither O(part_type) nor O(name) may be used with O(label=sun).

part_start.description: Where the partition starts as offset from the beginning of the disk, that is, the "distance" from the start of the disk. Negative numbers specify distance from the end of the disk. The distance can be specified with all the units supported by parted (except compat) and it is case sensitive, for example V(10GiB), V(15%). Using negative values may require setting of O(fs_type) (see notes).

part_end.description: Where the partition ends as offset from the beginning of the disk, that is, the "distance" from the start of the disk. Negative numbers specify distance from the end of the disk. The distance can be specified with all the units supported by parted (except compat) and it is case sensitive, for example V(10GiB), V(15%).

name.description: Sets the name for the partition number (GPT, Mac, MIPS and PC98 only).

flags.description: A list of the flags that has to be set on the partition.

state.description: Whether to create or delete a partition. If set to V(info) the module only returns the device information.

fs_type.description: If specified and the partition does not exist, sets filesystem type to given partition. Parameter optional, but see notes below about negative O(part_start) values.

resize.description: Call C(resizepart) on existing partitions to match the size specified by O(part_end).

community.general.pear.description: Manage PHP packages with the pear package manager.

name.description: Name of the package to install, upgrade, or remove.

state.description: Desired state of the package.

prompts.description: List of regular expressions that can be used to detect prompts during pear package installation to answer the expected question. Prompts are processed in the same order as the packages list. You can optionally specify an answer to any question in the list. If no answer is provided, the list item must contain only the regular expression. To specify an answer, the item must be a dictionary with the regular expression as key and the answer as value C(my_regular_expression: 'an_answer'). You can provide a list containing items with or without answer. A prompt list can be shorter or longer than the packages list but it issues a warning. If you want to specify that a package does not need prompts in the middle of a list, V(null).

community.general.pids.short_description: Retrieves process IDs list if the process is running otherwise return empty list
community.general.pids.description: Retrieves a list of PIDs of given process name in Ansible controller/controlled machines. Returns an empty list if no process in that name exists.

name.description: The name of the process(es) you want to get PID(s) for.

pattern.description: The pattern (regular expression) to match the process(es) you want to get PID(s) for.

ignore_case.description: Ignore case in pattern if using the O(pattern) option.

community.general.pingdom.description: This module lets you pause/unpause Pingdom alerts.

state.description: Define whether or not the check should be running or paused.

key.description: Pingdom API key.

community.general.pip_package_info.description: Return information about installed pip packages.

clients.description: A list of the pip executables that are used to get the packages. They can be supplied with the full path or just the executable name, for example V(pip3.7).

community.general.pipx.description: Manage Python applications installed in isolated virtualenvs using pipx.

state.description: Desired state for the application. The states V(present) and V(absent) are aliases to V(install) and V(uninstall), respectively. The state V(latest) is equivalent to executing the task twice, with state V(install) and then V(upgrade). It was added in community.general 5.5.0. The states V(install_all), V(uninject), V(upgrade_shared), V(pin) and V(unpin) are only available in C(pipx>=1.6.0), make sure to have a compatible version when using this option. These states have been added in community.general 9.4.0.

name.description: The name of the application and also the name of the Python package being installed. In C(pipx) documentation it is also referred to as the name of the virtual environment where the application is installed. If O(name) is a simple package name without version specifiers, then that name is used as the Python package name to be installed. Starting in community.general 10.7.0, you can use package specifiers when O(state=present) or O(state=install). For example, O(name=tox<4.0.0) or O(name=tox>3.0.27). Please note that when you use O(state=present) and O(name) with version specifiers, contrary to the behavior of C(pipx), this module honors the version specifier and installs a version of the application that satisfies it. If you want to ensure the reinstallation of the application even when the version specifier is met, then you must use O(force=true), or perhaps use O(state=upgrade) instead. Use O(source) for installing from URLs or directories.

source.description: Source for the package. This option is used when O(state=install) or O(state=latest), and it is ignored with other states. Use O(source) when installing a Python package with version specifier, or from a local path, from a VCS URL or compressed file. The value of this option is passed as-is to C(pipx). O(name) is still required when using O(source) to establish the application name without fetching the package from a remote source. The module is not idempotent when using O(source).

install_apps.description: Add apps from the injected packages. Only used when O(state=inject).

install_deps.description: Include applications of dependent packages. Only used when O(state=install), O(state=latest), or O(state=inject).

inject_packages.description: Packages to be injected into an existing virtual environment. Only used when O(state=inject).

force.description: Force modification of the application's virtual environment. See C(pipx) for details. Only used when O(state=install), O(state=upgrade), O(state=upgrade_all), O(state=latest), or O(state=inject). The module is not idempotent when O(force=true).

include_injected.description: Upgrade the injected packages along with the application. Only used when O(state=upgrade), O(state=upgrade_all), or O(state=latest). This is used with O(state=upgrade) and O(state=latest) since community.general 6.6.0.

index_url.description: Base URL of Python Package Index. Only used when O(state=install), O(state=upgrade), O(state=latest), or O(state=inject).

python.description: Python version to be used when creating the application virtual environment. Must be 3.6+. Only used when O(state=install), O(state=latest), O(state=reinstall), or O(state=reinstall_all).

system_site_packages.description: Give application virtual environment access to the system site-packages directory. Only used when O(state=install) or O(state=latest).

editable.description: Install the project in editable mode.

pip_args.description: Arbitrary arguments to pass directly to C(pip).

suffix.description: Optional suffix for virtual environment and executable names. B(Warning:) C(pipx) documentation states this is an B(experimental) feature subject to change.

spec_metadata.description: Spec metadata file for O(state=install_all). This content of the file is usually generated with C(pipx list --json), and it can be obtained with M(community.general.pipx_info) with O(community.general.pipx_info#module:include_raw=true) and obtaining the content from the RV(community.general.pipx_info#module:raw_output).

community.general.pipx_info.short_description: Rretrieves information about applications installed with pipx
community.general.pipx_info.description: Retrieve details about Python applications installed in isolated virtualenvs using pipx.

name.description: Name of an application installed with C(pipx).

include_deps.description: Include dependent packages in the output.

include_injected.description: Include injected packages in the output.

include_raw.description: Returns the raw output of C(pipx list --json). The raw output is not affected by O(include_deps) or O(include_injected).

community.general.pkg5.short_description: Manages packages with the Solaris 11 Image Packaging System
community.general.pkg5.description: IPS packages are the native packages in Solaris 11 and higher.

name.description: An FRMI of the package(s) to be installed/removed/updated. Multiple packages may be specified, separated by V(,).

state.description: Whether to install (V(present), V(latest)), or remove (V(absent)) a package.

be_name.description: Creates a new boot environment with the given name.

verbose.description: Set to V(true) to disable quiet execution.

community.general.pkg5_publisher.short_description: Manages Solaris 11 Image Packaging System publishers
community.general.pkg5_publisher.description: IPS packages are the native packages in Solaris 11 and higher. This module configures which publishers a client downloads IPS packages from.

name.description: The publisher's name.

state.description: Whether to ensure that a publisher is present or absent.

sticky.description: Packages installed from a sticky repository can only receive updates from that repository.

enabled.description: Is the repository enabled or disabled?

origin.description: A path or URL to the repository. Multiple values may be provided.

mirror.description: A path or URL to the repository mirror. Multiple values may be provided.

community.general.pkgin.short_description: Package manager for SmartOS, NetBSD, et al
community.general.pkgin.description: The standard package manager for SmartOS, but also usable on NetBSD or any OS that uses C(pkgsrc). (Home: U(http://pkgin.net/)).

name.description: Name of package to install/remove; Multiple names may be given, separated by commas.

state.description: Intended state of the package.

update_cache.description: Update repository database. Can be run with other steps or on its own.

upgrade.description: Upgrade main packages to their newer versions.

full_upgrade.description: Upgrade all packages to their newer versions.

force.description: Force package reinstall.

community.general.pkgng.description: Manage binary packages for FreeBSD using C(pkgng) which is available in versions after 9.0.

name.description: Name or list of names of packages to install/remove. With O(name=*), O(state=latest) operates, but O(state=present) and O(state=absent) are noops.

state.description: State of the package.

cached.description: Use local package base instead of fetching an updated one.

annotation.description: A list of keyvalue-pairs of the form C(<+/-/:><key>[=<value>]). A V(+) denotes adding an annotation, a V(-) denotes removing an annotation, and V(:) denotes modifying an annotation. If setting or modifying annotations, a value must be provided.

pkgsite.description: For C(pkgng) versions before 1.1.4, specify C(packagesite) to use for downloading packages. If not specified, use settings from C(/usr/local/etc/pkg.conf). For newer C(pkgng) versions, specify a the name of a repository configured in C(/usr/local/etc/pkg/repos).

rootdir.description: For C(pkgng) versions 1.5 and later, pkg installs all packages within the specified root directory. Can not be used together with O(chroot) or O(jail) options.

chroot.description: Pkg chroots in the specified environment. Can not be used together with O(rootdir) or O(jail) options.

jail.description: Pkg executes in the given jail name or ID. Can not be used together with O(chroot) or O(rootdir) options.

autoremove.description: Remove automatically installed packages which are no longer needed.

ignore_osver.description: Ignore FreeBSD OS version check, useful on C(-STABLE) and C(-CURRENT) branches. Defines the E(IGNORE_OSVERSION) environment variable.

use_globs.description: Treat the package names as shell glob patterns.

community.general.pkgutil.description: This module installs, updates and removes packages from the OpenCSW project for Solaris. Unlike the M(community.general.svr4pkg) module, it resolves and downloads dependencies. See U(https://www.opencsw.org/) for more information about the project.

name.description: The name of the package. When using O(state=latest), this can be V('*'), which updates all installed packages managed by pkgutil.

site.description: The repository path to install the package from. Its global definition is in C(/etc/opt/csw/pkgutil.conf).

state.description: Whether to install (V(present)/V(installed)), or remove (V(absent)/V(removed)) packages. The upgrade (V(latest)) operation updates/installs the packages to the latest version available.

update_catalog.description: If you always want to refresh your catalog from the mirror, even when it is not stale, set this to V(true).

force.description: To allow the update process to downgrade packages to match what is present in the repository, set this to V(true). This is useful for rolling back to stable from testing, or similar operations.

community.general.pmem.short_description: Configure Intel Optane Persistent Memory modules
community.general.pmem.description: This module allows Configuring Intel Optane Persistent Memory modules (PMem) using C(ipmctl) and C(ndctl) command line tools.

appdirect.description: Percentage of the total capacity to use in AppDirect Mode (V(0)-V(100)). Create AppDirect capacity utilizing hardware interleaving across the requested PMem modules if applicable given the specified target. Total of O(appdirect), O(memorymode) and O(reserved) must be V(100).

appdirect_interleaved.description: Create AppDirect capacity that is interleaved any other PMem modules.

memorymode.description: Percentage of the total capacity to use in Memory Mode (V(0)-V(100)).

reserved.description: Percentage of the capacity to reserve (V(0)-V(100)). O(reserved) is not mapped into the system physical address space and is presented as reserved capacity with Show Device and Show Memory Resources Commands. O(reserved) is set automatically if this is not configured.

socket.description: This enables to set the configuration for each socket by using the socket ID. Total of O(appdirect), O(memorymode) and O(reserved) must be V(100) within one socket.

namespace.description: This enables to set the configuration for the namespace of the PMem.

namespace_append.description: Enable to append the new namespaces to the system. The default is V(false) so the all existing namespaces not listed in O(namespace) are removed.

community.general.pnpm.description: Manage Node.js packages with the L(pnpm package manager, https://pnpm.io/).

name.description: The name of a Node.js library to install. All packages in C(package.json) are installed if not provided.

path.description: The base path to install the Node.js libraries.

version.description: The version of the library to be installed, in semver format.

executable.description: The executable location for pnpm. The default location it searches for is E(PATH), fails if not set.

ignore_scripts.description: Use the C(--ignore-scripts) flag when installing.

no_optional.description: Do not install optional packages, equivalent to C(--no-optional).

production.description: Install dependencies in production mode. Pnpm ignores any dependencies under C(devDependencies) in package.json.

dev.description: Install dependencies in development mode. Pnpm ignores any regular dependencies in C(package.json).

state.description: Installation state of the named Node.js library. If V(absent) is selected, a name option must be provided.

package.description: Package atom or set, for example V(sys-apps/foo) or V(>foo-2.13) or V(@world).

state.description: State of the package atom.

update.description: Update packages to the best version available (C(--update)).

deep.description: Consider the entire dependency tree of packages (C(--deep)).

newuse.description: Include installed packages where USE flags have changed (C(--newuse)).

changed_deps.description: Tells emerge to replace installed packages for which the ebuild dependencies have changed since the packages were built (C(--changed-deps)).

changed_use.description: Include installed packages where USE flags have changed, except when. Flags that the user has not enabled are added or removed. (C(--changed-use)).

oneshot.description: Do not add the packages to the world file (C(--oneshot)).

nodeps.description: Only merge packages but not their dependencies (C(--nodeps)).

onlydeps.description: Only merge packages' dependencies but not the packages (C(--onlydeps)).

depclean.description: Remove packages not needed by explicitly merged packages (C(--depclean)). If no package is specified, clean up the world's dependencies. Otherwise, C(--depclean) serves as a dependency aware version of C(--unmerge).

select.description: If set to V(true), explicitely add the package to the world file. Please note that this option is not used for idempotency, it is only used when actually installing a package.

sync.description: Sync package repositories first. If V(yes), perform C(emerge --sync). If V(web), perform C(emerge-webrsync).

getbinpkgonly.description: Merge only packages specified at C(PORTAGE_BINHOST) in C(make.conf).

getbinpkg.description: Prefer packages specified at C(PORTAGE_BINHOST) in C(make.conf).

usepkg.description: Tries to use the binary package(s) in the locally available packages directory.

keepgoing.description: Continue as much as possible after an error.

jobs.description: Specifies the number of packages to build simultaneously. Since version 2.6: Value of V(0) or V(false) resets any previously added C(--jobs) setting values.

loadavg.description: Specifies that no new builds should be started if there are other builds running and the load average is at least LOAD. Since version 2.6: Value of 0 or False resets any previously added C(--load-average) setting values.

withbdeps.description: Specifies that build time dependencies should be installed.

quietbuild.description: Redirect all build output to logs alone, and do not display it on stdout (C(--quiet-build)).

quietfail.description: Suppresses display of the build log on stdout (--quiet-fail). Only the die message and the path of the build log are displayed on stdout.

community.general.portinstall.short_description: Installing packages from FreeBSD's ports system
community.general.portinstall.description: Manage packages for FreeBSD using C(portinstall).

name.description: Name of package to install/remove.

state.description: State of the package.

use_packages.description: Use packages instead of ports whenever available.

community.general.pritunl_org.short_description: Manages Pritunl Organizations using the Pritunl API
community.general.pritunl_org.description: A module to manage Pritunl organizations using the Pritunl API.

name.description: The name of the organization to manage in Pritunl.

force.description: If O(force) is V(true) and O(state) is V(absent), the module deletes the organization, no matter if it contains users or not. By default O(force) is V(false), which causes the module to fail the deletion of the organization when it contains users.

state.description: If V(present), the module adds organization O(name) to Pritunl. If V(absent), attempt to delete the organization from Pritunl (please read about O(force) usage).

community.general.pritunl_org_info.short_description: List Pritunl Organizations using the Pritunl API
community.general.pritunl_org_info.description: A module to list Pritunl organizations using the Pritunl API.

organization.description: Name of the Pritunl organization to search for. If none provided, the module returns all Pritunl organizations.

community.general.pritunl_user.short_description: Manage Pritunl Users using the Pritunl API
community.general.pritunl_user.description: A module to manage Pritunl users using the Pritunl API.

organization.description: The name of the organization the user is part of.

state.description: If V(present), the module adds user O(user_name) to the Pritunl O(organization). If V(absent), removes the user O(user_name) from the Pritunl O(organization).

user_name.description: Name of the user to create or delete from Pritunl.

user_email.description: Email address associated with the user O(user_name).

user_groups.description: List of groups associated with the user O(user_name).

user_gravatar.description: Enable/Disable Gravatar usage for the user O(user_name).

user_mac_addresses.description: Allowed MAC addresses for the user O(user_name).

community.general.pritunl_user_info.short_description: List Pritunl Users using the Pritunl API
community.general.pritunl_user_info.description: A module to list Pritunl users using the Pritunl API.

organization.description: The name of the organization the user is part of.

user_name.description: Name of the user to filter on Pritunl.

community.general.pubnub_blocks.description: This module allows Ansible to interface with the PubNub BLOCKS infrastructure by providing the following operations: create / remove, start / stop and rename for blocks and create / modify / remove for event handlers.

email.description: Email from account for which new session should be started. Not required if O(cache) contains result of previous module call (in same play).

password.description: Password which match to account to which specified O(email) belong. Not required if O(cache) contains result of previous module call (in same play).

cache.description: In case if single play use blocks management module few times it is preferred to enabled 'caching' by making previous module to share gathered artifacts and pass them to this parameter.

account.description: Name of PubNub account for from which O(application) is used to manage blocks. User's account is used if value not set or empty.

application.description: Name of target PubNub application for which blocks configuration on specific O(keyset) is done.

keyset.description: Name of application's keys set which is bound to managed blocks.

state.description: Intended block state after event handlers creation / update process is completed.

name.description: Name of managed block which is later visible on admin.pubnub.com.

description.description: Short block description which is later visible on U(https://admin.pubnub.com). Used only if block does not exists and does not change description for existing block.

event_handlers.description: List of event handlers which should be updated for specified block O(name). Each entry for new event handler should contain: V(name), V(src), V(channels), V(event). V(name) used as event handler name which can be used later to make changes to it. C(src) is full path to file with event handler code. V(channels) is name of channel from which event handler is waiting for events. V(event) is type of event which is able to trigger event handler: V(js-before-publish), V(js-after-publish), V(js-after-presence). Each entry for existing handlers should contain C(name) (so target handler can be identified). Rest parameters (C(src), C(channels) and C(event)) can be added if changes required for them. It is possible to rename event handler by adding C(changes) key to event handler payload and pass dictionary, which contains single key C(name), where new name should be passed. To remove particular event handler it is possible to set C(state) for it to C(absent) and it is removed.

changes.description: List of fields which should be changed by block itself (does not affect any event handlers). Possible options for change is: O(name).

validate_certs.description: This key allow to try skip certificates check when performing REST API calls. Sometimes host may have issues with certificates on it and this causes problems to call PubNub REST API. If check should be ignored V(false) should be passed to this parameter.

community.general.pulp_repo.short_description: Add or remove Pulp repos from a remote host
community.general.pulp_repo.description: Add or remove Pulp repos from a remote host. Note, this is for Pulp 2 only.

add_export_distributor.description: Whether or not to add the export distributor to new C(rpm) repositories.

feed.description: Upstream feed URL to receive updates from.

force_basic_auth.description: C(httplib2), the library used by the M(ansible.builtin.uri) module only sends authentication information when a webservice responds to an initial request with a 401 status. Since some basic auth services do not properly send a 401, logins fail. This option forces the sending of the Basic authentication header upon initial request.

generate_sqlite.description: Boolean flag to indicate whether sqlite files should be generated during a repository publish.

feed_ca_cert.description: CA certificate string used to validate the feed source SSL certificate. This can be the file content or the path to the file.

feed_client_cert.description: Certificate used as the client certificate when synchronizing the repository. This is used to communicate authentication information to the feed source. The value to this option must be the full path to the certificate. The specified file may be the certificate itself or a single file containing both the certificate and private key. This can be the file content or the path to the file.

feed_client_key.description: Private key to the certificate specified in O(feed_client_cert), assuming it is not included in the certificate file itself. This can be the file content or the path to the file.

name.description: Name of the repo to add or remove. This correlates to repo-id in Pulp.

proxy_host.description: Proxy URL setting for the pulp repository importer. This is in the format V(scheme://host).

proxy_port.description: Proxy port setting for the pulp repository importer.

proxy_username.description: Proxy username for the pulp repository importer.

proxy_password.description: Proxy password for the pulp repository importer.

publish_distributor.description: Distributor to use when O(state=publish). The default is to publish all distributors.

pulp_host.description: URL of the pulp server to connect to.

relative_url.description: Relative URL for the local repository. It's required when state=present.

repo_type.description: Repo plugin type to use (that is, V(rpm), V(docker)).

repoview.description: Whether to generate repoview files for a published repository. Setting this to V(true) automatically activates O(generate_sqlite).

serve_http.description: Make the repo available over HTTP.

serve_https.description: Make the repo available over HTTPS.

state.description: The repo state. A state of V(sync) queues a sync of the repo. This is asynchronous but not delayed like a scheduled sync. A state of V(publish) uses the repository's distributor to publish the content.

url_password.description: The password for use in HTTP basic authentication to the pulp API. If the O(url_username) parameter is not specified, the O(url_password) parameter is not used.

url_username.description: The username for use in HTTP basic authentication to the pulp API.

validate_certs.description: If V(false), SSL certificates are not validated. This should only be used on personally controlled sites using self-signed certificates.

wait_for_completion.description: Wait for asynchronous tasks to complete before returning.

community.general.puppet.description: Runs C(puppet) agent or apply in a reliable manner.

timeout.description: How long to wait for C(puppet) to finish.

puppetmaster.description: The hostname of the puppetmaster to contact.

modulepath.description: Path to an alternate location for puppet modules.

manifest.description: Path to the manifest file to run puppet apply on.

noop.description: Override puppet.conf noop mode. When V(true), run Puppet agent with C(--noop) switch set. When V(false), run Puppet agent with C(--no-noop) switch set. When unset (default), use default or puppet.conf value if defined.

facts.description: A dict of values to pass in as persistent external facter facts.

facter_basename.description: Basename of the facter output file.

confdir.description: Path to the directory containing the puppet.conf file.

logdest.description: Where the puppet logs should go, if puppet apply is being used. V(all) goes to both C(console) and C(syslog). V(stdout) is deprecated and replaced by C(console).

certname.description: The name to use when handling certificates.

tags.description: A list of puppet tags to be used.

skip_tags.description: A list of puppet tags to be excluded.

execute.description: Execute a specific piece of Puppet code. It has no effect with a puppetmaster.

summarize.description: Whether to print a transaction summary.

waitforlock.description: The maximum amount of time C(puppet) should wait for an already running C(puppet) agent to finish before starting. If a number without unit is provided, it is assumed to be a number of seconds. Allowed units are V(m) for minutes and V(h) for hours.

show_diff.description: Whether to print file changes details.

environment_lang.description: The lang environment to use when running the puppet agent. The default value, V(C), is supported on every system, but can lead to encoding errors if UTF-8 is used in the output. Use V(C.UTF-8) or V(en_US.UTF-8) or similar UTF-8 supporting locales in case of problems. You need to make sure the selected locale is supported on the system the puppet agent runs on. Starting with community.general 9.1.0, you can use the value V(auto) and the module tries to determine the best parseable locale to use.

community.general.pushbullet.description: This module sends push notifications through Pushbullet to channels or devices.

api_key.description: Push bullet API token.

channel.description: The channel TAG you wish to broadcast a push notification, as seen on the "My Channels" > "Edit your channel" at Pushbullet page.

device.description: The device NAME you wish to send a push notification, as seen on the Pushbullet main page.

body.description: Body of the notification, for example details of the fault you are alerting.

url.description: URL field, used when O(push_type=link).

community.general.pushbullet.deprecated: Module relies on Python package pushbullet.py which is not maintained and supports only up to Python 3.2.

community.general.pushover.description: Send notifications through pushover to subscriber list of devices and email addresses. Requires pushover app on devices.

msg.description: What message you wish to send.

app_token.description: Pushover issued token identifying your pushover app.

user_key.description: Pushover issued authentication key for your user.

device.description: A device the message should be sent to. Multiple devices can be specified, separated by a comma.

community.general.python_requirements_in.short_description: Show python path and assert dependency versions
community.general.python_requirements_in.description: Get info about available Python requirements on the target host, including listing required libraries and gathering versions.

dependencies.description: A list of version-likes or module names to check for installation. Supported operators: C(<), C(>), C(<=), C(>=), or C(==). The bare module name like V(ansible), the module with a specific version like V(boto3==1.6.1), or a partial version like V(requests>2) are all valid specifications.

community.general.read_csv.description: Read a CSV file and return a list or a dictionary, containing one dictionary per row.

path.description: The CSV filename to read data from.

key.description: The column name used as a key for the resulting dictionary. If O(key) is unset, the module returns a list of dictionaries, where each dictionary is a row in the CSV file.

dialect.description: The CSV dialect to use when parsing the CSV file. Possible values include V(excel), V(excel-tab) or V(unix).

fieldnames.description: A list of field names for every column. This is needed if the CSV does not have a header.

unique.description: Whether the O(key) used is expected to be unique.

delimiter.description: A one-character string used to separate fields. When using this parameter, you change the default value used by O(dialect). The default value depends on the dialect used.

skipinitialspace.description: Whether to ignore any whitespaces immediately following the delimiter. When using this parameter, you change the default value used by O(dialect). The default value depends on the dialect used.

strict.description: Whether to raise an exception on bad CSV input. When using this parameter, you change the default value used by O(dialect). The default value depends on the dialect used.

community.general.redfish_command.short_description: Manages Out-Of-Band controllers using Redfish APIs
community.general.redfish_command.description: Builds Redfish URIs locally and sends them to remote OOB controllers to perform an action. Manages OOB controller ex. reboot, log management. Manages OOB controller users ex. add, remove, update. Manages system power ex. on, off, graceful and forced reboot.

category.description: Category to execute on OOB controller.

command.description: List of commands to execute on OOB controller.

username.description: Username for authenticating to OOB controller.

password.description: Password for authenticating to OOB controller.

auth_token.description: Security token for authenticating to OOB controller.

id.description: ID of account to delete/modify. Can also be used in account creation to work around vendor issues where the ID of the new user is required in the POST request.

new_password.description: New password of account to add/modify.

account_types.description: Array of account types to apply to a user account.

oem_account_types.description: Array of OEM account types to apply to a user account.

bootdevice.description: Boot device when setting boot configuration.

timeout.description: Timeout in seconds for HTTP requests to OOB controller. The default value for this parameter changed from V(10) to V(60) in community.general 9.0.0.

boot_override_mode.description: Boot mode when using an override.

uefi_target.description: UEFI boot target when bootdevice is "UefiTarget".

update_username.description: New user name for updating account_username.

account_properties.description: Properties of account service to update.

resource_id.description: ID of the System, Manager or Chassis to modify.

update_image_uri.description: URI of the image for the update.

update_image_file.description: Filename, with optional path, of the image for the update.

update_targets.description: List of target resource URIs to apply the update to.

update_creds.description: Credentials for retrieving the update image.

update_apply_time.description: Time when to apply the update.

update_oem_params.description: Properties for HTTP Multipart Push Updates.

update_handle.description: Handle to check the status of an update in progress.

update_custom_oem_header.description: Optional OEM header, sent as separate form-data for the Multipart HTTP push update. The header shall start with "Oem" according to DMTF Redfish spec 12.6.2.2. For more details, see U(https://www.dmtf.org/sites/default/files/standards/documents/DSP0266_1.21.0.html). If set, then O(update_custom_oem_params) is required too.

update_custom_oem_params.description: Custom OEM properties for HTTP Multipart Push updates. If set, then O(update_custom_oem_header) is required too.
