The properties are passed raw without any validation or conversion by Ansible. This means the content can be a file, a string, or any other data. If the content is a dictionary that should be converted to JSON, then the content must be converted to JSON before passing it to this module using the P(ansible.builtin.to_json#filter) filter.

update_custom_oem_mime_type.description: MIME Type for custom OEM properties for HTTP Multipart Push updates.

strip_etag_quotes.description: Removes surrounding quotes of etag used in C(If-Match) header of C(PATCH) requests. Only use this option to resolve bad vendor implementation where C(If-Match) only matches the unquoted etag string.

bios_attributes.description: BIOS attributes that needs to be verified in the given server.

reset_to_defaults_mode.description: Mode to apply when reseting to default.

wait.description: Block until the service is ready again.

wait_timeout.description: How long to block until the service is ready again before giving up.

community.general.redfish_config.short_description: Manages Out-Of-Band controllers using Redfish APIs
community.general.redfish_config.description: Builds Redfish URIs locally and sends them to remote OOB controllers to set or update a configuration attribute. Manages BIOS configuration settings. Manages OOB controller configuration settings.

category.description: Category to execute on OOB controller.

command.description: List of commands to execute on OOB controller.

username.description: Username for authenticating to OOB controller.

password.description: Password for authenticating to OOB controller.

auth_token.description: Security token for authenticating to OOB controller.

bios_attributes.description: Dictionary of BIOS attributes to update.

timeout.description: Timeout in seconds for HTTP requests to OOB controller. The default value for this parameter changed from V(10) to V(60) in community.general 9.0.0.

boot_order.description: List of BootOptionReference strings specifying the BootOrder.

network_protocols.description: Setting dict of manager services to update.

resource_id.description: ID of the System, Manager or Chassis to modify.

service_id.description: ID of the manager to update.

nic_addr.description: EthernetInterface Address string on OOB controller.

nic_config.description: Setting dict of EthernetInterface on OOB controller.

strip_etag_quotes.description: Removes surrounding quotes of etag used in C(If-Match) header of C(PATCH) requests. Only use this option to resolve bad vendor implementation where C(If-Match) only matches the unquoted etag string.

hostinterface_config.description: Setting dict of HostInterface on OOB controller.

hostinterface_id.description: Redfish HostInterface instance ID if multiple HostInterfaces are present.

storage_subsystem_id.description: ID of the Storage Subsystem on which the volume is to be created.

storage_none_volume_deletion.description: Indicates if all non-RAID volumes are automatically deleted prior to creating the new volume.

volume_ids.description: List of IDs of volumes to be deleted.

secure_boot_enable.description: Setting parameter to enable or disable SecureBoot.

volume_details.description: Setting dictionary of volume to be created. If C(CapacityBytes) key is not specified in this dictionary, the size of the volume is determined by the Redfish service. It is possible the size is not the maximum available size.

power_restore_policy.description: The desired power state of the system when power is restored after a power loss.

community.general.redfish_info.short_description: Manages Out-Of-Band controllers using Redfish APIs
community.general.redfish_info.description: Builds Redfish URIs locally and sends them to remote OOB controllers to get information back. Information retrieved is placed in a location specified by the user.

category.description: List of categories to execute on OOB controller.

command.description: List of commands to execute on OOB controller.

username.description: Username for authenticating to OOB controller.

password.description: Password for authenticating to OOB controller.

auth_token.description: Security token for authenticating to OOB controller.

manager.description: Name of manager on OOB controller to target.

timeout.description: Timeout in seconds for HTTP requests to OOB controller. The default value for this parameter changed from V(10) to V(60) in community.general 9.0.0.

update_handle.description: Handle to check the status of an update in progress.

community.general.redhat_subscription.short_description: Manage registration and subscriptions to RHSM using C(subscription-manager)
community.general.redhat_subscription.description: Manage registration and subscription to the Red Hat Subscription Management entitlement platform using the C(subscription-manager) command, registering using D-Bus if possible.

state.description: Whether to register and subscribe (V(present)), or unregister (V(absent)) a system.

username.description: Access.redhat.com or Red Hat Satellite or Katello username.

password.description: Access.redhat.com or Red Hat Satellite or Katello password.

token.description: Sso.redhat.com API access token.

server_hostname.description: Specify an alternative Red Hat Subscription Management or Red Hat Satellite or Katello server.

server_insecure.description: Enable or disable https server certificate verification when connecting to O(server_hostname).

server_prefix.description: Specify the prefix when registering to the Red Hat Subscription Management or Red Hat Satellite or Katello server.

server_port.description: Specify the port when registering to the Red Hat Subscription Management or Red Hat Satellite or Katello server.

rhsm_repo_ca_cert.description: Specify an alternative location for a CA certificate for CDN.

server_proxy_scheme.description: Specify an HTTP proxy scheme, for example V(http) or V(https).

server_proxy_user.description: Specify a user for HTTP proxy with basic authentication.

server_proxy_password.description: Specify a password for HTTP proxy with basic authentication.

auto_attach.description: Upon successful registration, auto-consume available subscriptions. Please note that the alias O(ignore:autosubscribe) was removed in community.general 9.0.0.

activationkey.description: Supply an activation key for use with registration.

org_id.description: Organization ID to use in conjunction with activationkey.

environment.description: Register with a specific environment in the destination org. Used with Red Hat Satellite or Katello.

pool_ids.description: Specify subscription pool IDs to consume. A pool ID may be specified as a C(string) - just the pool ID (for example V(0123456789abcdef0123456789abcdef)), or as a C(dict) with the pool ID as the key, and a quantity as the value (for example V(0123456789abcdef0123456789abcdef: 2). If the quantity is provided, it is used to consume multiple entitlements from a pool (the pool must support this).

consumer_type.description: The type of unit to register, defaults to system.

consumer_name.description: Name of the system to register, defaults to the hostname.

consumer_id.description: References an existing consumer ID to resume using a previous registration for this system. If the system's identity certificate is lost or corrupted, this option allows it to resume using its previous identity and subscriptions. The default is to not specify a consumer ID so a new ID is created.

force_register.description: Register the system even if it is already registered.

syspurpose.description: Set syspurpose attributes in file C(/etc/rhsm/syspurpose/syspurpose.json) and synchronize these attributes with RHSM server. Syspurpose attributes help attach the most appropriate subscriptions to the system automatically. When C(syspurpose.json) file already contains some attributes, then new attributes overwrite existing attributes. When some attribute is not listed in the new list of attributes, the existing attribute is removed from C(syspurpose.json) file. Unknown attributes are ignored.

community.general.redis.short_description: Various redis commands, replica and flush
community.general.redis.description: Unified utility to interact with redis instances.

command.description: The selected redis command. V(config) ensures a configuration setting on an instance. V(flush) flushes all the instance or a specified db. V(replica) sets a redis instance in replica or master mode. (V(slave) is an alias for V(replica)).

master_host.description: The host of the master instance [replica command].

master_port.description: The port of the master instance [replica command].

replica_mode.description: The mode of the redis instance [replica command]. V(slave) is an alias for V(replica).

db.description: The database to flush (used in DB mode) [flush command].

flush_mode.description: Type of flush (all the DBs in a redis instance or a specific one) [flush command].

name.description: A redis config key.

value.description: A redis config value. When memory size is needed, it is possible to specify it in the usual form of 1KB, 2M, 400MB where the base is 1024. Units are case insensitive, in other words 1m = 1mb = 1M = 1MB.

community.general.redis_data.short_description: Set key value pairs in Redis
community.general.redis_data.description: Set key value pairs in Redis database.

key.description: Database key.

value.description: Value that key should be set to.

expiration.description: Expiration time in milliseconds. Setting this option always results in a change in the database.

non_existing.description: Only set key if it does not already exist.

existing.description: Only set key if it already exists.

keep_ttl.description: Retain the time to live associated with the key.

state.description: State of the key.

community.general.redis_data_incr.description: Increment integers or float keys in Redis database and get new value. Default increment for all keys is V(1). For specific increments use the O(increment_int) and O(increment_float) options.

key.description: Database key.

increment_int.description: Integer amount to increment the key by.

increment_float.description: Float amount to increment the key by. This only works with keys that contain float values in their string representation.

community.general.redis_data_info.short_description: Get value of key in Redis database
community.general.redis_data_info.description: Get value of keys in Redis database.

key.description: Database key.

community.general.redis_info.description: Gathers information and statistics about Redis servers.

cluster.description: Get informations about cluster status as RV(cluster).

community.general.rhevm.description: This module only supports oVirt/RHEV version 3. A newer module M(ovirt.ovirt.ovirt_vm) supports oVirt/RHV version 4. Allows you to create/remove/update or powermanage virtual machines on a RHEV/oVirt platform.

user.description: The user to authenticate with.

password.description: The password for user authentication.

server.description: The name/IP of your RHEV-m/oVirt instance.

port.description: The port on which the API is reachable.

insecure_api.description: A boolean switch to make a secure or insecure connection to the server.

name.description: The name of the VM.

cluster.description: The RHEV/oVirt cluster in which you want you VM to start.

datacenter.description: The RHEV/oVirt datacenter in which you want you VM to start.

state.description: This serves to create/remove/update or powermanage your VM.

image.description: The template to use for the VM.

type.description: To define if the VM is a server or desktop.

vmhost.description: The host you wish your VM to run on.

vmcpu.description: The number of CPUs you want in your VM.

cpu_share.description: This parameter is used to configure the CPU share.

vmmem.description: The amount of memory you want your VM to use (in GB).

osver.description: The operating system option in RHEV/oVirt.

mempol.description: The minimum amount of memory you wish to reserve for this system.

vm_ha.description: To make your VM High Available.

disks.description: This option uses complex arguments and is a list of disks with the options V(name), V(size), and V(domain).

ifaces.description: This option uses complex arguments and is a list of interfaces with the options V(name) and V(vlan).

boot_order.description: This option uses complex arguments and is a list of items that specify the bootorder.

del_prot.description: This option sets the delete protection checkbox.

cd_drive.description: The CD you wish to have mounted on the VM when O(state=cd).

timeout.description: The timeout you wish to define for power actions. When O(state=up). When O(state=down). When O(state=restarted).

community.general.rhsm_release.short_description: Set or Unset RHSM Release version
community.general.rhsm_release.description: Sets or unsets the release version used by RHSM repositories.

release.description: RHSM release version to use. To unset either pass V(null) for this option, or omit this option.

community.general.rhsm_repository.short_description: Manage RHSM repositories using the subscription-manager command
community.general.rhsm_repository.description: Manage (Enable/Disable) RHSM repositories to the Red Hat Subscription Management entitlement platform using the C(subscription-manager) command.

state.description: If state is equal to present or disabled, indicates the desired repository state. In community.general 10.0.0 the states V(present) and V(absent) have been removed. Please use V(enabled) and V(disabled) instead.

name.description: The ID of repositories to enable. To operate on several repositories this can accept a comma separated list or a YAML list.

purge.description: Disable all currently enabled repositories that are not not specified in O(name). Only set this to V(true) if passing in a list of repositories to the O(name) field. Using this with C(loop) is likely not to have the desired result.

community.general.riak.short_description: This module handles some common Riak operations
community.general.riak.description: This module can be used to join nodes to a cluster, check the status of the cluster.

command.description: The command you would like to perform against the cluster.

config_dir.description: The path to the riak configuration directory.

http_conn.description: The IP address and port that is listening for Riak HTTP queries.

target_node.description: The target node for certain operations (join, ping).

wait_for_handoffs.description: Number of seconds to wait for handoffs to complete.

wait_for_ring.description: Number of seconds to wait for all nodes to agree on the ring.

wait_for_service.description: Waits for a riak service to come online before continuing.

validate_certs.description: If V(false), SSL certificates are not validated. This should only be used on personally controlled sites using self-signed certificates.

community.general.rocketchat.description: This module sends notifications to Rocket Chat through the Incoming WebHook integration.

domain.description: The domain for your environment without protocol. (For example V(example.com) or V(chat.example.com)).

token.description: Rocket Chat Incoming Webhook integration token. This provides authentication to Rocket Chat's Incoming webhook for posting messages.

protocol.description: Specify the protocol used to send notification messages before the webhook URL (that is, V(http) or V(https)).

channel.description: Channel to send the message to. If absent, the message goes to the channel selected for the O(token) specified during the creation of webhook.

username.description: This is the sender of the message.

icon_url.description: URL for the message sender's icon.

icon_emoji.description: Emoji for the message sender. The representation for the available emojis can be got from Rocket Chat. For example V(:thumbsup:). If O(icon_emoji) is set, O(icon_url) is not used.

link_names.description: Automatically create links for channels and usernames in O(msg).

validate_certs.description: If V(false), SSL certificates are not validated. This should only be used on personally controlled sites using self-signed certificates.

color.description: Allow text to use default colors - use the default of V(normal) to not send a custom color bar at the start of the message.

is_pre740.description: If V(true), the payload matches Rocket.Chat prior to 7.4.0 format. This format has been used by the module since its inception, but is no longer supported by Rocket.Chat 7.4.0. The default value of the option, V(true), is B(deprecated) since community.general 11.2.0 and will change to V(false) in community.general 13.0.0. This parameter is going to be removed in a future release when Rocket.Chat 7.4.0 becomes the minimum supported version.

token.description: Your project access token.

environment.description: Name of the environment being deployed, for example V(production).

user.description: User who deployed.

rollbar_user.description: Rollbar username of the user who deployed.

comment.description: Deploy comment (for example what is being deployed).

url.description: Optional URL to submit the notification to.

validate_certs.description: If V(false), SSL certificates for the target URL are not validated. This should only be used on personally controlled sites using self-signed certificates.

community.general.rpm_ostree_pkg.short_description: Install or uninstall overlay additional packages
community.general.rpm_ostree_pkg.description: Install or uninstall overlay additional packages using C(rpm-ostree) command.

name.description: Name of overlay package to install or remove.

state.description: State of the overlay package. V(present) simply ensures that a desired package is installed. V(absent) removes the specified package.

apply_live.description: Adds the options C(--apply-live) when O(state=present). Option is ignored when O(state=absent). For more information, please see U(https://coreos.github.io/rpm-ostree/apply-live/).

community.general.rundeck_acl_policy.description: Create, update and remove Rundeck ACL policies through HTTP API.

state.description: Create or remove Rundeck project.

name.description: Sets the project name.

api_token.description: Sets the token to authenticate against Rundeck API.

project.description: Sets the project which receive the ACL policy. If unset, it is a system ACL policy.

policy.description: Sets the ACL policy content. ACL policy content is a YAML object as described in U(http://rundeck.org/docs/man5/aclpolicy.html). It can be a YAML string or a pure Ansible inventory YAML object.

community.general.rundeck_job_executions.short_description: Query executions for a Rundeck job
community.general.rundeck_job_executions.description: This module gets the list of executions for a specified Rundeck job.

offset.description: The start point to return the results.

community.general.rundeck_job_run.description: This module runs a Rundeck job specified by ID.

job_options.description: The job options for the steps. Numeric values must be quoted.

filter_nodes.description: Filter the nodes where the jobs must run. See U(https://docs.rundeck.com/docs/manual/11-node-filters.html#node-filter-syntax).

run_at_time.description: Schedule the job execution to run at specific date and time. ISO-8601 date and time format like V(2021-10-05T15:45:00-03:00).

wait_execution.description: Wait until the job finished the execution.

wait_execution_delay.description: Delay, in seconds, between job execution status check requests.

wait_execution_timeout.description: Job execution wait timeout in seconds. If the timeout is reached, the job is aborted. Keep in mind that there is a sleep based on O(wait_execution_delay) after each job status check.

abort_on_timeout.description: Send a job abort request if exceeded the O(wait_execution_timeout) specified.

community.general.rundeck_project.description: Create and remove Rundeck projects through HTTP API.

state.description: Create or remove Rundeck project.

name.description: Sets the project name.

api_token.description: Sets the token to authenticate against Rundeck API.

community.general.runit.description: Controls runit services on remote hosts using the sv utility.

name.description: Name of the service to manage.

state.description: V(started)/V(stopped) are idempotent actions that do not run commands unless necessary. V(restarted) always bounces the service (sv restart) and V(killed) always bounces the service (sv force-stop). V(reloaded) always sends a HUP (sv reload). V(once) runs a normally downed sv once (sv once), not really an idempotent operation.

enabled.description: Whether the service is enabled or not, if disabled it also implies stopped.

service_src.description: Directory where services are defined, the source of symlinks to O(service_dir).

community.general.say.description: Makes a computer speak! Amuse your friends, annoy your coworkers!

community.general.scaleway_compute.description: This module manages compute instances on Scaleway.

public_ip.description: Manage public IP on a Scaleway server. Could be Scaleway IP address UUID. V(dynamic) Means that IP is destroyed at the same time the host is destroyed. V(absent) Means no public IP at all.

enable_ipv6.description: Enable public IPv6 connectivity on the instance.

image.description: Image identifier used to start the instance with.

name.description: Name of the instance.

organization.description: Organization identifier. Exactly one of O(project) and O(organization) must be specified.

project.description: Project identifier. Exactly one of O(project) and O(organization) must be specified.

state.description: Indicate desired state of the instance.

tags.description: List of tags to apply to the instance (5 max).

region.description: Scaleway compute zone.

commercial_type.description: Commercial name of the compute node.

wait.description: Wait for the instance to reach its desired state before returning.

wait_timeout.description: Time to wait for the server to reach the expected state.

wait_sleep_time.description: Time to wait before every attempt to check the state of the server.

security_group.description: Security group unique identifier. If no value provided, the default security group or current security group is used.

community.general.scaleway_compute_priva.description: This module add or remove a private network to a compute instance (U(https://developer.scaleway.com)).

state.description: Indicate desired state of the VPC.

project.description: Project identifier.

region.description: Scaleway region to use (for example V(par1)).

compute_id.description: ID of the compute instance (see M(community.general.scaleway_compute)).

community.general.scaleway_container.description: This module manages container on Scaleway account.

state.description: Indicate desired state of the container.

region.description: Scaleway region to use (for example V(fr-par)).

name.description: Name of the container namespace.

min_scale.description: Minimum number of replicas for the container.

max_scale.description: Maximum number of replicas for the container.

environment_variables.description: Environment variables of the container namespace. Injected in container at runtime.

secret_environment_variables.description: Secret environment variables of the container namespace. Updating those values does not output a RV(ignore:changed) state in Ansible. Injected in container at runtime.

cpu_limit.description: Resources define performance characteristics of your container. They are allocated to your container at runtime. Unit is 1/1000 of a VCPU.

memory_limit.description: Resources define performance characteristics of your container. They are allocated to your container at runtime. Unit is MB of memory.

container_timeout.description: The length of time your handler can spend processing a request before being stopped.

privacy.description: Privacy policies define whether a container can be executed anonymously. Choose V(public) to enable anonymous execution, or V(private) to protect your container with an authentication mechanism provided by the Scaleway API.

registry_image.description: The name of image used for the container.

max_concurrency.description: Maximum number of connections per container. This parameter is used to trigger autoscaling.

port.description: Listen port used to expose the container.

redeploy.description: Redeploy the container if update is required.

community.general.scaleway_container_inf.description: This module return information about a container on Scaleway account.

region.description: Scaleway region to use (for example C(fr-par)).

name.description: Name of the container.

community.general.scaleway_container_nam.description: This module manages container namespaces on Scaleway account.

state.description: Indicate desired state of the container namespace.

region.description: Scaleway region to use (for example V(fr-par)).

name.description: Name of the container namespace.

environment_variables.description: Environment variables of the container namespace. Injected in containers at runtime.

secret_environment_variables.description: Secret environment variables of the container namespace. Updating those values does not output a RV(ignore:changed) state in Ansible. Injected in containers at runtime.

community.general.scaleway_container_nam.short_description: Retrieve information on Scaleway Container namespace
community.general.scaleway_container_nam.description: This module return information about a container namespace on Scaleway account.

region.description: Scaleway region to use (for example C(fr-par)).

name.description: Name of the container namespace.

community.general.scaleway_container_reg.description: This module manages container registries on Scaleway account.

state.description: Indicate desired state of the container registry.

region.description: Scaleway region to use (for example V(fr-par)).

name.description: Name of the container registry.

privacy_policy.description: Default visibility policy. Everyone can pull images from a V(public) registry.

community.general.scaleway_container_reg.description: This module return information about a container registry on Scaleway account.

region.description: Scaleway region to use (for example C(fr-par)).

name.description: Name of the container registry.

community.general.scaleway_database_back.description: This module manages database backups on Scaleway account U(https://developer.scaleway.com).

state.description: Indicate desired state of the database backup. V(present) creates a backup. V(absent) deletes the backup. V(exported) creates a download link for the backup. V(restored) restores the backup to a new database.

region.description: Scaleway region to use (for example V(fr-par)).

id.description: UUID used to identify the database backup. Required for V(absent), V(exported) and V(restored) states.

name.description: Name used to identify the database backup. Required for V(present) state. Ignored when O(state=absent), O(state=exported) or O(state=restored).

database_name.description: Name used to identify the database. Required for V(present) and V(restored) states. Ignored when O(state=absent) or O(state=exported).

instance_id.description: UUID of the instance associated to the database backup. Required for V(present) and V(restored) states. Ignored when O(state=absent) or O(state=exported).

expires_at.description: Expiration datetime of the database backup (ISO 8601 format). Ignored when O(state=absent), O(state=exported) or O(state=restored).

wait.description: Wait for the instance to reach its desired state before returning.

wait_timeout.description: Time to wait for the backup to reach the expected state.

wait_sleep_time.description: Time to wait before every attempt to check the state of the backup.

community.general.scaleway_function.description: This module manages function on Scaleway account.

state.description: Indicate desired state of the function.

region.description: Scaleway region to use (for example V(fr-par)).

name.description: Name of the function.

min_scale.description: Minimum number of replicas for the function.

max_scale.description: Maximum number of replicas for the function.

environment_variables.description: Environment variables of the function. Injected in function at runtime.

secret_environment_variables.description: Secret environment variables of the function. Updating those values does not output a RV(ignore:changed) state in Ansible. Injected in function at runtime.

runtime.description: Runtime of the function. See U(https://www.scaleway.com/en/docs/compute/functions/reference-content/functions-lifecycle/) for all available runtimes.

memory_limit.description: Resources define performance characteristics of your function. They are allocated to your function at runtime.

function_timeout.description: The length of time your handler can spend processing a request before being stopped.

handler.description: The C(module-name.export) value in your function.

privacy.description: Privacy policies define whether a function can be executed anonymously. Choose V(public) to enable anonymous execution, or V(private) to protect your function with an authentication mechanism provided by the Scaleway API.

redeploy.description: Redeploy the function if update is required.

community.general.scaleway_function_info.description: This module return information about a function on Scaleway account.

region.description: Scaleway region to use (for example C(fr-par)).

name.description: Name of the function.

community.general.scaleway_function_name.description: This module manages function namespaces on Scaleway account.

state.description: Indicate desired state of the function namespace.

region.description: Scaleway region to use (for example V(fr-par)).

name.description: Name of the function namespace.

environment_variables.description: Environment variables of the function namespace. Injected in functions at runtime.

secret_environment_variables.description: Secret environment variables of the function namespace. Updating those values does not output a RV(ignore:changed) state in Ansible. Injected in functions at runtime.

community.general.scaleway_function_name.short_description: Retrieve information on Scaleway Function namespace
community.general.scaleway_function_name.description: This module return information about a function namespace on Scaleway account.

region.description: Scaleway region to use (for example C(fr-par)).

name.description: Name of the function namespace.

community.general.scaleway_image_info.short_description: Gather information about the Scaleway images available
community.general.scaleway_image_info.description: Gather information about the Scaleway images available.

region.description: Scaleway compute zone.

community.general.scaleway_ip.description: This module manages IP on Scaleway account U(https://developer.scaleway.com).

state.description: Indicate desired state of the IP.

organization.description: Scaleway organization identifier. Exactly one of O(project) and O(organization) must be specified.

project.description: Project identifier. Exactly one of O(project) and O(organization) must be specified.

region.description: Scaleway region to use (for example par1).

id.description: ID of the Scaleway IP (UUID).

server.description: ID of the server you want to attach an IP to. To unattach an IP do not specify this option.

reverse.description: Reverse to assign to the IP.

community.general.scaleway_ip_info.short_description: Gather information about the Scaleway IPs available
community.general.scaleway_ip_info.description: Gather information about the Scaleway IPs available.

region.description: Scaleway region to use (for example C(par1)).

community.general.scaleway_lb.description: This module manages load-balancers on Scaleway.

name.description: Name of the load-balancer.

state.description: Indicate desired state of the instance.

region.description: Scaleway zone.

tags.description: List of tags to apply to the load-balancer.

wait.description: Wait for the load-balancer to reach its desired state before returning.

wait_timeout.description: Time to wait for the load-balancer to reach the expected state.

wait_sleep_time.description: Time to wait before every attempt to check the state of the load-balancer.

community.general.scaleway_organization_.short_description: Gather information about the Scaleway organizations available
community.general.scaleway_organization_.description: Gather information about the Scaleway organizations available.

community.general.scaleway_private_netwo.description: This module manages private network on Scaleway account (U(https://developer.scaleway.com)).

state.description: Indicate desired state of the VPC.

project.description: Project identifier.

region.description: Scaleway region to use (for example V(par1)).

name.description: Name of the VPC.

tags.description: List of tags to apply to the instance.

community.general.scaleway_security_grou.description: This module manages Security Group on Scaleway account U(https://developer.scaleway.com).

state.description: Indicate desired state of the Security Group.

organization.description: Organization identifier. Exactly one of O(project) and O(organization) must be specified.

region.description: Scaleway region to use (for example V(par1)).

name.description: Name of the Security Group.

stateful.description: Create a stateful security group which allows established connections in and out.

organization_default.description: Create security group to be the default one.

project.description: Project identifier. Exactly one of O(project) and O(organization) must be specified.

community.general.scaleway_security_grou.short_description: Gather information about the Scaleway security groups available
community.general.scaleway_security_grou.description: Gather information about the Scaleway security groups available.

region.description: Scaleway region to use (for example C(par1)).

community.general.scaleway_security_grou.short_description: Scaleway Security Group Rule management module
community.general.scaleway_security_grou.description: This module manages Security Group Rule on Scaleway account U(https://developer.scaleway.com).

state.description: Indicate desired state of the Security Group Rule.

region.description: Scaleway region to use (for example V(par1)).

port.description: Port related to the rule, null value for all the ports.

ip_range.description: IPV4 CIDR notation to apply to the rule.

community.general.scaleway_server_info.short_description: Gather information about the Scaleway servers available
community.general.scaleway_server_info.description: Gather information about the Scaleway servers available.

region.description: Scaleway region to use (for example C(par1)).

community.general.scaleway_snapshot_info.short_description: Gather information about the Scaleway snapshots available
community.general.scaleway_snapshot_info.description: Gather information about the Scaleway snapshot available.

region.description: Scaleway region to use (for example C(par1)).

community.general.scaleway_sshkey.description: This module manages SSH keys on Scaleway account (U(https://developer.scaleway.com)).

state.description: Indicate desired state of the SSH key.

ssh_pub_key.description: The public SSH key as a string to add.

community.general.scaleway_user_data.description: This module manages user_data on compute instances on Scaleway. It can be used to configure cloud-init for instance.

server_id.description: Scaleway Compute instance ID of the server.

user_data.description: User defined data. Typically used with C(cloud-init). Pass your C(cloud-init) script here as a string.

region.description: Scaleway compute zone.

community.general.scaleway_volume.description: This module manages volumes on Scaleway account U(https://developer.scaleway.com).

state.description: Indicate desired state of the volume.

region.description: Scaleway region to use (for example par1).

name.description: Name used to identify the volume.

project.description: Scaleway project ID to which volume belongs.

organization.description: ScaleWay organization ID to which volume belongs.

size.description: Size of the volume in bytes.

community.general.scaleway_volume_info.short_description: Gather information about the Scaleway volumes available
community.general.scaleway_volume_info.description: Gather information about the Scaleway volumes available.

region.description: Scaleway region to use (for example C(par1)).

community.general.sefcontext.short_description: Manages SELinux file context mapping definitions
community.general.sefcontext.description: Manages SELinux file context mapping definitions. Similar to the C(semanage fcontext) command.

ftype.description: The file type that should have SELinux contexts applied. The following file type options are available: V(a) for all files, V(b) for block devices, V(c) for character devices, V(d) for directories, V(f) for regular files, V(l) for symbolic links, V(p) for named pipes, V(s) for socket files.

setype.description: SELinux type for the specified O(target).

substitute.description: Path to use to substitute file context(s) for the specified O(target). The context labeling for the O(target) subtree is made equivalent to this path. This is also referred to as SELinux file context equivalence and it implements the C(equal) functionality of the SELinux management tools.

seuser.description: SELinux user for the specified O(target). Defaults to V(system_u) for new file contexts and to existing value when modifying file contexts.

selevel.description: SELinux range for the specified O(target). Defaults to V(s0) for new file contexts and to existing value when modifying file contexts.

state.description: Whether the SELinux file context must be V(absent) or V(present). Specifying V(absent) without either O(setype) or O(substitute) deletes both SELinux type or path substitution mappings that match O(target).

reload.description: Reload SELinux policy after commit. Note that this does not apply SELinux file contexts to existing files.

ignore_selinux_state.description: Useful for scenarios (chrooted environment) that you cannot get the real SELinux state.

community.general.selinux_permissive.short_description: Change permissive domain in SELinux policy
community.general.selinux_permissive.description: Add and remove a domain from the list of permissive domains.

domain.description: The domain that is added or removed from the list of permissive domains.

permissive.description: Indicate if the domain should or should not be set as permissive.

no_reload.description: Disable reloading of the SELinux policy after making change to a domain's permissive setting. The default is V(false), which causes policy to be reloaded when a domain changes state. Reloading the policy does not work on older versions of the C(policycoreutils-python) library, for example in EL 6.".

store.description: Name of the SELinux policy store to use.

community.general.selogin.short_description: Manages linux user to SELinux user mapping
community.general.selogin.description: Manages linux user to SELinux user mapping.

selevel.description: MLS/MCS Security Range (MLS/MCS Systems only) SELinux Range for SELinux login mapping defaults to the SELinux user record range.

state.description: Desired mapping value.

ignore_selinux_state.description: Run independent of selinux runtime state.

community.general.sendgrid.short_description: Sends an email with the SendGrid API
community.general.sendgrid.description: Sends an email with a SendGrid account through their API, not through the SMTP service.

username.description: Username for logging into the SendGrid account. It is only required if O(api_key) is not supplied.

password.description: Password that corresponds to the username. It is only required if O(api_key) is not supplied.

from_address.description: The address in the "from" field for the email.

to_addresses.description: A list with one or more recipient email addresses.

subject.description: The desired subject for the email.

api_key.description: Sendgrid API key to use instead of username/password.

cc.description: A list of email addresses to cc.

bcc.description: A list of email addresses to bcc.

attachments.description: A list of relative or explicit paths of files you want to attach (7MB limit as per SendGrid docs).

from_name.description: The name you want to appear in the from field, for example V(John Doe).

html_body.description: Whether the body is HTML content that should be rendered.

headers.description: A dict to pass on as headers.

community.general.sensu_check.description: Manage the checks that should be run on a machine by I(Sensu). Most options do not have a default and are not added to the check definition unless specified. All defaults except O(path), O(state), O(backup) and O(metric) are not managed by this module, they are simply specified for your convenience.

name.description: The name of the check. This is the key that is used to determine whether a check exists.

state.description: Whether the check should be present or not.

path.description: Path to the JSON file of the check to be added/removed. It is created if it does not exist (unless O(state=absent)). The parent folders need to exist when O(state=present), otherwise an error is thrown.

backup.description: Create a backup file (if yes), including the timestamp information so you can get the original file back if you somehow clobbered it incorrectly.

command.description: Path to the sensu check to run (not required when O(state=absent)).

handlers.description: List of handlers to notify when the check fails.

subscribers.description: List of subscribers/channels this check should run for. See sensu_subscribers to subscribe a machine to a channel.

timeout.description: Timeout for the check. If not specified, it defaults to 10.

ttl.description: Time to live in seconds until the check is considered stale.

handle.description: Whether the check should be handled or not. Default is V(false).

subdue_begin.description: When to disable handling of check failures.

subdue_end.description: When to enable handling of check failures.

dependencies.description: Other checks this one depends on. If dependencies fail handling of this check is disabled.

metric.description: Whether the check is a metric.

standalone.description: Whether the check should be scheduled by the sensu client or server. This option obviates the need for specifying the O(subscribers) option. Default is V(false).

publish.description: Whether the check should be scheduled at all. You can still issue it using the sensu API. Default is V(false).

occurrences.description: Number of event occurrences before the handler should take action. If not specified, defaults to 1.

refresh.description: Number of seconds handlers should wait before taking second action.

aggregate.description: Classifies the check as an aggregate check, making it available using the aggregate API. Default is V(false).

low_flap_threshold.description: The low threshold for flap detection.

high_flap_threshold.description: The high threshold for flap detection.

custom.description: A hash/dictionary of custom parameters for mixing to the configuration. You cannot rewrite other module parameters using this.

source.description: The check source, used to create a JIT Sensu client for an external resource (for example a network switch).

community.general.sensu_check.deprecated: Sensu Core and Sensu Enterprise products have been End of Life since 2019/20.

community.general.sensu_client.description: Manages Sensu client configuration. For more information, refer to the L(Sensu documentation, https://sensuapp.org/docs/latest/reference/clients.html).

state.description: Whether the client should be present or not.

name.description: A unique name for the client. The name cannot contain special characters or spaces. If not specified, it defaults to the system hostname as determined by Ruby Socket.gethostname (provided by Sensu).

address.description: An address to help identify and reach the client. This is only informational, usually an IP address or hostname. If not specified it defaults to non-loopback IPv4 address as determined by Ruby C(Socket.ip_address_list) (provided by Sensu).

subscriptions.description: An array of client subscriptions, a list of roles and/or responsibilities assigned to the system (for example V(webserver)). These subscriptions determine which monitoring checks are executed by the client, as check requests are sent to subscriptions. The subscriptions array items must be strings.

safe_mode.description: If safe mode is enabled for the client. Safe mode requires local check definitions in order to accept a check request and execute the check.

redact.description: Client definition attributes to redact (values) when logging and sending client keepalives.

socket.description: The socket definition scope, used to configure the Sensu client socket.

keepalives.description: If Sensu should monitor keepalives for this client.

keepalive.description: The keepalive definition scope, used to configure Sensu client keepalives behavior (for example keepalive thresholds and so).

registration.description: The registration definition scope, used to configure Sensu registration event handlers.

deregister.description: If a deregistration event should be created upon Sensu client process stop. Default is V(false).

deregistration.description: The deregistration definition scope, used to configure automated Sensu client de-registration.

ec2.description: The ec2 definition scope, used to configure the Sensu Enterprise AWS EC2 integration (Sensu Enterprise users only).

chef.description: The chef definition scope, used to configure the Sensu Enterprise Chef integration (Sensu Enterprise users only).

puppet.description: The puppet definition scope, used to configure the Sensu Enterprise Puppet integration (Sensu Enterprise users only).

servicenow.description: The servicenow definition scope, used to configure the Sensu Enterprise ServiceNow integration (Sensu Enterprise users only).

community.general.sensu_client.deprecated: Sensu Core and Sensu Enterprise products have been End of Life since 2019/20.

community.general.sensu_handler.description: Manages Sensu handler configuration. For more information, refer to the L(Sensu documentation, https://sensuapp.org/docs/latest/reference/handlers.html).

state.description: Whether the handler should be present or not.

name.description: A unique name for the handler. The name cannot contain special characters or spaces.

type.description: The handler type.

filter.description: The Sensu event filter (name) to use when filtering events for the handler.

filters.description: An array of Sensu event filters (names) to use when filtering events for the handler. Each array item must be a string.

severities.description: An array of check result severities the handler handles. NOTE: event resolution bypasses this filtering. Example: [ 'warning', 'critical', 'unknown' ].

mutator.description: The Sensu event mutator (name) to use to mutate event data for the handler.

timeout.description: The handler execution duration timeout in seconds (hard stop). Only used by pipe and tcp handler types.

handle_silenced.description: If events matching one or more silence entries should be handled.

handle_flapping.description: If events in the flapping state should be handled.

command.description: The handler command to be executed. The event data is passed to the process using STDIN. NOTE: the O(command) attribute is only required for Pipe handlers (that is, handlers configured with O(type=pipe)).

socket.description: The socket definition scope, used to configure the TCP/UDP handler socket. NOTE: the O(socket) attribute is only required for TCP/UDP handlers (that is, handlers configured with O(type=tcp) or O(type=udp)).

pipe.description: The pipe definition scope, used to configure the Sensu transport pipe. NOTE: the O(pipe) attribute is only required for Transport handlers (that is, handlers configured with O(type=transport)).

handlers.description: An array of Sensu event handlers (names) to use for events using the handler set. NOTE: the O(handlers) attribute is only required for handler sets (that is, handlers configured with O(type=set)).

community.general.sensu_handler.deprecated: Sensu Core and Sensu Enterprise products have been End of Life since 2019/20.

community.general.sensu_silence.description: Create and clear (delete) a silence entries using the Sensu API for subscriptions and checks.

check.description: Specifies the check which the silence entry applies to.

creator.description: Specifies the entity responsible for this entry.

expire.description: If specified, the silence entry is automatically cleared after this number of seconds.

expire_on_resolve.description: If specified as true, the silence entry is automatically cleared once the condition it is silencing is resolved.

reason.description: If specified, this free-form string is used to provide context or rationale for the reason this silence entry was created.

state.description: Specifies to create or clear (delete) a silence entry using the Sensu API.

subscription.description: Specifies the subscription which the silence entry applies to. To create a silence entry for a client prepend C(client:) to client name. Example - C(client:server1.example.dev).

url.description: Specifies the URL of the Sensu monitoring host server.

community.general.sensu_silence.deprecated: Sensu Core and Sensu Enterprise products have been End of Life since 2019/20.

community.general.sensu_subscription.description: Manage which I(sensu channels) a machine should subscribe to.

name.description: The name of the channel.

state.description: Whether the machine should subscribe or unsubscribe from the channel.

path.description: Path to the subscriptions JSON file.

backup.description: Create a backup file (if yes), including the timestamp information so you can get the original file back if you somehow clobbered it incorrectly.

community.general.sensu_subscription.deprecated: Sensu Core and Sensu Enterprise products have been End of Life since 2019/20.

community.general.seport.short_description: Manages SELinux network port type definitions
community.general.seport.description: Manages SELinux network port type definitions.

ports.description: Ports or port ranges. Can be a list (since 2.6) or comma separated string.

proto.description: Protocol for the specified port. Support for V(dccp) and V(sctp) has been added in community.general 12.4.0.

setype.description: SELinux type for the specified port.

state.description: Desired boolean value.

ignore_selinux_state.description: Run independent of selinux runtime state.

community.general.serverless.description: Provides support for managing Serverless Framework (U(https://serverless.com/)) project deployments and stacks.

state.description: Goal state of given stage/project.

serverless_bin_path.description: The path of a serverless framework binary relative to the O(service_path), for example V(node_module/.bin/serverless).

service_path.description: The path to the root of the Serverless Service to be operated on.

stage.description: The name of the serverless framework project stage to deploy to. This uses the serverless framework default "dev".

region.description: AWS region to deploy the service to. This parameter defaults to V(us-east-1).

deploy.description: Whether or not to deploy artifacts after building them. When this option is V(false) all the functions are built, but no stack update is run to send them out. This is mostly useful for generating artifacts to be stored/deployed elsewhere.

force.description: Whether or not to force full deployment, equivalent to serverless C(--force) option.

verbose.description: Shows all stack events during deployment, and display any Stack Output.

delay.description: Seconds to wait before shutdown. Passed as a parameter to the shutdown command. On Linux, macOS and OpenBSD, this is converted to minutes and rounded down. If less than 60, it is set to 0. On Solaris and FreeBSD, this represents seconds.

msg.description: Message to display to users before shutdown.

search_paths.description: Paths to search on the remote machine for the C(shutdown) command. I(Only) these paths are searched for the C(shutdown) command. E(PATH) is ignored in the remote node when searching for the C(shutdown) command.

community.general.simpleinit_msb.short_description: Manage services on Source Mage GNU/Linux
community.general.simpleinit_msb.description: Controls services on remote hosts using C(simpleinit-msb).

name.description: Name of the service.

state.description: V(started)/V(stopped) are idempotent actions that do not run commands unless necessary. V(restarted) always bounces the service. V(reloaded) always reloads. At least one of O(state) and O(enabled) are required. Note that V(reloaded) starts the service if it is not already started, even if your chosen init system would not normally.

enabled.description: Whether the service should start on boot. At least one of O(state) and O(enabled) are required.

community.general.sl_vm.short_description: Create or cancel a virtual instance in SoftLayer
community.general.sl_vm.description: Creates or cancels SoftLayer instances. When created, optionally waits for it to be 'running'.

instance_id.description: Instance ID of the virtual instance to perform action option.

hostname.description: Hostname to be provided to a virtual instance.

domain.description: Domain name to be provided to a virtual instance.

datacenter.description: Datacenter for the virtual instance to be deployed.

tags.description: Tag or list of tags to be provided to a virtual instance.

hourly.description: Flag to determine if the instance should be hourly billed.

private.description: Flag to determine if the instance should be private only.

dedicated.description: Flag to determine if the instance should be deployed in dedicated space.

local_disk.description: Flag to determine if local disk should be used for the new instance.

cpus.description: Count of cpus to be assigned to new virtual instance.

memory.description: Amount of memory to be assigned to new virtual instance.

flavor.description: Specify which SoftLayer flavor template to use instead of cpus and memory.

disks.description: List of disk sizes to be assigned to new virtual instance.

os_code.description: OS Code to be used for new virtual instance.

image_id.description: Image Template to be used for new virtual instance.

nic_speed.description: NIC Speed to be assigned to new virtual instance.

public_vlan.description: VLAN by its ID to be assigned to the public NIC.

private_vlan.description: VLAN by its ID to be assigned to the private NIC.

ssh_keys.description: List of ssh keys by their ID to be assigned to a virtual instance.

post_uri.description: URL of a post provisioning script to be loaded and executed on virtual instance.

state.description: Create, or cancel a virtual instance. Specify V(present) for create, V(absent) to cancel.

wait.description: Flag used to wait for active status before returning.

wait_time.description: Time in seconds before wait returns.

community.general.slack.description: The M(community.general.slack) module sends notifications to U(http://slack.com) using the Incoming WebHook integration.

domain.description: When using new format 'Webhook token' and WebAPI tokens: this can be V(slack.com) or V(slack-gov.com) and is ignored otherwise. When using old format 'Webhook token': Slack (sub)domain for your environment without protocol. (For example V(example.slack.com).) in Ansible 1.8 and beyond, this is deprecated and may be ignored. See token documentation for information.

token.description: Slack integration token. This authenticates you to the Slack service. Make sure to use the correct type of token, depending on what method you use. Webhook token: Prior to Ansible 1.8, a token looked like V(3Ffe373sfhRE6y42Fg3rvf4GlK). In Ansible 1.8 and above, Ansible adapts to the new Slack API where tokens look like V(G922VJP24/D921DW937/3Ffe373sfhRE6y42Fg3rvf4GlK). If tokens are in the new format then Slack ignores any value of domain except V(slack.com) or V(slack-gov.com). If the token is in the old format the domain is required. Ansible has no control of when Slack is going to remove the old API. When Slack does that the old format is going to cease working. B(Please keep in mind the tokens are not the API tokens but are the webhook tokens.) In Slack these are found in the webhook URL which are obtained under the apps and integrations. The incoming webhooks can be added in that area. In some cases this may be locked by your Slack admin and you must request access. It is there that the incoming webhooks can be added. The key is on the end of the URL given to you in that section. WebAPI token: Slack WebAPI requires a personal, bot or work application token. These tokens start with V(xoxp-), V(xoxb-) or V(xoxa-), for example V(xoxb-1234-56789abcdefghijklmnop). WebAPI token is required if you intend to receive thread_id. See Slack's documentation (U(https://api.slack.com/docs/token-types)) for more information.

msg.description: Message to send. Note that the module does not handle escaping characters. Plain-text angle brackets and ampersands should be converted to HTML entities (for example C(&) to C(&)) before sending. See Slack's documentation (U(https://api.slack.com/docs/message-formatting)) for more.

channel.description: Channel to send the message to. If absent, the message goes to the channel selected for the O(token).

thread_id.description: Optional. Timestamp of parent message to thread this message, see U(https://api.slack.com/docs/message-threading).

message_id.description: Optional. Message ID to edit, instead of posting a new message. If supplied O(channel) must be in form of C(C0xxxxxxx). use C({{ slack_response.channel }}) to get RV(ignore:channel) from previous task run. The token needs history scope to get information on the message to edit (C(channels:history,groups:history,mpim:history,im:history)). Corresponds to C(ts) in the Slack API (U(https://api.slack.com/messaging/modifying)).

username.description: This is the sender of the message.

icon_url.description: URL for the message sender's icon.

icon_emoji.description: Emoji for the message sender. See Slack documentation for options. If O(icon_emoji) is set, O(icon_url) is not used.

link_names.description: Automatically create links for channels and usernames in O(msg).

parse.description: Setting for the message parser at Slack.

validate_certs.description: If V(false), SSL certificates are not validated. This should only be used on personally controlled sites using self-signed certificates.

color.description: Allow text to use default colors - use the default of V(normal) to not send a custom color bar at the start of the message. Allowed values for color can be one of V(normal), V(good), V(warning), V(danger), any valid 3 digit or 6 digit hex color value.

attachments.description: Define a list of attachments. This list mirrors the Slack JSON API. For more information, see U(https://api.slack.com/docs/attachments).

blocks.description: Define a list of blocks. This list mirrors the Slack JSON API. For more information, see U(https://api.slack.com/block-kit).

prepend_hash.description: Setting for automatically prepending a V(#) symbol on the passed in O(channel). The V(auto) method prepends a V(#) unless O(channel) starts with one of V(#), V(@), V(C0), V(GF), V(G0), V(CP). These prefixes only cover a small set of the prefixes that should not have a V(#) prepended. Since an exact condition which O(channel) values must not have the V(#) prefix is not known, the value V(auto) for this option is deprecated in the future. It is best to explicitly set O(prepend_hash=always) or O(prepend_hash=never) to obtain the needed behavior. Before community.general 12.0.0, the default was V(auto). It has been deprecated since community.general 10.2.0. Note that V(auto) will be deprecated in a future version.

community.general.slackpkg.description: Manage binary packages for Slackware using C(slackpkg) which is available in versions after 12.2.

name.description: Name of package to install/remove.

state.description: State of the package, you can use V(installed) as an alias for V(present) and V(removed) as one for V(absent).

community.general.smartos_image_info.description: Retrieve information about all installed images on SmartOS.

filters.description: Criteria for selecting image. Can be any value from image manifest and V(published_date), V(published), V(source), V(clones), and V(size). More information can be found at U(https://smartos.org/man/8/imgadm) under C(imgadm list).

name.description: Name of the snaps to be installed. Any named snap accepted by the C(snap) command is valid. O(dangerous=true) may be necessary when installing C(.snap) files. See O(dangerous) for more details.

state.description: Desired state of the package. When O(state=present) the module uses C(snap install) if the snap is not installed, and C(snap refresh) if it is installed but from a different channel.

classic.description: Install a snap that has classic confinement. This option corresponds to the C(--classic) argument of the C(snap install) command. This level of confinement is permissive, granting full system access, similar to that of traditionally packaged applications that do not use sandboxing mechanisms. This option can only be specified when the task involves a single snap. See U(https://snapcraft.io/docs/snap-confinement) for more details about classic confinement and confinement levels.

channel.description: Define which release of a snap is installed and tracked for updates. This option can only be specified if there is a single snap in the task. If not passed, the C(snap) command defaults to V(stable). If the value passed does not contain the C(track), it defaults to C(latest). For example, if V(edge) is passed, the module assumes the channel to be V(latest/edge). See U(https://snapcraft.io/docs/channels) for more details about snap channels.

options.description: Set options with pattern C(key=value) or C(snap:key=value). If a snap name is given, the option is applied to that snap only. If the snap name is omitted, the options are applied to all snaps listed in O(name). Options are only applied to active snaps. Options are only applied when C(state) is set to V(present). This is done after the necessary installation or refresh (upgrade/downgrade) of all the snaps listed in O(name). See U(https://snapcraft.io/docs/configuration-in-snaps) for more details about snap configuration options.

dangerous.description: Install the snap in dangerous mode, without validating its assertions and signatures. This is useful when installing local snaps that are either unsigned or have signatures that have not been acknowledged. See U(https://snapcraft.io/docs/install-modes) for more details about installation modes.

state.description: Desired state of the alias.

name.description: Name of the snap.

alias.description: Aliases to be created or removed.

community.general.snmp_facts.short_description: Retrieve facts for a device using SNMP
community.general.snmp_facts.description: Retrieve facts for a device using SNMP. The facts are inserted to the RV(ansible_facts) key.

host.description: Set to target SNMP server (normally C({{ inventory_hostname }})).

version.description: SNMP Version to use, V(v2), V(v2c) or V(v3).

community.description: The SNMP community string, required if O(version) is V(v2) or V(v2c).

username.description: Username for SNMPv3. Required if O(version=v3).

timeout.description: Response timeout in seconds.

retries.description: Maximum number of request retries, 0 retries means just a single request.

community.general.solaris_zone.description: Create, start, stop and delete Solaris zones. This module does not currently allow changing of options for a zone that is already been created.

state.description: V(present), configure and install the zone. V(installed), synonym for V(present). V(running), if the zone already exists, boot it, otherwise, configure and install the zone first, then boot it. V(started), synonym for V(running). V(stopped), shutdown a zone. V(absent), destroy the zone. V(configured), configure the ready so that it is to be attached. V(attached), attach a zone, but do not boot it. V(detached), shutdown and detach a zone.

name.description: Zone name. A zone name must be unique name. A zone name must begin with an alphanumeric character. The name can contain alphanumeric characters, underscores V(_), hyphens V(-), and periods V(.). The name cannot be longer than 64 characters.

path.description: The path where the zone is created. This is required when the zone is created, but not used otherwise.

sparse.description: Whether to create a sparse (V(true)) or whole root (V(false)) zone.

root_password.description: The password hash for the root account. If not specified, the zone's root account does not have a password.

config.description: The C(zonecfg) configuration commands for this zone. See zonecfg(1M) for the valid options and syntax. Typically this is a list of options separated by semi-colons or new lines, for example V(set auto-boot=true;add net;set physical=bge0;set address=10.1.1.1;end).

create_options.description: Extra options to the zonecfg(1M) create command.

install_options.description: Extra options to the zoneadm(1M) install command. To automate Solaris 11 zone creation, use this to specify the profile XML file, for example O(install_options=-c sc_profile.xml).

attach_options.description: Extra options to the zoneadm attach command. For example, this can be used to specify whether a minimum or full update of packages is required and if any packages need to be deleted. For valid values, see zoneadm(1M).

timeout.description: Timeout, in seconds, for zone to boot.

community.general.sorcery.short_description: Package manager for Source Mage GNU/Linux
community.general.sorcery.description: Manages "spells" on Source Mage GNU/Linux using I(sorcery) toolchain.

name.description: Name of the spell or grimoire. Multiple names can be given, separated by commas. Special value V(*) in conjunction with states V(latest) or V(rebuild) updates or rebuilds the whole system respectively. The alias O(grimoire) was added in community.general 7.3.0.

repository.description: Repository location. If specified, O(name) represents grimoire(s) instead of spell(s). Special value V(*) pulls grimoire from the official location. Only single item in O(name) in conjunction with V(*) can be used. O(state=absent) must be used with a special value V(*).

state.description: Whether to cast, dispel or rebuild a package. State V(cast) is an equivalent of V(present), not V(latest). State V(rebuild) implies cast of all specified spells, not only those existed before.

depends.description: Comma-separated list of _optional_ dependencies to build a spell (or make sure it is built) with; use V(+)/V(-) in front of dependency to turn it on/off (V(+) is optional though). This option is ignored if O(name) parameter is equal to V(*) or contains more than one spell. Providers must be supplied in the form recognized by Sorcery, for example 'V(openssl(SSL\))'.

update.description: Whether or not to update sorcery scripts at the very first stage.

update_cache.description: Whether or not to update grimoire collection before casting spells.

cache_valid_time.description: Time in seconds to invalidate grimoire collection on update. Especially useful for SCM and rsync grimoires. Makes sense only in pair with O(update_cache).

community.general.spectrum_device.description: This module allows you to create and delete devices in CA Spectrum U(https://www.ca.com/us/products/ca-spectrum.html). Tested on CA Spectrum 9.4.2, 10.1.1 and 10.2.1.

device.description: IP address of the device. If a hostname is given, it is resolved to the IP address.

community.description: SNMP community used for device discovery. Required when O(state=present).

landscape.description: Landscape handle of the SpectroServer to which add or remove the device.

state.description: On V(present) creates the device when it does not exist. On V(absent) removes the device when it exists.

url.description: HTTP, HTTPS URL of the Oneclick server in the form V((http|https\)://host.domain[:port]).

use_proxy.description: If V(false), it does not use a proxy, even if one is defined in an environment variable on the target hosts.

validate_certs.description: If V(false), SSL certificates are not validated. This should only be used on personally controlled sites using self-signed certificates.

agentport.description: UDP port used for SNMP discovery.

community.general.spectrum_model_attrs.short_description: Enforce a model's attributes in CA Spectrum
community.general.spectrum_model_attrs.description: This module can be used to enforce a model's attributes in CA Spectrum.

url.description: URL of OneClick server.

use_proxy.description: If V(false), it does not use a proxy, even if one is defined in an environment variable on the target hosts.

name.description: Model name.

type.description: Model type.

validate_certs.description: Validate SSL certificates. Only change this to V(false) if you can guarantee that you are talking to the correct endpoint and there is no man-in-the-middle attack happening.

attributes.description: A list of attribute names and values to enforce. All values and parameters are case sensitive and must be provided as strings only.

community.general.spotinst_aws_elastigro.short_description: Create, update or delete Spotinst AWS Elastigroups
community.general.spotinst_aws_elastigro.description: Can create, update, or delete Spotinst AWS Elastigroups Launch configuration is part of the elastigroup configuration, so no additional modules are necessary for handling the launch configuration. You must have a credentials file in this location - C($HOME/.spotinst/credentials). The credentials file must contain a row that looks like this C(token = <YOUR TOKEN>). Full documentation available at U(https://help.spotinst.com/hc/en-us/articles/115003530285-Ansible-).

credentials_path.description: Optional parameter that allows to set a non-default credentials path.

account_id.description: Optional parameter that allows to set an account-id inside the module configuration. By default this is retrieved from the credentials path.

token.description: A Personal API Access Token issued by Spotinst. When not specified, the module tries to obtain it, in that order, from environment variable E(SPOTINST_TOKEN), or from the credentials path.

availability_vs_cost.description: The strategy orientation. The choices available are: V(availabilityOriented), V(costOriented), V(balanced).

availability_zones.description: A list of hash/dictionaries of Availability Zones that are configured in the elastigroup; '[{"key":"value", "key":"value"}]'; keys allowed are name (String), subnet_id (String), placement_group_name (String),.

block_device_mappings.description: A list of hash/dictionaries of Block Device Mappings for elastigroup instances; You can specify virtual devices and EBS volumes.; '[{"key":"value", "key":"value"}]'; keys allowed are device_name (List of Strings), virtual_name (String), no_device (String), ebs (Object, expects the following keys- delete_on_termination(Boolean), encrypted(Boolean), iops (Integer), snapshot_id(Integer), volume_type(String), volume_size(Integer)).

chef.description: The Chef integration configuration.; Expects the following keys - chef_server (String), organization (String), user (String), pem_key (String), chef_version (String).

draining_timeout.description: Time for instance to be drained from incoming requests and deregistered from ELB before termination.

ebs_optimized.description: Enable EBS optimization for supported instances which are not enabled by default. Note - additional charges are applied.

ebs_volume_pool.description: A list of hash/dictionaries of EBS devices to reattach to the elastigroup when available; '[{"key":"value", "key":"value"}]'; keys allowed are - volume_ids (List of Strings), device_name (String).

ecs.description: The ECS integration configuration.; Expects the following key - cluster_name (String).

elastic_ips.description: List of ElasticIps Allocation IDs (example V(eipalloc-9d4e16f8)) to associate to the group instances.

fallback_to_od.description: In case of no spots available, Elastigroup launches an On-demand instance instead.

health_check_grace_period.description: The amount of time, in seconds, after the instance has launched to start and check its health. If not specified, it defaults to V(300).

health_check_unhealthy_duration_before_r.description: Minimal mount of time instance should be unhealthy for us to consider it unhealthy.

health_check_type.description: The service to use for the health check. The choices available are: V(ELB), V(HCS), V(TARGET_GROUP), V(MLB), V(EC2).

iam_role_name.description: The instance profile iamRole name. Only use O(iam_role_arn) or O(iam_role_name).

iam_role_arn.description: The instance profile iamRole arn. Only use O(iam_role_arn) or O(iam_role_name).

id.description: The group ID if it already exists and you want to update, or delete it. This does not work unless the O(uniqueness_by) field is set to ID. When this is set, and the O(uniqueness_by) field is set, the group is either updated or deleted, but not created.

image_id.description: The image ID used to launch the instance.; In case of conflict between Instance type and image type, an error is be returned.

key_pair.description: Specify a Key Pair to attach to the instances.

kubernetes.description: The Kubernetes integration configuration. Expects the following keys - api_server (String), token (String).

max_size.description: The upper limit number of instances that you can scale up to.

mesosphere.description: The Mesosphere integration configuration. Expects the following key - api_server (String).

min_size.description: The lower limit number of instances that you can scale down to.

monitoring.description: Describes whether instance Enhanced Monitoring is enabled.

name.description: Unique name for elastigroup to be created, updated or deleted.

network_interfaces.description: A list of hash/dictionaries of network interfaces to add to the elastigroup; '[{"key":"value", "key":"value"}]'; keys allowed are - description (String), device_index (Integer), secondary_private_ip_address_count (Integer), associate_public_ip_address (Boolean), delete_on_termination (Boolean), groups (List of Strings), network_interface_id (String), private_ip_address (String), subnet_id (String), associate_ipv6_address (Boolean), private_ip_addresses (List of Objects, Keys are privateIpAddress (String, required) and primary (Boolean)).

on_demand_count.description: Required if risk is not set. Number of on demand instances to launch. All other instances are spot instances.; Either set this parameter or the O(risk) parameter.

on_demand_instance_type.description: On-demand instance type that is provisioned.

opsworks.description: The elastigroup OpsWorks integration configuration.; Expects the following key - layer_id (String).

persistence.description: The Stateful elastigroup configuration.; Accepts the following keys - should_persist_root_device (Boolean), should_persist_block_devices (Boolean), should_persist_private_ip (Boolean).

product.description: Operation system type. Available choices are: V(Linux/UNIX), V(SUSE Linux), V(Windows), V(Linux/UNIX (Amazon VPC)), V(SUSE Linux (Amazon VPC)).

rancher.description: The Rancher integration configuration.; Expects the following keys - version (String), access_key (String), secret_key (String), master_host (String).

right_scale.description: The Rightscale integration configuration.; Expects the following keys - account_id (String), refresh_token (String).

risk.description: Required if on demand is not set. The percentage of Spot instances to launch (0 - 100).

roll_config.description: Roll configuration. If you would like the group to roll after updating, please use this feature. Accepts the following keys - batch_size_percentage(Integer, Required), grace_period - (Integer, Required), health_check_type(String, Optional).

scheduled_tasks.description: A list of hash/dictionaries of scheduled tasks to configure in the elastigroup, as in V([{"key":"value", "key":"value"}]). Keys allowed are: adjustment (Integer), scale_target_capacity (Integer), scale_min_capacity (Integer), scale_max_capacity (Integer), adjustment_percentage (Integer), batch_size_percentage (Integer), cron_expression (String), frequency (String), grace_period (Integer), task_type (String, required), is_enabled (Boolean).

security_group_ids.description: One or more security group IDs. In case of update it overrides the existing Security Group with the new given array.

shutdown_script.description: The Base64-encoded shutdown script that executes prior to instance termination. Encode before setting.

signals.description: A list of hash/dictionaries of signals to configure in the elastigroup; keys allowed are - name (String, required), timeout (Integer).

spin_up_time.description: Spin up time, in seconds, for the instance.

spot_instance_types.description: Spot instance type that is provisioned.

state.description: Create or delete the elastigroup.

tags.description: A list of tags to configure in the elastigroup. Please specify list of keys and values (key colon value).

target.description: The number of instances to launch.

target_group_arns.description: List of target group arns instances should be registered to.

tenancy.description: Dedicated or shared tenancy. The available choices are: V(default), V(dedicated).

terminate_at_end_of_billing_hour.description: Terminate at the end of billing hour.

unit.description: The capacity unit to launch instances by. The available choices are: V(instance), V(weight).

up_scaling_policies.description: A list of hash/dictionaries of scaling policies to configure in the elastigroup; '[{"key":"value", "key":"value"}]'; keys allowed are - policy_name (String, required), namespace (String, required), metric_name (String, required), dimensions (List of Objects, Keys allowed are name (String, required) and value (String)), statistic (String, required) evaluation_periods (String, required), period (String, required), threshold (String, required), cooldown (String, required), unit (String, required), operator (String, required), action_type (String, required), adjustment (String), min_target_capacity (String), target (String), maximum (String), minimum (String).
